title: BlueBridge renewal: runway, term change, and QBR slot
preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Oliver Chen <oliver.chen@bluebridge.com>, Priya Nair <priya.nair@bluebridge.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: BlueBridge renewal: runway, term change, and QBR slot

Hi Oliver / Priya,

Thanks again for the time last week — great to see usage trends solidifying. Per our conversation, BlueBridge is in a good spot for a renewal this summer with a modest capacity uplift and a short-term term change to align with your fiscal year. I wanted to start the formal kickoff thread so we can lock dates and owners.

High-level proposal (draft):
- Term: move from 12-month to 18-month to smooth pricing step-downs (we can map economics below).
- Capacity: +30% committed tokens (forecast attached) to cover projected Q3 burst.
- Timeline: mutual action plan (MAP) and legal redlines in June; QBR & executive readout week of June 28.

Immediate asks / next steps:
1) Can you confirm a preferred QBR window (week of June 21 or June 28)? I created a quick availability poll here: https://cal.poll/bluebridge-qbr-availability
2) Priya — please confirm who from procurement/legal should be on the MAP and review loop.
3) Oliver — we sent a usage export for May (attached); any surprises or additional telemetry you want included?

I've attached a draft MAP (BlueBridge_MAP_v1.docx) and the May usage CSV. Rishi — can you sketch the pricing delta for 18-month vs 12-month with the 30% uplift and confirm run-rate savings? Marissa — can you prep an ops readout for the QBR (latency, headroom, failover)?

Thanks — Mateo

--
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123
Redwood Inference


From: Oliver Chen <oliver.chen@bluebridge.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Priya Nair <priya.nair@bluebridge.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 12:15:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: BlueBridge renewal: runway, term change, and QBR slot

Thanks Mateo — appreciate the clear summary.

Quick answers:
- QBR: week of June 28 works better for our execs (June 29 or 30 ideal).
- Procurement/legal: loop in contracts@bluebridge.com (specifically Anya Patel) — Priya, can you add her?
- Usage: saw the CSV — traffic is trending up as expected; we do have a campaign spike projected mid-July (so the +30% makes sense).

One ask on terms: we prefer a 15-month alignment if that keeps price exposure manageable on our side. Would Redwood consider 15 months with a mid-term review clause? Also, any way to temporarily increase burst capacity for the July campaign without re-pricing the whole commit?

Oliver
Director, Platform Engineering
BlueBridge Inc.
oliver.chen@bluebridge.com | +1 646-555-7789


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Oliver Chen <oliver.chen@bluebridge.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Priya Nair <priya.nair@bluebridge.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 09:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: BlueBridge renewal: runway, term change, and QBR slot

Oliver — thanks for the quick replies and the preference for 15 months. Two quick notes on economics and burst: 

1) Term economics (draft):
- 12mo baseline: list price (no commit) and benchmark.
- 15mo commit: ~8% effective discount vs monthly renewals (we'll run final numbers with exact usage), plus smoothing on unit price across the contract.
- 18mo: ~12% effective discount.

2) Burst policy suggestion: we can offer an emergency burst pool tied to a one-time credit balance (no contract reprice) for the July campaign that triggers on a usage threshold and is capped. If you want, I can draft a blow-by-blow: trigger threshold, cap, and cost (likely cheaper than a permanent uplift).

3) Legal/Doc signs: Mateo — when you have final MAP, we'll prep a DocuSign envelope. Priya / Anya — copying for procurement/legal alignment.

I'll attach a quick sketch of expected unit economics (send separately as pricing_workup_RM.xlsx) and propose a short internal sync Thursday to finalize numbers. Marissa — can you confirm QBR ops topics and whether we need an on-call engineer present for the July spike?

- Rishi
Senior Commercial Manager, Redwood Inference
rishi.malhotra@redwood.com | +1 617-555-0098


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Oliver Chen <oliver.chen@bluebridge.com>, Priya Nair <priya.nair@bluebridge.com>, Anya Patel <anya.patel@bluebridge.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 26 May 2026 15:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: BlueBridge renewal: runway, term change, and QBR slot

Oliver / Priya / Anya — thanks everyone. Quick readout and agreed next steps: 

1) QBR date: locked for Tuesday, June 29, 09:30–11:00 PT (exec slot reserved). Calendar invite + agenda to follow.
2) Term: Redwood will prepare a 15-month proposal with a mid-term review clause (Rishi will send economics by end of day Thursday).
3) Burst: Rishi will draft a temporary burst pool proposal (trigger/cap/cost) for the July campaign; if agreeable we'll include it in the MAP as an appendix so no re-pricing is required.
4) Legal: Anya — we'll send the MAP and a redline-ready contract stub via DocuSign: https://docusign.com/RED-BB-2026/Envelope12345
5) Ops: Marissa will prepare the QBR readout (latency, headroom, runbook) and confirm on-call coverage for July spike.

Attachments: BlueBridge_MAP_v1.docx, usage_report_May2026.csv

If anything above is incorrect, flag now — otherwise we'll proceed on this plan.

Thanks — looking forward to the QBR.

--
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123
Redwood Inference

title: Dedicated GPU mix and operational plan for Cobalt Labs — SKU tradeoffs and slot hedging
preview:
From: Sophia King <sophia.king@cobaltlabs.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: Paul Hardin <paul.hardin@cobaltlabs.com>, procurement@cobaltlabs.com
Date: 2026-05-20T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Request — guidance on dedicated GPU options for our inference workloads

Hi Mateo,

Thanks for the intro last week. We attached a short RFP (Cobalt_RFP_20260519.pdf) describing two main workloads:
- GenAI assistant with ~64k token context windows (few concurrent sessions, high memory).
- Query/embedding service for search ranking (large concurrent throughput, short context).

We need recommendations for a Dedicated capacity SKU mix (H100 vs A100 vs L4) that balances latency and cost. Our procurement deadline is the end of June — can you help with a short recommendation and a preliminary cost model?

Also useful: guidance on pre-warm / cache strategies, and whether we should request reserved capacity + a small burst pool.

Thanks — happy to hop on 30 min call if helpful.

Sophia
Sophia King
Head of ML Infrastructure, Cobalt Labs
Sophia.King@cobaltlabs.com


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.inference.com>
To: Sophia King <sophia.king@cobaltlabs.com>
Date: 2026-05-20T12:31:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request — guidance on dedicated GPU options for our inference workloads

Hi Sophia — thanks for the RFP, received and skimmed.

Quick summary / next steps: 1) short call to lock in peak qps and tail latency SLOs (30 min), 2) we’ll run a tensor-efficiency sizing pass and return a 1‑pager + cost model (xls) by EOD Friday, 3) propose a canary plan for deployment and warm pools.

High-level takeaways from the RFP: the assistant is memory-bound (large KV cache + >32k context), embeddings are throughput-bound. That maps naturally to: H100 for memory-first long-context serving; A100 for sustained throughput and mixed-precision batch efficiency; L4 for lower-cost low-latency embedding/mini-inference routes.

Operational asks: please confirm (a) expected concurrent long-context sessions, (b) average and 95th percentile tokens per session, and (c) acceptable cold-start penalty (ms). We’ll use those to size reserved slots vs burst reservoir.

I attached a first-pass cost workbook (cost_model_v2.xlsx) and a short one-pager we can refine. If you’re OK, I’ll loop in Jordan and Irene to propose the exact slot allocation and fallback (tiling vs compaction) heuristics.

Best,
Mateo
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Solutions Engineer — Dedicated Capacity
Redwood Inference


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.inference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.inference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.inference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference.com>
Date: 2026-05-21T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Internal: Cobalt Labs — SKU recommendation draft

Team — drafting recommended SKU mix for Cobalt based on RFP + follow-up q’s. Notes below, please review and add operational caveats. I want to finalize for customer by EOD Friday.

Workloads mapped:
- Assistant (64k long context, KV-heavy): strongly memory-bound. Primary: H100 (tensor memory + large-device KV cache), fallback: A100 with compaction for <=32k sessions.
- Embeddings/ranking: high qps short context: primary A100 for batching efficiency; opportunistic L4 nodes for low-cost, low-latency routes and bursty spikes.

Tradeoffs and reasoning (short):
- H100: best for >32k contexts, big model footprints, kernel-level memory optimizations (tensor core tiling + momory-compaction). Higher $/hr but avoids session-eviction OOMs and expensive re-requests.
- A100: sweet spot for steady-state throughput; excellent fp16 mixed-precision perf; cheaper per-token when you can batch. Works as a fallback when we can accept compaction or split context across shard/tiling.
- L4: cost-effective for small models, embedding generation, and synchronous low-latency endpoints. Lower memory means not suitable for large contexts but very efficient for high-concurrency small requests.

Recommended reserve / burst plan (proposed):
- Reserved pool: 40% H100 / 40% A100 / 20% L4 (by slot count). Rationale: ensures memory capacity for assistant while keeping throughput capacity for embeddings.
- Burst reservoir (on-demand): 10% of total capacity as transient slots (preferably A100 and L4 mix) to absorb traffic spikes and onboarding churn.
- Pre-warm pool: small set of H100 pre-warmed for canary sessions to reduce cold-start penalty for long contexts.
- Slot hedging: configure eviction thresholds and a 2‑minute KV cache TTL for assistant sessions to reduce re-loads during short spikes.

Operational controls and SLOs to include in offer:
- Per-route concurrency windows and token budget caps (to avoid runaway KV growth).
- Automatic fallback policy: attempt A100 compaction path for 1 attempt, then failover to L4 read-only routing for non-critical, short-lived requests.
- Canary rollout: 5% traffic on mixed-sku pool for 48 hours, monitor token-per-second and OOMs.

Cost notes:
- Estimate per-token cost delta: H100 ~ +30–45% vs A100 for dense batching when memory is not the limiter; L4 ~ -60% per token for short-context routes. Final numbers in cost_model_v2.xlsx — please sanity check the assumptions (tokens/sec, average batch size, effective utilization).

Questions for ops and SRE (Irene): can we pre-allocate a 4-node H100 pre-warm group with pinned KV cache and 12-hour keepalive? Also need a recommended eviction policy and a telemetry tag to surface session-compaction events.

Jordan / Marissa — can you prepare a customer-facing one-pager that summarizes the recommended SKU mix, expected capex/opex delta, and the canary plan? I’ll include it with the quote.

Thanks — Mateo


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.inference.com>
To: Sophia King <sophia.king@cobaltlabs.com>, Paul Hardin <paul.hardin@cobaltlabs.com>, procurement@cobaltlabs.com
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.inference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.inference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference.com>
Date: 2026-05-22T15:43:00-07:00
Subject: Proposal: Dedicated SKU mix and initial runbook for Cobalt Labs (draft)

Hi Sophia / Paul — thanks for the quick sync earlier today. Attached is a draft one-pager and the cost workbook (dedicated_sku_onepager_v1.pdf, cost_model_v2.xlsx). Below is the TL;DR of our recommended offering and immediate next steps.

TL;DR recommendation:
- Reserve 40% H100 / 40% A100 / 20% L4 by slot count.
- Add a 10% burst reservoir (mixed A100/L4) for peak absorb.
- Pre-warm a 4-node H100 pool for long-context canaries.
- Operational controls: per-route token caps, 2-minute KV TTL, and a two-tier fallback (A100 compaction then L4 read-only) for memory pressure.

Why this is different: it preserves session-level memory for your >32k assistant workloads while keeping per-token economics reasonable for embedding/ranking. The mix allows live rollbacks to cheaper L4 capacity for non-critical paths while guaranteeing memory for active sessions on H100.

Requested from your side:
1) Confirm expected concurrent long-context sessions (estimate is fine).
2) Sign off on the proposed canary window (48h, 5% traffic) and warm pool size.
3) Procurement: suggest we start with a 6-month reserved commitment with a 4-week opt-in to increase burst reservoir if needed.

If you confirm, we’ll: 1) finalize the quote and DocuSign package, 2) provide a short runbook and telemetry tags for your SRE, and 3) schedule the canary deployment with our Ops team. DocuSign link included in the Drive/HubSpot references.

Open items: I want Irene to confirm pre-warm feasibility and Marissa / Jordan to finalize customer one-pager by tomorrow morning.

Best — looking forward to moving this across the finish line.

Mateo Alvarez
Senior Solutions Engineer — Dedicated Capacity
Redwood Inference
Mateo.Alvarez@redwood.inference.com
+1 (415) 555-0198

title: Subscriber Onboarding Bonus: 2-week Trial Codes
preview:
From: Courtney Zhang <courtney@eventworks.co>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>

title: Yearly token allocation proposal — Starter/Growth/Enterprise options
preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jared Klein <jared.k@northbridge.ai>, Northbridge Analytics - Procurement <procurement@northbridge.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 17 May 2026 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Yearly token allocation proposal — Starter/Growth/Enterprise options

Hi Jared / Procurement team,

Thanks for the time last week — attaching a clean proposal that lays out three annual commit tiers for Northbridge's hosted API usage. The attached PDF has line-item math; below is a condensed overview and the levers I think matter most for your forecast.

Summary (annual commits, per-token effective rate):
- Starter: 2,000,000 tokens / year — $0.00090 per token (≈10% off list)
- Growth: 10,000,000 tokens / year — $0.00065 per token (≈25% off list)
- Enterprise: 50,000,000 tokens / year — $0.00045 per token (≈40% off list)

Key terms we propose:
- Rollover: up to 10% of unused committed tokens may carry to next quarter (optional for Starter; default for Growth/Enterprise)
- Bursting: 20% monthly burst allowance above committed rate billed at a blended overage rate (full details in PDF)
- Ramp: 3-month smoothing window for usage ramp-up without penalty
- True-up: quarterly true-up with 30-day net payment terms; early prepayment discounts available for annual upfronts
- SLA: 99.9% availability on hosted API; priority queueing for Enterprise tier

We can add a short Dedicated pilot for Enterprise customers if you want isolation and predictable throughput — pricing separate.

Next steps I recommend: 1) confirm which tier you'd like us to model against your Q3 forecast, 2) I’ll produce a 12-month cost model with your forecast (example attached), and 3) we can schedule a 30-minute PO + procurement checklist call.

Attachments: NB_Yearly_Token_Allocation_Proposal.pdf, NB_usage_forecast_q3-q4.xlsx

Thanks,
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0127
redwoodinference.com


From: Jared Klein <jared.k@northbridge.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Northbridge Analytics - Procurement <procurement@northbridge.ai>
Date: Mon, 17 May 2026 11:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Yearly token allocation proposal — Starter/Growth/Enterprise options

Thanks Mateo — appreciate the clear tiers. A few quick clarifying questions before we loop procurement: 

1) Ramp & true-up: if we pick Growth (10M) but end the year at ~6.5M, what's the financial outcome? Is there a minimum true-up or do we simply pay actual usage with no refund?
2) Carryover: can you confirm the carryover is per-quarter or annual? Can we opt for a larger carryover for the first year only?
3) Bursting: we have unpredictable spikes related to batch scoring windows. Can we get a hard cap on monthly overage in dollars or total tokens before throttling?
4) Timeline: procurement wants clear PO terms and a DocuSign-ready schedule — what's typical turnaround after finalizing tier?

If you can send a short worked example (Growth vs. pay-as-you-go at our forecasted usage profile) that would help. Happy to take a quick call Thur/Fri.

Best,
Jared
Jared Klein
Head of Product — Northbridge Analytics
jared.k@northbridge.ai


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 17 May 2026 12:03:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Jared's questions on ramp/carryover/burst — suggested responses and concessions

Matt / Karthik — quick internal note so we align before Mateo responds to Jared. Suggested concessions to keep deal velocity high while protecting unit economics: 

1) Growth true-up: allow quarterly true-up to actuals with a 75% floor of committed tokens billed if usage < committed for the year (i.e., minimum revenue protection). That is, if end-of-year actual < commit, customer pays max(actual, 0.75 * committed). For Starter make floor 50%.
2) Carryover: default per-quarter carryover up to 10% of quarterly commit; first-year option to expand carryover to 20% once only (to support initial migration).
3) Bursting: offer a dollar cap option for Growth tier: cap = committed_monthly_tokens * 1.3 * blended_rate; once cap reached, we throttle to best-effort (or we can switch to Dedicated pilot if they need higher SLAs).
4) Onboarding credits: include a one-time 60-day trial credit of 250k tokens for Growth / 1M for Enterprise to mitigate migration risk.

Action items: Mateo to present a worked cost model (Growth vs PAYG), Karthik to approve floor percentages and cap language for legal, I will draft the amendment language for PO.

-Marissa
Marissa Cole
Director, Sales | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jared Klein <jared.k@northbridge.ai>, Northbridge Analytics - Procurement <procurement@northbridge.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 18 May 2026 09:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Yearly token allocation proposal — Starter/Growth/Enterprise options

Hi Jared — thanks for the questions. We discussed internally and are happy to propose the following updated offer tailored to Northbridge's profile: 

Updated Growth offer (recommended):
- Commit: 10,000,000 tokens / year at $0.00065 / token
- First-year onboarding credit: 250,000 tokens applied in month 1
- Ramp protection: 3-month smoothing window; quarterly true-up to actuals with a 75% committed-floor (Starter = 50% floor)
- Carryover: per-quarter carryover up to 10% of that quarter's committed tokens; one-time first-year bump to 20% available upon request
- Bursting: 30% monthly burst allowed, with an optional dollar-cap at 130% committed_monthly * blended_rate; if cap is reached we throttle unless Enterprise Dedicated pilot is agreed
- Billing/PO: 30-day net invoicing; DocuSign-ready amendment and SOW from our side within 3 business days after confirmation

Attached is a worked example comparing Growth vs PAYG using the usage profile you shared (see NB_usage_forecast_q3-q4.xlsx) and a revised PDF with contract language redlines. If the cap option is attractive, say so and I’ll add the exact dollar number to the SOW.

Proposed next steps:
1) Confirm Growth + cap option (or tell me you prefer Starter/Enterprise)
2) I’ll push the DocuSign amendment and cost model into HubSpot (link below)
3) Procurement signs amendment, we begin onboarding (3-week playbook)

HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/12345
DocuSign preview: https://www.docusign.com/envelope/XYZ-789

Would you be free for a 30-minute alignment call Friday 11am PT?

Thanks again,
Mateo
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0127
Attachments: NB_Yearly_Token_Allocation_Proposal.pdf, NB_usage_forecast_q3-q4.xlsx

title: Fulfillment webhook backstop & invoice reconciliation for AWS Marketplace listing
preview:
From: Amanda Carter <amanda.carter@aws-partner.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 10 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Fulfillment webhook backstop & invoice reconciliation for Redwood SaaS listing

Hi Mateo / Kimberly,

Quick heads up from the AWS listings ops team: we've seen intermittent 500 responses from your fulfillment webhook for the ACME listing (productId: rw-inference-acme-v2) over the past 48 hours. When the webhook fails the marketplace falls back to metering exports, which your billing pipeline consumes. Two problems we've observed:

- Underreporting risk when the webhook fails and exports are delayed
- Duplicate invoice drafts when a late-export is ingested alongside a successful webhook delivery

To avoid customer billing surprises we'd like to align on a short-term backstop and a durable reconciliation process:

1) A temporary backfill/ingest for any webhook exposures in the last 7 days (S3 export)
2) A conservative webhook retry/backoff policy and a durable request-id in payloads to dedupe
3) A mapping table we can use for invoice reconciliation (orderId <-> entitlementId)

Can you share: a sample failed webhook payload, the headers you rely on for dedupe, and whether you can accept an S3 export ingestion we populate from the marketplace? We aim for a 72-hour mitigation window.

Thanks,
Amanda Carter
Marketplace Integration Lead — AWS Partner Network

Attachment: acme-failed-delivery.log (text/plain)


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Amanda Carter <amanda.carter@aws-partner.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 10 May 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fulfillment webhook backstop & invoice reconciliation for Redwood SaaS listing

Amanda — thanks for flagging this.

Short answer: we can implement a backfill and a dedupe flow. Proposed immediate plan:

- Mateo: run a canary ingestion that consumes the S3 failed-deliveries for the last 7 days and writes a reconciliation report to s3://redwood-recon/acme/HB-33891/ (owner: mateo.alvarez@redwood.com).
- Kimberly: coordinate with Finance on invoice hold/unhold rules and confirm whether they want a provisional credit for affected accounts.
- Engineering: add a request-id header (X-RW-Request-Id) to webhook responses we accept and a 5-retry exponential backoff with jitter on 5xx.

Deliverables and timeline (target):
- Today/T+24: sample webhook payload and the failure log we generated (attached).
- T+48: run one canary backfill into a staging invoice queue and share reconciliation csv.
- T+72: promote reconciliation to prod and provide a post-mortem and invoice adjustments if needed.

Please attach the raw webhook payloads (if possible) and confirm the S3 path you want us to read. If you prefer, we can spin up a short-lived API endpoint to replay the missed payloads instead of S3.

I attached a sanitized failure sample.

Thanks,

—
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Partner Engineer, Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com

Attachment: webhook-failure-sample.json (application/json)


From: Amanda Carter <amanda.carter@aws-partner.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, ACME Billing <billing@acme.software>
Date: Mon, 11 May 2026 08:41:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fulfillment webhook backstop & invoice reconciliation for Redwood SaaS listing

Thanks Mateo — attaching a truncated example payload and the S3 path we already populate: s3://acme-marketplace-exports/2026/05/10/failed-deliveries.log.

Payload notes: we include marketplaceOrderId and entitlementId in the body; there is an X-AMZ-Request-Id header from the marketplace but it is not unique per delivery (so your X-RW-Request-Id sounds good). The S3 file contains the full POST body JSON lines and a timestamp.

We can also open a test seller account for you to replay payloads directly if that simplifies testing — ping me and I will provision.

ACME asked to be copied on reconciliation because their accounting team wants to see any invoice edits. Their billing contact is on this thread.

Best,
Amanda

Attachment: acme-failed-delivery.log (text/plain)


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Amanda Carter <amanda.carter@aws-partner.com>, ACME Billing <billing@acme.software>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 12 May 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fulfillment webhook backstop & invoice reconciliation for Redwood SaaS listing

Amanda / ACME — appreciate the fast turnaround and the S3 path. Quick confirmation and next steps:

- We will run the first canary ingest today (mateo.alvarez@redwood.com). Outputs will land in s3://redwood-recon/acme/HB-33891/canary-20260512.csv.
- Kimberly will follow up with Finance on provisional crediting and invoice hold policy (Kimberly — can you confirm preferred reconciliation window and whether Finance wants a separate CSV with line-item deltas?).
- I'll open SUP-2176 in our tracker (https://jira.redwood.com/browse/SUP-2176) and attach the canary output and scripts. The backfill script we used is attached as a stub; we will remove any PII before sharing externally.
- For dedupe, we will add X-RW-Request-Id to our accepted webhook pipeline and treat marketplaceOrderId+X-RW-Request-Id as a composite key for idempotency.

We have tentatively scheduled a 30-minute sync to review the canary outputs and reconciliation mapping tomorrow at 10:00 AM PT: https://calendar.redwood.com/event/6b8f9a. Please accept if that works.

Document linkage: I've also created a short HubSpot activity on the deal with the plan: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/52345/HB-33891.

Thanks — we will proceed and share the first reconciliation CSV within 24 hours.

—
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Partner Engineer, Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com

title: Post-call recap & prompt set capture request — next steps for model fit/evals
preview:
From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine_liu@redwood.com>
To: Maya Patel <maya@lexiq.ai>, Arman Rao <arman@lexiq.ai>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie_nguyen@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Apr 2026 16:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call recap & immediate asks — prompt collection and redaction guidance

Thanks again for a productive call today — great to meet both of you. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we keep momentum toward the eval and pilot: 

Summary (2 min):
- Goal: Evaluate Redwood inference for LexiQ’s knowledge‑augmented summarization and document QA flows (legal contracts + extracted entities).
- Success signals: >92% accuracy on our benchmark QA set, median token latency <120ms, and predictable unit cost under committed usage.

Immediate asks (what we need from LexiQ this week):
1) Representative prompt set: 150–250 prompts that reflect production traffic (mix of short queries, long-context summaries, multi-document Q&A). Please include examples of failure modes / edge cases you’re most worried about (PII redaction, nested citations, OCR noise).
2) Redaction guidance doc: one‑page bullets of fields that must be sanitized (names, SSNs, internal emails) and fields that can remain (document IDs, case numbers).
3) Priority scenarios: note 3 flows in order of importance (e.g., ‘contract-summary-high-precision’, ‘clause-extraction-entity-level’, ‘ad-hoc-QA’).

Attachments: I’ve attached a prompt_template.xlsx you can fill (columns: prompt_id, user_intent, input_example, expected_output_format, edge_case_flags). Also attached a short redaction_guidelines.pdf we typically use as a starting point.

Next Redwood deliverables:
- We’ll run an initial model-fit pass using two model classes (13B instruction-tuned and 70B quantized chat) and share a benchmark report with cost/latency/quality tradeoffs and suggested prompt-tuning items.
- Proposed timeline: LexiQ sends prompt set + guidance by EOD Friday (2026-04-17) → Redwood run and deliver preliminary benchmark by Tue 2026-04-21.

Could you confirm the prompts delivery timing and whether the attached template works for you? If email is inconvenient, we can drop a Drive folder and request a link.

Thanks,

Jasmine Liu
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
jasmine_liu@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0184

[Attachment: prompt_template.xlsx, redaction_guidelines.pdf]

From: Maya Patel <maya@lexiq.ai>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine_liu@redwood.com>
Cc: Arman Rao <arman@lexiq.ai>
Date: Thu, 16 Apr 2026 09:08:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Post-call recap & immediate asks — prompt collection and redaction guidance

Thanks Jasmine — appreciate the clear recap. Confirming we can deliver the initial set this week. A few notes from our side: 

- We’ll provide ~200 prompts by Friday (mix of voice transcriptions and extracted text). Some of the items have OCR artifacts and inconsistent punctuation — we’ll flag those rows in the template.
- Redaction: we need clarification on whether Redwood prefers masks (e.g., [REDACTED_NAME]) vs. token-level pseudonymization. Our compliance team favors masking for auditability.
- Edge cases to highlight: nested clauses with cross-references, anonymized party names that appear as acronyms, and very long exhibits (>20 pages).

Attached is a starter file (lexiq_sample_prompts.txt) with 30 representative prompts and a quick note on which flows are highest priority. We’ll fill the template and upload the full file by Friday.

On models: if you can include a lower-cost 7–13B candidate for initial sanity checks that would be ideal — we want to balance cost during the pilot.

Best,

Maya Patel
Head of Product, LexiQ Analytics
maya@lexiq.ai
[Attachment: lexiq_sample_prompts.txt]

On Wed, Apr 15, 2026 at 4:12 PM Jasmine Liu <jasmine_liu@redwood.com> wrote:
> (original message)

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine_liu@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie_nguyen@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Apr 2026 11:22:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: recommended model candidates & eval checklist for LexiQ

Looping internal notes here after Maya’s sample prompts. Key points for the evaluation plan: 

Recommended model slate (initial pass):
- Option A (cost/latency balanced): 13B instruction-tuned quantized model (13B-ptq) — good for quick iterations and prompt engineering checks.
- Option B (quality first): 70B chat quantized (70B-INT8/4) — expected to yield higher factuality on long-context summarization but higher token cost.
- Option C (cheap smoke test): 7B distilled variant — useful to triage prompt issues quickly before larger runs.

Evaluation checklist:
- Build eval sets from LexiQ prompts: QA, summarization, extraction. Ensure redaction test subset for PII detection and removal.
- Measure: exact-match / F1 for extraction, ROUGE-L + human spot checks for summaries, token latency distribution (p50/p90/p99), cost per 1k tokens.

title: Foundry Labs — request: convert cohort credits to platform voucher
preview:
From: Amy Chen <amy@foundrylabs.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 13 Apr 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Foundry Labs — request: convert cohort credits to platform voucher

Hi Mateo,

Quick one from our side post-demo — Foundry Labs was part of your Spring Cohort and we still have about $12,500 of unused promotional credits sitting in the cohort account. Our finance team is asking whether we can either (a) convert those credits into a sandbox/platform voucher that our engineering team can use on an ongoing basis, or (b) get an applied one-time invoice credit toward a committed Dedicated pool for a short pilot.

We saw the standard terms mention credits are cohort-bound, but given we are evaluating a paid Dedicated deployment next quarter, this conversion would help our adoption/commitment. Can you outline what exceptions are possible and any approval steps?

Happy to jump on a 20-min call if easier.

Best,
Amy
Amy Chen
Head of Engineering, Foundry Labs
amy@foundrylabs.com

--
Attachment: foundry_promo_summary.pdf (summary of cohort usage and remaining credits)

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>
To: Amy Chen <amy@foundrylabs.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 13 Apr 2026 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Foundry Labs — request: convert cohort credits to platform voucher

Thanks Amy — appreciate the clear ask. Short answer: we can sometimes convert cohort credits, but it requires a one-time exception (and a small ops/finance adjustment). Two pragmatic options to propose: 

1) Convert remaining credits into a one-off sandbox voucher tied to your account (non-transferable) — good for dev/testing; needs approval from Finance and we would issue an invoice-credit reconciliation (no cash refund).

2) Apply the dollar value as a credit toward a pre-paid Dedicated commitment (effectively reducing the first invoice) — requires PO and a short addendum for accounting. This is the cleaner path if you intend to go Dedicated.

To keep momentum, I can surface this to Ben (billing/partner programs) and Laura (finance) so we can confirm limits and any % add-on. If you’re open, can you share the cohort promo ID and the preferred option (sandbox voucher vs applied invoice credit)? I’ll attach our standard promo terms for reference.

I’ll propose a narrow exception (cap $15k) if we need to waive the cohort-bound rule.

– Mateo
Mateo Alvarez
Solutions Lead, Redwood Inference
mateo@redwood.ai

--
Attachment: promo_terms.pdf (promo eligibility and cohort rules)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 13 Apr 2026 14:41:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Foundry Labs — request: convert cohort credits to platform voucher

Forwarding Foundry’s ask — they have ~$12.5k remaining. Per policy, credits are cohort-bound but we do occasional exceptions for pipeline acceleration.

Question for Finance: are we comfortable converting to a sandbox voucher (option 1) without a PO? Alternatively, to offer a billing credit against a Dedicated commitment, we should require a PO and track it against HSP-12847.

My preference: offer option 2 (apply to first Dedicated invoice) but allow option 1 as a limited goodwill gesture if Foundry isn’t ready for a PO. If we make an exception, keep it one-time and document in an addendum.

Notes: we should avoid open-ended transfers of credits across accounts for audit reasons. If approved, I’ll draft the addendum and the small billing memo.

Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Partnerships, Redwood Inference

--
Attachment: invoice-draft.xlsx (pro forma showing credit applied scenario)

From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Apr 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Foundry Labs — request: convert cohort credits to platform voucher

Ben / Mateo — Finance is OK with a documented exception under the following conditions: 

- Cap the conversion at $15,000 (total promo value)
- If applying to Dedicated, require a PO and a signed one-page addendum (revenue recognition needs this)
- If issuing a sandbox voucher instead, mark it as non-transferable and track usage for 12 months; no cash refunds.
- Create a short internal memo referencing deal HSP-12847 and attach the promo_terms.pdf to the customer file.

I approve the exception for Foundry Labs given the pipeline potential. Please do not make this a precedent without a Finance/RevOps review.

Laura Bennett
Director, Finance Ops, Redwood Inference

Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 15 Apr 2026 15:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Foundry Labs — request: convert cohort credits to platform voucher

Thanks Amy — good news. Finance approved a one-time exception up to $15k. Two paths: 

A) If you’re ready to move toward Dedicated: we can apply $12.5k as a credit against your first Dedicated invoice. That requires a PO and a signed addendum (I’ve attached the template to this thread). We’ll record it to deal HSP-12847 and process on receipt of PO.

B) If you want quick access for devs: we can convert to a non-transferable sandbox voucher for $12.5k value; voucher valid for 12 months. No PO required.

Which do you prefer? Once you confirm, I’ll send the addendum/DocuSign or issue the voucher code and the invoice memo.

title: Clarify: bespoke data residency & deletion timelines for Zenith Health SLA
preview:
From: Jackson Lee <jlee@zenithhealth.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Zenith Security Team <security@zenithhealth.com>, Zenith Legal <contracts@zenithhealth.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Mar 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Data residency / deletion timelines — questions for SLA

Hi Mateo,

Thanks again for the demo — our security and procurement teams reviewed the baseline SLA and have a few questions specific to our compliance needs. We're a covered entity and have to ensure certain PHI-derived artifacts are removed within a defined window. Can you confirm whether Redwood can: 

- Guarantee deletion of customer-scoped model input/output and derived embeddings within a bespoke window (we're asking for 90 days, with options for 30 and 365 for different projects)
- Provide a secure deletion certificate or audit within 30 days of a deletion request
- Specify physical residency (data stored in us-west region only) for the Zenith account and separate logs retention window (we'd like logs for 365 days)

I've attached our short questionnaire (zenith-data-retention-questions.pdf) with exact fields. Procurement would like a short write-up we can attach to the order form that codifies the deletion timeline and proof-of-deletion process.

Can you send a suggested SLA clause and indicate any price delta for bespoke windows?

Jackson
Jackson Lee
Senior Procurement Manager
Zenith Health


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Mar 2026 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Data residency / deletion timelines — questions for SLA

Team — forwarding Zenith's questionnaire. Quick ask: can we produce a clean proposal that covers (a) bespoke deletion windows 30/90/365, (b) certified deletion attestation within 30 days, and (c) account-level residency to us-west only? Procurement is explicit about attaching it to the order form.

Notes:
- Karthik, please flag expected monthly delta for each window (ballpark %) and whether Dedicated vs Hosted affects this.
- Marissa, can you draft a short clause (1 paragraph + bullets) that is legally safe and includes an audit certificate option?
- Stephanie, if this requires a special onboarding or additional engineering work (region pinning, log segregation), please call out timelines.

I'll combine into a single .docx for them.

Thanks — Mateo

> On Wed, 18 Mar 2026 at 09:12 Jackson Lee <jlee@zenithhealth.com> wrote:
> ... (attached questionnaire)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Mar 2026 11:28:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Data residency / deletion timelines — questions for SLA

Agree we should move fast. Quick pricing note based on past bespoke retention deals:

- 30-day deletion window (high churn): +18% on base hosted rate (higher ingress/egress and more frequent secure wipe cycles)
- 90-day deletion window (medium): +8% on base hosted rate
- 365-day window (longer retention): +2% or no change for Dedicated customers; Hosted may incur +5% for longer log retention

Operational notes:
- Residency pinning to us-west is supported for Dedicated and Private; Hosted can be pinned to region with a short lead time (1–2 weeks) but may require capacity reservation.
- Certified deletion: we can provide an automated deletion job plus a signed attestation (PDF) generated after the job completes. Engineering estimate: 3 business days to schedule + 7 days to run and verify for the first full account purge.

Attaching a draft SLA proposal (redwood-sla-proposal-v1.docx). I'll iterate once Marissa provides clause language.

-Karthik


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Mar 2026 15:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Data residency / deletion timelines — questions for SLA

I've drafted a short clause below; please review and call out any operational gaps. I kept the legal language tight and added an "Audit and Proof of Deletion" sub-clause.

Proposed clause (for order form):
"Redwood will implement customer-specified data deletion windows for account-scoped data (including request payloads, generated outputs, and embeddings). Customer may choose a deletion window of 30, 90, or 365 days in the applicable Order Form. Upon execution of a deletion request, Redwood will perform secure deletion and provide a signed Deletion Attestation within thirty (30) calendar days. Residency: Customer data shall be stored only in the region specified in the Order Form (United States — us-west). Logs required for operational monitoring may be retained for up to 365 days as set forth in the Order Form."

Comments:
- We should separate "operational logs" from "customer-scoped data" in the OF.
- For certified deletion, suggest: "attestation describing the deletion job id, objects removed, and verification hash" — engineering: is providing a hash feasible?

Stephanie: can you confirm timeline estimates for the first full-account purge and whether we can include a standard 10-business-day SLA to schedule the job (vs the 3/7 Karthik threw out)?

Marissa
Legal — Redwood Inference


From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.com>
To: Jackson Lee <jlee@zenithhealth.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Mar 2026 09:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Data residency / deletion timelines — Zenith request

Hi Jackson / team — sorry for the incoming chain; quick operational clarifications from infra: 

- Residency pinning to us-west: we can do this for Zenith's account. For Hosted customers, we will need a 7–10 business day lead time to provision reserved capacity to guarantee residency and performance. Dedicated or Private deployments are faster if the customer already has a reserved pool.
- Certified deletion attestation: we can emit a PDF attestation. We cannot reliably provide a SHA256 hash for ephemeral internal objects unless we modify the deletion pipeline — that would add ~2 weeks of work. Suggest attestation include: deletion job id, timestamp, list of object prefixes removed, and signed operator statement.
- First full-account purge: scheduleable within 10 business days after Order Form execution; the run itself depends on dataset size but typical accounts complete in 24–72 hours.

I'll update the SLA doc to include a short operations annex with scheduling steps. Attaching updated PDF (redwood-sla-proposal-v2.pdf).

Thanks,
Stephanie
Senior Solutions Engineer


From: Jackson Lee <jlee@zenithhealth.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.com>
Cc: Zenith Legal <contracts@zenithhealth.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 19 Mar 2026 16:24:00 -07:00

Upon execution of a deletion request, Redwood will perform secure deletion and provide a signed Deletion Attestation within thirty (30) calendar days. Residency: Customer data shall be stored only in the region specified in the Order Form (United States — us-west). Logs required for operational monitoring may be retained for up to 365 days as set forth in the Order Form."

Hi Olivia — thanks for the note and the intake form.

Best,

title: Mid-Q OKR touchpoint: reprioritization and open dependencies
preview:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@vendorco.com
Date: Tue, 10 Mar 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Mid-Q OKR touchpoint: reprioritization and open dependencies

Team —

Quick mid-quarter check. We're ~6 weeks into Q2; I want a concise update (one paragraph + explicit asks) from each of you on the OKRs you own by EOD today so I can finalize the QBR prep doc. Please include:

- Current RAG (Green / Yellow / Red) and one-sentence rationale
- Any suggested re-scopes (minimize scope, postpone sub-goal, or add headcount/tooling)
- Blocking dependencies and owners (infra, security, procurement, partnerships)
- Impact if the blocker isn't resolved by the end of the quarter (ETA / risk)

Background: leadership wants a prioritized list for the exec QBR and a short ask sheet for procurement/security. I put a working doc here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1a2B-OKR-midq-reprioritization — I'm attaching a draft workbook that maps OKRs to dependencies.

Two quick rules: 1) if you mark something Red, propose a minimal viable slice to preserve customer-facing value; 2) label any items that require external contracts (procurement) so I can escalate.

Thanks — I know this is tight, but having these in one place will make the QBR conversation concrete.

Marissa
Marissa Cole
Head of Product Operations
Redwood Inference

Attachment: Q2-OKR-reprioritization.xlsx (spreadsheet stubs)


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Mar 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Mid-Q OKR touchpoint: reprioritization and open dependencies

Marissa —

Summary for OKR: "Increase production inference throughput by 2.5x for live workloads"
- RAG: Yellow — good progress on batching and caching prototypes, but perf regressions on long-tail prompts.
- Suggested re-scope: shift one perf micro-feature (dynamic KV eviction) from Q2 -> Q3, keep primary batching/packed-kv work in Q2. This preserves the main throughput gains.
- Blockers: Need an additional 2 A100s (or equivalent burst capacity) for stability tests and perf runs. Procurement ticket raised (PO#REQ-3421).
- Impact if unresolved: If we cannot run the full stress matrix by week 10, risk that we miss the 2.5x validation window (impact: delayed launch to partner pilots).

Proposed owner for infra request: Vivek to coordinate with procurement and infra. I flagged the procurement ticket and cc'd procurement@vendorco.com.

—Lauren
Lauren Bishop
Director, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

Date: Tue, 10 Mar 2026 14:28:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Mid-Q OKR touchpoint: reprioritization and open dependencies

Marissa / all —

OKR: "Launch Redwood Optimize pilot with three customers and a model-cost reduction playbook"
- RAG: Yellow -> Red for the customer pilot integration (one of three). Integration with Acme failed due to data-format mismatch and contract clause on logging retention.
- Suggested re-scope: defer non-essential analytics dashboards for the pilot; focus on six core optimizations and documentation for customer-runner. Keep enrollment to two customers for Q2 while we remediate the Acme contract issue.
- Blockers & owners: Legal/Procurement (external contract clause) + Security (retention policy) — Jessica from Acme (jessica@acme-corp.com) is waiting on our updated data retention language. Procurement ticket pending; referenced PR #ENGINF-742.
- Impact: If contract and retention are not aligned, we lose Acme as a pilot and miss the 3-pilot target, which reduces our QBR ask credibility.

Action items: requesting Marissa to escalate to Legal and Procurement. I can draft the redline language this afternoon if someone from Legal/procurement is looped in.

—Mateo
Mateo Alvarez
Head of Customer Solutions
Redwood Inference

Subject: Re: Mid-Q OKR touchpoint: reprioritization and open dependencies — consolidated asks and next steps

Thanks for the updates — this is exactly what I needed. Consolidated takeaways and owners below (I'll copy this into the QBR doc and send a short ask note to legal/procurement):

1) Serving runtime throughput (Lauren) — RAG: Yellow.
   - Ask: Vivek to secure 2x A100-equivalent burst capacity or confirm alternate GPU SKU by Fri 3/13. Procurement ticket REQ-3421 (procurement@vendorco.com).
   - Temporary re-scope approved: move dynamic KV eviction to Q3.

2) Optimize pilot (Mateo) — RAG: Yellow -> Red for Acme integration.
   - Ask: Marissa to escalate to Legal & Procurement; Mateo to draft redline language by EOD 3/11; Jessica (Acme) looped here to align on retention window (thanks Jessica).
   - Temporary re-scope: limit to 2 pilots for Q2; keep core optimization features prioritized in workbook.

3) Cross-team dependency tracking
   - Ask: Tessa to maintain the dependency-tracker.csv (attached) and own weekly sync with procurement/security.
   - Tessa to add a severity column (blocker/high/med) and next-review date.

Next steps: I will:
- Send a short escalation note to Legal & Procurement tomorrow AM with the redlines and PO references.
- Update the QBR deck with the reprioritized list and visible asks for exec sign-off.

Please confirm owners and ETA on the above by EOD Thursday 3/12 so I can finalize the QBR deck before Friday leadership review.

Marissa
Marissa Cole
Head of Product Operations
Redwood Inference

title: Scheduling: Lumera product demo + US/EU handoff (DST sensitivity)
preview:
From: Sofie Lindström <sofie@lumerahealth.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-06T14:12:00+01:00
Subject: Quick demo next week?

Hi Logan,

Thanks for the intro — we'd like to see Redwood's inference platform applied to our personalization pipeline. Are you available for a 45-minute demo the week of March 9? Our EMEA engineering and procurement folks (copying Anders) are available afternoons CET.

Preferred windows: Tue (Mar 9) 14:00–17:00 CET or Wed 10:00–12:00 CET.

Appreciate a couple of proposed slots so we can lock something in.

Best,
Sofie
Sofie Lindström
Senior Technical Program Manager | Lumera Health

[attachment: lumera_intro.pdf (pdf)]

From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
To: Sofie Lindström <sofie@lumerahealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-07T10:05:00-05:00
Subject: Re: Quick demo next week? — propose Tue Mar 9 11:00 ET / 16:00 CET

Hi Sofie — great to meet you.

Thanks for the windows. Can we do Tue Mar 9 at 11:00 AM ET (that should be 16:00 CET)? I've put Marissa (solutions lead) and Mateo (EMEA AE) on the thread — Mateo will take the deep product walkthrough after a 10–15 minute intro.

Calendar invite: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=lumera-demo-20260309

If that works I’ll send the invite now.

Logan
SDR, Redwood Inference

---
Attachments: none

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-08T08:22:00+01:00
Subject: Re: Quick demo next week? — timezone check

On Mon, Mar 8, 2026 at 02:22 Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com> wrote:
> Can we do Tue Mar 9 at 11:00 AM ET (that should be 16:00 CET)?

Heads up — US moves to DST on March 8 (tomorrow). CET doesn't change until later in March. That means some calendar clients will show a shifted time depending on whether the invite was created before/after the change.

I see the invite on my calendar as Tue Mar 9 15:00 CET (10:00 ET). Can we confirm the final UTC time to avoid any drift? I can cover the full demo at 15:00 CET if that's the time the client accepted, otherwise I can shift to 16:00 CET but will need to adjust my schedule.

Suggest we: 1) include UTC in the invite (e.g., 2026-03-09 15:00 UTC+1 / 11:00 UTC-4) and 2) send a brief single-line confirmation to the client with the explicit UTC time.

— Mateo
Account Executive, EMEA

From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Sofie Lindström <sofie@lumerahealth.com>, Anders Jorgensen <anders.j@lumerahealth.com>
Date: 2026-03-08T11:40:00-04:00
Subject: Re: Quick demo next week? — calendar shows 11:00 ET / 16:00 CET

Thanks for the callout Mateo — you were right about the DST quirk. I created the invite before the US DST switch; the client accepted after the switch and some calendars (including mine) adjusted differently. Right now:
- My calendar: Tue Mar 9, 11:00 AM ET (UTC-4)
- Sofie's calendar: Tue Mar 9, 16:00 CET (UTC+1)
- Mateo's calendar: Tue Mar 9, 15:00 CET (UTC+1)

That mismatch is on us. Proposal: keep the meeting at the time Anders accepted (Sofie confirmed 16:00 CET) but update the invite description to include the explicit UTC timestamp and a short note about DST. I'll reissue the invite with the UTC time and ask everyone to accept the updated event.

Marissa — can you attach the Mutual Action Plan and confirm you're comfortable leading the first 10–15 minutes before handing to Mateo? Also looping Laura for manager visibility.

Logan

[Calendar invite re-sent: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=lumera-demo-20260309-updated]

title: URGENT: Acme Retail - p95/p99 latency spike (possible routing issue)
preview:
From: support@acme-retail.com (Jules Carter)
To: marissa.cole@redwood.example
Date: 2026-03-07T08:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Intermittent latency spikes this morning

Hi Marissa —

We started seeing a big jump in API latency around 07:55 PT this morning. Our p95 went from ~120ms to ~1.1s and p99s were hitting 2.6s for several hundred requests. Error rate for token streaming also climbed briefly.

This is affecting our checkout recommendation calls. Can you confirm whether anything changed on Redwood's side? We have a support window later today but this is impacting live traffic.

Attached: sample-request-ids.txt (filenames as stub)

Thanks,
Jules
Support Engineer, Acme Retail


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.example>
To: rishi.malhotra@redwood.example, mateo.alvarez@redwood.example, evelyn.hart@redwood.example
Cc: ops@acme-retail.com (Daniel Reyes)
Date: 2026-03-07T08:25:00-07:00
Subject: FW: Re: Intermittent latency spikes this morning

Team — forwarding Acme's report. High-priority escalation: customer p95/p99 spiking across hosted API in us-west region, started ~07:55 PT. Customer is on production traffic (checkout recommendations) and is seeing errors.

Key asks:
- Quick triage whether this is a provider upstream event, infra capacity, or config change.
- If it's a routing/config issue, propose immediate mitigation (rollback or emergency reroute).
- I'll coordinate comms with Acme and escalate to my manager and platform leadership if we need a cross-region action.

I attached their sample request IDs. I can jump on voice if helpful — I'm available now.

— Marissa
Account Exec, Strategic Accounts | Redwood Inference
phone: +1 415-555-0123


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.example>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.example>, mateo.alvarez@redwood.example, evelyn.hart@redwood.example
Cc: marissa.cole@redwood.example
Date: 2026-03-07T08:40:00-07:00
Subject: Re: FW: Re: Intermittent latency spikes this morning

Marissa / team — quick update from initial triage:

What I see:
- Grafana (link below) shows a pronounced jump in p95 and p99 starting at 07:54 PT for the hosted-api route servicing Acme (route tag: acme-prod-usw).
- Error rate spike correlates with increased server-side RTT and a subset of requests showing cross-region hop timeouts.
- No instance OOMs or obvious GPU saturation. CPU on control plane nodes is normal.

Important find: after pulling the routing config audit logs, there was an automated roll (or rollout) to the global router at 07:50 PT that added a new preference weight to the us-east endpoint for this route. The change was pushed by a regional autoscaler job (job id: autoscale-20260307-0750). Diff attached (routing-table-diff-20260307.txt).

Hypothesis: the new weight caused a portion of production traffic to be redirected from us-west to us-east via our cross-region proxy path. That path has ~70ms extra RTT on average for this tenant (Acme's traffic is sensitive to RTT distribution), which would explain p95/p99 jumps without instance saturation.

Immediate mitigation options:
1) Rollback the 07:50 global routing change (fast, low-risk) — recommended first.
2) Emergency reroute Acme's traffic to reserved us-west pool (requires leadership sign-off if we exceed reserved capacity).
3) Apply a temporary prefix cache tweak to reduce tokenization backpressure (shorter-term band-aid).

I'll prepare an automated rollback runbook. If we agree, I can run the rollback and monitor metrics; estimated time-to-stable ~5-8 minutes post-rollback.

Rishi
Senior Site Reliability Engineer | Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.example>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.example>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.example>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.example>
Cc: rishi.malhotra@redwood.example, mateo.alvarez@redwood.example, evelyn.hart@redwood.example
Date: 2026-03-07T08:48:00-07:00
Subject: Escalation: Acme Retail experiencing production p95/p99 spike — need approval for rollback

Markus / Jonas / Vivek —

Acme (Strategic account, active expansion deal ACME-2025-Expansion) is seeing a sharp p95/p99 latency regression and transient token streaming errors starting ~07:55 PT. Rishi traced this to a global routing weight change pushed at 07:50 PT that increased preference for us-east, causing cross-region hops and higher RTT for Acme's sessions.

Rishi recommends an immediate rollback of the 07:50 routing change (option 1) which is low-risk and should restore p95/p99 within ~5-8m. Option 2 is an emergency reroute to reserved capacity but may incur committed capacity side effects.

Customer impact: checkout recommendation latency, potential lost conversions. They're asking for immediate remediation and an RCA once stable.

Requesting approval to:
- Proceed with Rishi's rollback now (we will communicate to Acme within the hour and follow up with RCA and credits discussion).
- If approved, I'll coordinate the customer notification and post-incident follow-up.

Please reply-all with APPROVE or otherwise.

Thanks,
Marissa


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.example>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.example>, rishi.malhotra@redwood.example, evelyn.hart@redwood.example
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.example>
Date: 2026-03-07T08:56:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Escalation: Acme Retail experiencing production p95/p99 spike — need approval for rollback

APPROVE — Markus verbally acknowledged on call. I've initiated the rollback using the automated runbook (rollback-runbook.pdf attached).

Action taken:
- Rolled back global routing change id autoscale-20260307-0750 at 08:58 PT.
- Monitored metrics: p95 dropped from ~1.1s to ~140ms within ~6 minutes, p99 back below 400ms. Error rate normalized.

Attachments: grafana-p95-p99-20260307.png (before/after), routing-table-diff-20260307.txt

Marissa — please inform Acme that we've reverted the change and we're investigating root cause and will provide RCA + proposed credits.

Mateo
Platform Operations Engineer | Redwood Inference


From: Jules Carter <support@acme-retail.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.example>, mateo.alvarez@redwood.example
Date: 2026-03-07T17:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: URGENT: Acme Retail - p95/p99 latency spike (possible routing issue)

Thanks for the quick turnaround — we're seeing normal latency again on our end (p95 back in the ~120-160ms range). Appreciate the fast response.

Requesting:
- A short RCA that explains why the autoscale job changed the global routing weights and what controls will prevent an unintended reroute in future.
- Please include any proposed credits for the affected window (07:50–08:58 PT).

We can take the RCA offline — would like it by EOD tomorrow.

Best,
Jules


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.example>
To: Jules Carter <support@acme-retail.com (Jules Carter)>, ops@acme-retail.com (Daniel Reyes)
Cc: rishi.malhotra@redwood.example, mateo.alvarez@redwood.example, evelyn.hart@redwood.example, markus.klein@redwood.example
Date: 2026-03-07T17:25:00-07:00
Subject: Re: URGENT: Acme Retail - p95/p99 latency spike (possible routing issue)

Jules / Daniel —

Thanks for confirming. We're preparing a short RCA and will include: timeline, root cause (autoscaler weight change to global router triggered unintended cross-region routing), mitigation steps taken (rollback), and concrete action items to prevent recurrence (change control for autoscaler, alerting on routing-weight deltas, and a two-minute warmup reroute test).

We will also propose a service credit for the impacted window (07:50–08:58 PT) and follow up with your procurement lead (cc'd) to finalize. Expect the RCA by EOD tomorrow.

Apologies again for the disruption, and thank you for the quick verification.

— Marissa

title: SeedKit: combining onboarding credits with reserved capacity timing
preview:
From: Samira Khan <samira@seedkit.ai>
To: irene.choi@redwood.com
Date: Wed, 18 Feb 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Quick question on onboarding credits + reserved commitment timing

Hi Irene,

Thanks again for the demo last week — team is excited by the latency figures. Two quick questions from our CFO/ops: can the 50k onboarding credits we got from the accelerator be combined with an early reserved-capacity commit (we plan to go reserved in Q2)? In particular: 

- Are credits usable against a Reserved/Dedicated invoice or only against hosted usage?
- If we sign a 12-month reserved contract in March, can we delay when the reserved billing starts by up to 30 days while we finish migration?

Also looping in legal (legal@seedkit.ai) who asked for the term sheet. Attaching a brief summary of our expected consumption.

Best,
Samira
Head of Finance, SeedKit
Attachment: seedkit_onboarding_term_sheet.pdf (pdf)


From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
To: Samira Khan <samira@seedkit.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Feb 2026 12:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Quick question on onboarding credits + reserved commitment timing

Hi Samira — great questions. Short answers up front: yes (in most cases) and usually yes on a short start delay, but there are a few operational caveats. Details below so legal and finance have something they can rely on for the contract.

Summary: 
- Onboarding credits: can be applied to covered token usage across Hosted API and, at activation, converted to a credit balance that offsets invoices tied to a customer account. For Reserved (Dedicated) we require the credits to be converted to a billing credit applied to the invoice account that pays for the reserved nodes.
- Timing/start delay: we can support a 30-day deferred billing window from contract signature for Reserved commits for migrations, provided the first invoice still occurs within the first billing period and usage thresholds for autoscaling are agreed in the SOW.

Operational notes/controls (what we need):
- We need the account ID that will own the Reserved contract and confirmation that SeedKit accepts credits being applied to that billing account.
- For a conversion we usually require a short amendment: one paragraph in the SOW that references promo credit ID and the reserved contract number.

I've attached the standard reserved pricing table and a sample amendment. Ben / Laura — can you confirm if the promo-credit conversion needs a legal redline or if finance ops can do a short addendum?

Thanks,
Irene
Sales Lead, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
Attachment: redwood-dedicated-pricing-table.xlsx (xlsx)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Samira Khan <samira@seedkit.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Feb 2026 08:43:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Quick question on onboarding credits + reserved commitment timing

Samira — quick operational follow-up from finance/ops side:

- We can map the accelerator promo credit to the billing account, but credits that were originally issued for a trial may have an expiry. Can you confirm the promo code ID on the term sheet you attached? If it's the accelerator batch 'SK-ACC-0226' we already flagged it for conversion prep.
- On the 30-day delay: we'll mark the reserved pool as "pending" in our provisioning system. That avoids charging compute hours but reserves capacity for the agreed start window. There is a small setup fee that can be waived for SeedKit given this is part of the pilot-to-paid conversion — I'll propose that in the commercial note.

title: Dedicated: override requirements + precedence for batching SLO tiers (fast/standard/cost-efficient)
preview:
From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sofia Ivanova <sofia.ivanova@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 18, 2026 at 9:14 AM
Subject: Dedicated: override requirements + precedence for batching SLO tiers (fast/standard/cost-efficient)
Attachments: Dedicated-batching-tier-overrides-requirements-v1.pdf

Sofia — looping you in as the runtime owner for scheduling.

We’re getting consistent asks from Dedicated prospects/customers: they want the new batching SLO tiers *as defaults*, but with clear override semantics so they can meet contract SLAs and avoid surprises during rollout.

I attached a 1-pager, but the key product requirements for Dedicated are:

1) Override levels + precedence (must be unambiguous)
- Dedicated tenant override (cluster/tenant-level) should win over any route policy and any global default.
- Route-level policy should win over global default.
- Global default tier remains for “no config” cases.

Proposed precedence order (please confirm works for runtime/control-plane):
Tenant override > Route/model policy > Tier default (workload-aware selection) > Fallback behavior (overload mode / capacity constraints).

2) Scope of overrides
- Set default tier per tenant (fast/standard/cost-efficient).
- Optionally set per-route tier (for streaming endpoints vs batch summarization).
- Ability to “pin” batching profile version (or at least pin tier behavior) for SLA stability during a contract period.

3) SLA + change management requirements
- For Dedicated SLAs, we need a statement of what is/ isn’t impacted when tiers roll out (runtime version + tier mapping changes).
- A kill switch at tenant level (immediate revert to previous behavior) is required.
- We should be able to explain “effective tier” in Console and via API, including whether selection came from override vs default.

4) Edge cases we need specified
- What happens if tenant forces fast tier but workload is mostly long prompts + high concurrency? (admission control / backpressure expectations)
- Overload mode: do we ever *change tier* automatically or do we keep tier constant and degrade via load shed?

Can you respond with:
(a) what the runtime will support in 1.22 vs later,
(b) what knobs are safe to expose for Dedicated overrides,
(c) any constraints we should build into messaging/SLAs.

Thanks,
Lauren

—
Lauren Bishop
Product Manager, Redwood Dedicated
Redwood Inference



From: Sofia Ivanova <sofia.ivanova@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 18, 2026 at 11:02 AM
Subject: Re: Dedicated: override requirements + precedence for batching SLO tiers (fast/standard/cost-efficient)

This all makes sense and is consistent w/ how we’ve been thinking about policy evaluation.

Runtime perspective / constraints:

1) Precedence
Your proposed order is implementable. One nuance: “fallback behavior (capacity constraints)” isn’t purely last in the chain — e.g., model constraints or safety guardrails can clamp batching even if tenant forces cost-efficient.

I’d phrase as:
- Determine requested tier via precedence (tenant > route > default)
- Validate vs model/hw constraints (may clamp profile)
- Apply overload mode guardrails (may tighten wait budget / shed load)

2) What we can support for 1.22
- Tenant default tier override: YES
- Per-route tier override: YES (assuming control plane surfaces it; runtime side is straightforward)
- Pin “profile version”: TBD. We can pin tier->knobs mapping by runtime version; pinning *independently* of runtime version gets complex.

3) Safe knobs to expose
Strong preference: expose tier selection + a limited set of guardrail knobs (e.g., max_wait_ms cap) rather than raw scheduler knobs.
Reason: raw knobs are a foot-gun and will bypass the workload-aware heuristics.

4) Overload mode behavior
We should NOT automatically change tiers. Tier remains constant; overload mode can:
- reduce batching wait time (protect latency)
- apply admission control / reject early
- shed load more aggressively for fast tier

If tenant forces fast with long prompts + high concurrency: we’ll keep p99 protections by tightening wait budgets and shedding sooner. Throughput may suffer. This needs to be explicit in Dedicated docs.

If helpful, I can jump on a 30 min working session with you + Ethan/Benji to confirm control-plane schema and reason codes.

-Sofia

—
Sofia Ivanova
Engineering Manager, Scheduling (Serving Runtime)
Redwood Inference



From: Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sofia Ivanova <sofia.ivanova@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 18, 2026 at 11:26 AM
Subject: Re: Dedicated: override requirements + precedence for batching SLO tiers (fast/standard/cost-efficient)

+1 on not exposing raw knobs.

Two Hosted API parity questions that might impact Dedicated requirements/messaging:
1) Will we standardize on the same reason codes everywhere? (override vs default vs constraint vs overload)
2) Can we guarantee that “fast” means the same thing across Hosted and Dedicated, or will Dedicated clusters have a different tier->knob mapping due to capacity variance?

If Dedicated mapping can diverge, we should avoid promising absolute numbers in the tier name itself (and stick to “target SLOs”).

-Nikhil



From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Ivanova <sofia.ivanova@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 18, 2026 at 12:08 PM
Subject: Re: Dedicated: override requirements + precedence for batching SLO tiers (fast/standard/cost-efficient)

This is helpful.

Re: “pin profile version” — I’m ok if v1 is “pinned to runtime version” as long as we can offer:
- explicit change notices when tier mappings change across runtime upgrades
- opt-in window / canary for Dedicated tenants before it becomes default

On Dedicated mapping divergence: I think we should assume it *can* diverge per hardware class (A10 vs L4 vs H100 clusters, etc.). For SLAs, we’ll anchor to the Dedicated contract’s latency terms and describe tier as an internal batching policy that is *compatible with* the contract.

Concrete asks from Dedicated side:
- We need a documented answer to: “If I set fast tier, does Redwood ever silently relax it?” Sounds like: no tier change, but overload guardrails may shed.
- Console needs to show “Effective tier” + “Why” (reason code) because Support is already seeing confusion around precedence.

Sofia — if you can share the proposed reason code taxonomy (even draft), I’ll incorporate into Dedicated requirements + Support playbook.

Lauren



From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Ivanova <sofia.ivanova@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 18, 2026 at 1:41 PM
Subject: Re: Dedicated: override requirements + precedence for batching SLO tiers (fast/standard/cost-efficient)

Aligning on the product stance:

- Dedicated should get strong controls (tenant + route overrides) but we should keep the interface “tier-first.”
- For SLA: fast tier is *not* a promise to never shed; it’s a promise that the scheduler will prioritize tail latency and protect the cluster by shedding earlier.

Action items:
1) Sofia: please post draft reason codes + precedence evaluation order to the design doc and link here.
2) Lauren: update Dedicated PRD section with language about runtime-version pinning + upgrade notice process.
3) Nikhil/Aditya: confirm shared taxonomy for reason codes and Console surface area.

Also: make sure ENG-18430 explicitly covers tenant-level kill switch semantics (revert-to-previous vs disable-tier-labeling vs disable-workload-aware selection).

Mateo

—
Mateo Alvarez
VP Engineering, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference



From: Sofia Ivanova <sofia.ivanova@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 19, 2026 at 4:37 PM
Subject: Re: Dedicated: override requirements + precedence for batching SLO tiers (fast/standard/cost-efficient)

Draft reason codes we’re implementing (names may change, but semantics should hold):

Tier source
- TIER_DEFAULT_WORKLOAD_AWARE (no explicit config; selected via heuristics)
- TIER_ROUTE_POLICY
- TIER_TENANT_OVERRIDE

Constraints / clamping
- PROFILE_CLAMP_MODEL_CONSTRAINT (e.g., max batch tokens limited by model family)
- PROFILE_CLAMP_HARDWARE_CLASS (e.g., different caps on smaller GPUs)

Overload / protection modes
- OVERLOAD_ADMISSION_CONTROL
- OVERLOAD_SHED_LOAD
- OVERLOAD_WAIT_BUDGET_TIGHTENED

We’ll emit:
- effective_tier
- requested_tier
- reason_code
- (optional) clamp_reason

Re: kill switch: current plan is “disable tiered defaults and revert to legacy batching config for that tenant/cluster” (not just hide labels). That gives Dedicated an immediate escape hatch.

If you want a sharper line for SLAs: I suggest we keep it simple — tier is a scheduler policy; SLA is measured at endpoint and enforced by backpressure and admission control under overload.

-Sofia
