title: Runtime 1.21 — Kernel Auto-selection v2 rollout + risk review (canary/rollback/comms)
preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison_grant@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar_elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha_bello@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 8:17 AM PT
Subject: Runtime 1.21 — Kernel Auto-selection v2 rollout + risk review (canary/rollback/comms)
Attachments: runtime-1.21-launch-brief.pdf; kernel-autoselection-v2-rollout-checklist.xlsx

Team — before we lock the 1.21 canary window, I want a crisp risk review + decision/authority alignment for kernel auto-selection v2.

Can you confirm / propose:
1) Canary schedule (Hosted first, then Dedicated pools) with named “go/no-go” checkpoints.
2) Rollback authority + who is on point (single-threaded) if we see tail spikes.
3) Customer comms guidance: what we say in release notes vs when we proactively reach out (esp. Dedicated/Private customers with constrained SKUs).

Constraints: zero correctness regressions, deterministic selection/replay requirements, and we can’t take p99 hits on long-context workloads.

I’m attaching the current launch brief + checklist draft. Please reply-all with edits so we can close this today.

— Mateo
Executive Sponsor, Runtime


---

From: Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Allison Grant <allison_grant@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar_elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha_bello@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 9:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Runtime 1.21 — Kernel Auto-selection v2 rollout + risk review (canary/rollback/comms)

Proposed schedule (assuming perf suite is green by EOD Fri):

Hosted:
- Mon 2/16 10:00–14:00 PT: internal canary (5% traffic) in us-west + us-east.
- Tue 2/17 10:00 PT: expand to 25% (same regions) if gates pass.
- Wed 2/18 10:00 PT: expand to 50% + add eu-west if no regression.

Dedicated:
- Thu 2/19: 1 dedicated pool (A100) w/ customer-notified maintenance window.
- Fri 2/20: 1 dedicated pool (H100) if A100 stable.

Checkpoints:
- p95 and p99 latency deltas vs v1 within budgets (Aisha to confirm exact thresholds from SLO gates).
- error rate flat (no increase in invalid outputs / kernel launch failures)
- selection telemetry cardinality stable (Allison’s note from INT-10411)

Rollback: I recommend a single “stop/go” owner per window. Happy to be the release train owner; need SRE to confirm who has authority to flip feature gate quickly across regions.

Comms: release notes mention “improved GPU kernel selection for better tail latency stability” with a one-liner about the feature gate and rollback procedure. I do *not* want broad proactive outreach unless we hit a supportable customer-facing behavior change.

Connor
Release Train Lead


---

From: Allison Grant <allison_grant@redwoodinference.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar_elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha_bello@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 9:26 AM PT
Subject: Re: Runtime 1.21 — Kernel Auto-selection v2 rollout + risk review (canary/rollback/comms)

+1 on schedule.

Two specific observability/ops risks to call out:

1) Telemetry + dashboards: we had alert noise last time due to metric cardinality change (INT-10411). For v2 selection events, we need hard sampling caps in Hosted and a strict label allowlist (model_family/model_version/gpu_sku/seq_bucket only). No raw model IDs in high-cardinality labels.

2) Rollback motion: SRE needs an explicit “rollback trigger” table. If we’re waiting for a meeting, we’re already late.

Proposal for triggers (we can tune numbers):
- p99 latency +10% vs baseline for >15 min in any region/model_family/sku combination
- or kernel launch / runtime errors +0.25% absolute increase
- or selection distribution shift >20% to non-preferred kernel on a SKU (suggests misclassification)

If triggered, oncall can:
- disable v2 at the feature gate for the affected region
- additionally denylist (model_family, gpu_sku) pair if we’ve isolated the bad combo

Omar/Aisha: can you confirm the dashboards have a clean “v1 vs v2” slice for canary?

Allison
Observability Standards + Alerts


---

From: Aisha Bello <aisha_bello@redwoodinference.com>
To: Allison Grant <allison_grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar_elsayed@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 10:11 AM PT
Subject: Re: Runtime 1.21 — Kernel Auto-selection v2 rollout + risk review (canary/rollback/comms)

Confirmed on SLO gates (from slo-toolkit/pr-284):

Hosted canary budgets (per region, aggregated by model_family + seq_bucket):
- p95: <= +5% regression vs 7-day baseline
- p99: <= +8% regression vs 7-day baseline
- error rate: <= +0.10% absolute

If we exceed p99 budget for >10 min OR error budget for >5 min => automated recommendation triggers (not an auto-rollback yet), but we should treat that as “pause expansion” and likely rollback.

Dashboards:
- We have v2_enabled=true/false split.
- We also have chosen_kernel and fallback_reason panels (sampled).

Action for Omar: verify the “long-context” view is correctly keyed off seq_bucket=long_prefill (we’ve had confusion between prompt_len vs total_len in earlier panels).

Aisha
SLO Dashboards + Rollout Guardrails


---

From: Omar El-Sayed <omar_elsayed@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aisha Bello <aisha_bello@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison_grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 11:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Runtime 1.21 — Kernel Auto-selection v2 rollout + risk review (canary/rollback/comms)

Re: long-context view — yes, we’ll key off the selector’s seq_bucket (the exact bucket used in decision logic), not a proxy derived in the dashboard.

Also want to flag the prior failure mode we saw during early perf runs: long-context summarization traffic (large prefill) getting routed to a “fast decode” kernel on L40S, causing p99 spikes.

Mitigations in place now:
- conservative thresholds on long_prefill bucket
- explicit fallback precedence on SKU when preferred kernel missing/unstable
- guardrail to disable v2 on known-bad driver/CUDA combos

For rollback authority: suggest we keep it simple:
- Connor is “release owner” for expansion steps
- SRE oncall is the execution arm (flip gates, denylist)
- Mateo is final tie-breaker if we’re on the fence about proceeding after a partial rollback

Comms: I agree w/ Connor: don’t proactively blast customers. But we should pre-brief Support/CS with a one-pager: symptom patterns + how to pin/disable.

Omar
Runtime Observability + Tail Latency


---

From: Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Allison Grant <allison_grant@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar_elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha_bello@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 12:18 PM PT
Subject: Re: Runtime 1.21 — Kernel Auto-selection v2 rollout + risk review (canary/rollback/comms)

Capturing decisions + asks so we can close:

Decisions (proposed):
1) Canary schedule as Connor outlined (Hosted Mon–Wed; Dedicated Thu–Fri), with expansion pauses on any SLO gate breach.
2) Rollback authority: Connor owns go/no-go for expansion; SRE oncall executes rollback immediately upon trigger; Mateo as escalation/tie-break.
3) Comms: release notes only + internal support brief; proactive customer outreach only if (a) we disable v2 for a Dedicated customer pool or (b) we recommend a pin/override.

Open items to close today:
- Allison: confirm sampling caps + metric label allowlist is merged and deployed prior to canary.
- Aisha/Omar: confirm long-context panel matches selector’s seq_bucket.
- Connor: add the trigger table into the launch brief + checklist (so oncall has it in one place).

If you all agree, I’ll mark this as final in the rollout checklist and send a short note to eng-releases channel.

Talia
Program Manager


---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Allison Grant <allison_grant@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar_elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha_bello@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 1:04 PM PT
Subject: Re: Runtime 1.21 — Kernel Auto-selection v2 rollout + risk review (canary/rollback/comms)

Approved with one tweak:

- If we see p99 regression on long-context (seq_bucket=long_prefill) on *any* SKU during Hosted canary, we rollback first and analyze second. We cannot “watch it” for an hour.

Otherwise aligned on authority + comms.

Connor — please make sure the rollback trigger table is explicit about the long-context case.

Mateo


---

From: Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Allison Grant <allison_grant@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar_elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha_bello@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 13, 2026 at 4:41 PM PT
Subject: Re: Runtime 1.21 — Kernel Auto-selection v2 rollout + risk review (canary/rollback/comms)

Done:
- Updated launch brief + checklist with rollback trigger table (including “immediate rollback on long_prefill p99 breach”).
- Added named DRI per window: Connor (release), SRE oncall (execution), Omar (runtime SME), Allison/Aisha (dashboards).
- Added a short Support/CS guidance paragraph + link placeholder to runbook (disable v2 by model/SKU/region; pin kernel; validate recovery).

Allison confirms telemetry sampling caps landed and will be in place before canary (separate note incoming).

Unless objections, we’re locked for Mon 2/16 10:00 PT start.

Connor

title: FP8/INT8 perf sprint — exec update + risk plan (needs capacity + priority alignment)
preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: 
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 9:14 AM PST
Subject: FP8/INT8 perf sprint — exec update + risk plan (needs capacity + priority alignment)

Neha, Maya — quick mid-sprint exec update + the risk plan. We’re on track technically, but we need two decisions today to avoid either (a) slipping the matrix coverage or (b) taking on unacceptable rollout risk.

Where we are (as of this morning)
- Harness: FP8/INT8 modes are wired end-to-end for both microbench and serving runs; artifacts include SHA/CUDA/driver/clocks metadata.
- First calibrated runs: H100 + A100 baseline vs FP8 attention + INT8 matmul are stable after warmup changes and clock pinning.
- Early signal (directional):
  - H100: FP8 attention is a clear throughput win in decode-heavy regimes; mixed workloads show smaller wins due to scheduling/launch overhead.
  - A100: INT8 matmul wins are shape-dependent; we need guardrails to avoid correctness + perf cliffs.

Key risks + mitigations (what could bite us)
1) Quality regressions under quantization (highest risk)
   - We have one long-context regression flagged on a long-form summarization prompt set (model-family specific). Not catastrophic, but it’s visible in eval deltas.
   - Mitigation: require eval sign-off + keep FP8/INT8 default off for the impacted model until we have either (a) a calibration tweak or (b) an auto-fallback policy for long-context.
   - Ask for Maya: alignment that “default enablement” is gated on the quick sanity suite + one targeted long-context subset, not just kernel correctness.

2) Benchmark reproducibility / noise (medium risk)
   - Variance spikes were correlated with clocks not locking on shared nodes + driver mismatch across a subset of H100 clusters.
   - Mitigation: runner pins container/driver; we route runs only to approved pools; rerun policy on outlier variance.

3) Customer-impact risk during rollout (medium risk)
   - We’ve already seen that kernel wins can disappear (or invert) when prefill/decode interleaving changes.
   - Mitigation: per-model/per-region flags + perf canary gates on TTFT/TPOT with auto rollback; SRE on point.

What we need today (two decisions)
A) Capacity alignment (Neha)
- We need an additional 220 H100-hours (spread across 2 regions) to complete the full matrix for top hosted models and to rerun any high-variance cases with pinned drivers.
- If we don’t get this by EOD, we’ll either drop L4 coverage or reduce run counts (which weakens defensibility).
- Guardrail proposal: hard block against Dedicated customer pools; all runs in reserved benchmark partitions; no opportunistic spillover.

B) Quality gate / default policy alignment (Maya)
- Proposed default enablement policy for hosted catalog:
  1) No default enablement without: correctness + quick quality sanity suite pass + targeted long-context check.
  2) If quality delta > threshold or long-context regression present: keep quantized mode behind opt-in flag + implement auto-fallback for long contexts.
  3) For “safe” models: enable FP8 on H100 first (limited regions), then expand; INT8 stays opt-in until we finish calibration guidance.

Attachments
- fp8-int8-sprint-exec-onepager-2026-02-12.pdf
- h100-reservation-asks-and-costs-2026-02-12.xlsx

Pointers (for reference)
- Linear: ENG-7421 (epic), ENG-7428 (run full matrix)
- Drive: fp8-int8-benchmark-matrix-v1
- Confluence: kernel benchmarking methodology (top hosted models)

If you reply-all with approval/edits on A and B, we can keep the sprint on its current timeline and still ship behind flags with a defensible benchmark story.

— Mateo
Mateo Alvarez
Engineering (Serving Runtime)
Redwood Inference


From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: 
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 10:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: FP8/INT8 perf sprint — exec update + risk plan (needs capacity + priority alignment)

Approved on the additional 220 H100-hours with the guardrails you proposed.

Conditions (please confirm):
1) No impact to customer Dedicated pools — reservation must be isolated (benchmark partition only).
2) Use the pinned driver/container runner only; if a cluster can’t meet the pin, we don’t run there.
3) Daily update in #eng-infra with: hours consumed vs plan + any contention incidents.

Mateo — please work with Irene/Vanessa to lock the reservation windows today.

Neha
Neha Kapoor
VP Infrastructure
Redwood Inference

On Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 9:14 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Neha, Maya — quick mid-sprint exec update + the risk plan...


From: Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: 
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 11:26 AM PST
Subject: Re: FP8/INT8 perf sprint — exec update + risk plan (needs capacity + priority alignment)

Aligned on gating. Please do NOT default-enable any quantized mode purely off kernel correctness.

What I’m comfortable signing off on for “default on”:
- Passes quick sanity suite (the one tied to the benchmark suite prompt sets), AND
- No statistically meaningful regression on the targeted long-context subset, OR we have a deterministic fallback (e.g., long-context route to baseline) that is tested + canary’d.

For the model-family with the long-context regression:
- Keep opt-in only.
- I want the mitigation documented (what workloads are impacted + how we detect it) before we flip anything beyond a small canary.

If you can add one line item in the exec readout that makes the policy explicit (default-on criteria + what stays opt-in), I’ll own the messaging internally.

Maya

text:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: 
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 9:14 AM PST
Subject: FP8/INT8 perf sprint — exec update + risk plan (needs capacity + priority alignment)

Neha, Maya — quick mid-sprint exec update + the risk plan. We’re on track technically, but we need two decisions today to avoid either (a) slipping the matrix coverage or (b) taking on unacceptable rollout risk.

Where we are (as of this morning)
- Harness: FP8/INT8 modes are wired end-to-end for both microbench and serving runs; artifacts include SHA/CUDA/driver/clocks metadata.
- First calibrated runs: H100 + A100 baseline vs FP8 attention + INT8 matmul are stable after warmup changes and clock pinning.
- Early signal (directional):
  - H100: FP8 attention is a clear throughput win in decode-heavy regimes; mixed workloads show smaller wins due to scheduling/launch overhead.
  - A100: INT8 matmul wins are shape-dependent; we need guardrails to avoid correctness + perf cliffs.

Key risks + mitigations (what could bite us)
1) Quality regressions under quantization (highest risk)
   - We have one long-context regression flagged on a long-form summarization prompt set (model-family specific). Not catastrophic, but it’s visible in eval deltas.
   - Mitigation: require eval sign-off + keep FP8/INT8 default off for the impacted model until we have either (a) a calibration tweak or (b) an auto-fallback policy for long-context.
   - Ask for Maya: alignment that “default enablement” is gated on the quick sanity suite + one targeted long-context subset, not just kernel correctness.

2) Benchmark reproducibility / noise (medium risk)
   - Variance spikes were correlated with clocks not locking on shared nodes + driver mismatch across a subset of H100 clusters.
   - Mitigation: runner pins container/driver; we route runs only to approved pools; rerun policy on outlier variance.

3) Customer-impact risk during rollout (medium risk)
   - We’ve already seen that kernel wins can disappear (or invert) when prefill/decode interleaving changes.
   - Mitigation: per-model/per-region flags + perf canary gates on TTFT/TPOT with auto rollback; SRE on point.

What we need today (two decisions)
A) Capacity alignment (Neha)
- We need an additional 220 H100-hours (spread across 2 regions) to complete the full matrix for top hosted models and to rerun any high-variance cases with pinned drivers.
- If we don’t get this by EOD, we’ll either drop L4 coverage or reduce run counts (which weakens defensibility).
- Guardrail proposal: hard block against Dedicated customer pools; all runs in reserved benchmark partitions; no opportunistic spillover.

B) Quality gate / default policy alignment (Maya)
- Proposed default enablement policy for hosted catalog:
  1) No default enablement without: correctness + quick quality sanity suite pass + targeted long-context check.
  2) If quality delta > threshold or long-context regression present: keep quantized mode behind opt-in flag + implement auto-fallback for long contexts.
  3) For “safe” models: enable FP8 on H100 first (limited regions), then expand; INT8 stays opt-in until we finish calibration guidance.

Attachments
- fp8-int8-sprint-exec-onepager-2026-02-12.pdf
- h100-reservation-asks-and-costs-2026-02-12.xlsx

Pointers (for reference)
- Linear: ENG-7421 (epic), ENG-7428 (run full matrix)
- Drive: fp8-int8-benchmark-matrix-v1
- Confluence: kernel benchmarking methodology (top hosted models)

If you reply-all with approval/edits on A and B, we can keep the sprint on its current timeline and still ship behind flags with a defensible benchmark story.

— Mateo
Mateo Alvarez
Engineering (Serving Runtime)
Redwood Inference


From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: 
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 10:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: FP8/INT8 perf sprint — exec update + risk plan (needs capacity + priority alignment)

Approved on the additional 220 H100-hours with the guardrails you proposed.

Conditions (please confirm):
1) No impact to customer Dedicated pools — reservation must be isolated (benchmark partition only).
2) Use the pinned driver/container runner only; if a cluster can’t meet the pin, we don’t run there.
3) Daily update in #eng-infra with: hours consumed vs plan + any contention incidents.

Mateo — please work with Irene/Vanessa to lock the reservation windows today.

Neha
Neha Kapoor

Aligned on gating. Please do NOT default-enable any quantized mode purely off kernel correctness.

What I’m comfortable signing off on for “default on”:
- Passes quick sanity suite (the one tied to the benchmark suite prompt sets), AND
- No statistically meaningful regression on the targeted long-context subset, OR we have a deterministic fallback (e.g., long-context route to baseline) that is tested + canary’d.

For the model-family with the long-context regression:
- Keep opt-in only.
- I want the mitigation documented (what workloads are impacted + how we detect it) before we flip anything beyond a small canary.

If you can add one line item in the exec readout that makes the policy explicit (default-on criteria + what stays opt-in), I’ll own the messaging internally.

Hey Mateo,

Two main asks: 1) can you map expected monthly cost delta if we move from the Standard uptime band to Premium (99.95 -> 99.99) at our current commit? and 2) is there an incentive if we sign an early 12-month renewal now rather than waiting until April?

We’ll likely need stronger escalation coverage (two-hour response for Sev1, on-call rotation) and a small allocation of playbook onboarding time. Amy is on copy for security and legal.

- Premium uptime (99.99) vs Standard at your commit level increases unit pricing ~14% given the dedicated on-call and prioritized capacity lanes.
- Two-hour Sev1 response and a designated on-call rotation are included in the Premium bundle; add-on playbook onboarding (8 hours) is a one-time services fee.
- Early renewal: we can offer a 7% credit applied to the first three billing cycles if you execute a 12-month renewal by March 1. If BriskPay commits to a 12-month reserved capacity uplift (10% higher commit), we can extend that to a 10% credit and reduce the per-unit uplift to ~10%.

Suggested next steps:
1) Review the attached sheet and confirm which bundle variant you want (Premium base vs Premium+Onboarding).
2) If you want the early-renewal credit, sign the renewal LOI by March 1 — we’ll route to legal for a short amendment.
3) I’ll loop in Marissa to review discount approval and approve the credit terms if needed.

I’ll be available Monday for a 30-minute walk-through.

—Mateo

Account Executive — Redwood Inference

- Proposed bands (attached): Senior Infra Eng = 160-200k base + 30-50k equity; Runtime Engineer (senior) = 150-185k base + 25-40k equity. Total OTE ranges are in the attached PDF.
- Budget ask: existing engineering HC budget covers the base salaries but we need a one-time hiring premium for relocation/market adjustments (details in spreadsheet).

Lauren

Lauren

title: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Team,

After yesterday’s near-miss (Optimize change nearly promoted without meeting gates), we need to tighten the override policy immediately.

Two goals:
1) Overrides remain possible for true emergencies (e.g., rollback unblockers), but
2) They cannot be a “convenience button” that bypasses quality and compliance.

Please reply today with:
- Who should be allowed to approve overrides (roles + named groups)
- Time bounds (how long an override can live)
- What must be recorded for audit (who/what/why + artifacts)
- Where enforcement lives (Optimize apply boundary vs rollout controller vs both)

We should also assume this will come up in enterprise security reviews. If our answer is “it’s logged somewhere,” that’s not sufficient.

Ava

—
Ava Chen
VP, Product & Platform
Redwood Inference


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 9:46 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Proposed policy (MVP → tighten further at GA if needed):

Approvers:
- Only “Optimize Override Approver” role (not general admin), initially: Optimize oncall (rotation) + Optimize PM lead (me) as backup.
- For enterprise accounts, require a second approver (SRE oncall or Security) depending on reason code.

Time bounds:
- Default 24h expiration.
- Max 72h (requires escalation beyond oncall).

Required evidence (must be attached/linked in the override request):
- Gate run id + summary (pass/fail deltas + flake score)
- The exact config diff hash
- Reason code (prod incident, capacity, false positive/flaky suite, customer block)
- Ticket reference (INC/SUP/INT)

Audit:
- Emit audit events on: override requested, override approved/denied, override expired, override revoked.
- Include approver identity + RBAC role + timestamps.

Enforcement:
- Block at Optimize “apply config” boundary AND rollout controller should refuse canary promotion without gate=passed OR override=active.

If we align on this today, we can update PM-482 and coordinate RBAC via INT-3391.

Aditya

—
Aditya Rao
Product, Optimize


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 10:18 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

+1 on defense-in-depth.

Security requirements I’d like to see explicitly:

1) Two-person rule when the override would allow production traffic to run on a config that failed quality gates.
   - One approver can be Product (Optimize), second should be SRE or Security (depending on reason). This avoids “PM approves their own ship.”

2) No permanent overrides.
   - 24h default is good. I’d push max to 48h unless a VP signs off.

3) Audit trail must be exportable (not only UI).
   - Audit log event must include: account_id, route_id, model_id, config_diff_hash, baseline reference, gate_run_id(s), reason code, approver(s), expiry.
   - Kevin: please confirm we can route these events into the enterprise audit export / SIEM pipeline.

4) “Break-glass” access should be rare.
   - If we need an emergency knob (e.g., to restore service during an incident), it must be separate from the normal override path and auto-notify #incidents.

Marcus

—
Marcus Lin
Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 11:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

From SRE POV:

- Agree on two-person approval if the gate is failing.
- We should distinguish “override because gates are flaky/infra broken” vs “override because we accept regression.” Those have different blast radius.

Operational asks:
1) Any override must require a ticket id (INC- / SUP- / INT-). No ticket, no override.
2) Auto-expire and auto-revoke. No “forgotten override” risk.
3) Post to #incidents (or at least #eng-sre) when an override is granted for production routes.
4) Add a dashboard panel: active overrides by route/account + time to expiry.

Also, I’d rather we keep max duration short (48h) and require Ava/Priya for anything longer.

Sean

—
Sean Gallagher
SRE Lead
Redwood Inference


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 12:24 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Couple implementation notes so we don’t end up with a policy that’s unenforceable:

- Enforcement should be in *both* places:
  (a) Optimize service blocks apply unless (gate==passed) OR (override==active)
  (b) Rollout controller blocks promotion unless (gate==passed) OR (override==active)
  This covers UI/API clients and any drift in rollout workflows.

- RBAC needs to be role-based, not “admins.” Let’s implement a dedicated role:
  QUALITY_GATE_OVERRIDE_APPROVER
  and keep membership narrow.

- Override object needs to include: scope (account/route/model), diff hash, expiry, reason code, ticket ref, and approver set.

- Please avoid “VP in the loop for every override” unless we can make it asynchronous.
  Suggestion: two-person rule (PM + SRE/Sec) for <=48h, VP escalation only for >48h.

Priya

—
Priya Natarajan
VP Engineering, Platform
Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 1:37 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss) — decision

Decision (effective immediately for Hosted API routes; extend to Dedicated as we roll out):

1) Two-person approval REQUIRED for any override that permits applying/promoting a config change when the quality gate is failing.
   - Approver A: Optimize PM oncall OR Optimize product lead (Aditya as backup)
   - Approver B: SRE oncall (primary) OR Security (Marcus delegate) if reason code is security/compliance-critical

2) Time bounds:
   - Default expiration: 24 hours
   - Maximum: 48 hours
   - >48 hours requires VP escalation (Priya or me) AND a written plan to resolve (e.g., fix flaky suite, adjust prompt coverage) + follow-up owner.

3) Required fields/evidence (no exceptions):
   - Ticket id (INC/SUP/INT). No ticket, no override.
   - Gate run id(s) + artifact links
   - Config diff hash + scope (account_id / route_id / model)
   - Reason code (choose from a fixed list)
   - Free-text justification (1–3 paragraphs) including risk assessment

4) Audit & notification:
   - Emit audit log events for request/approve/deny/expire/revoke.
   - Events must include: account_id, route_id, model_id, config_diff_hash, gate_run_id(s), reason code, both approvers, expiry timestamp.
   - Overrides granted for production must post to #incidents and be visible in Console audit view.
   - Kevin: ensure these events are included in audit export (enterprise ask).

5) Enforcement must be defense-in-depth:
   - Optimize apply boundary AND rollout controller promotion checks must enforce gate==passed OR override==active.

Talia: please capture this as an update in PM-482 / the Confluence policy doc and track the RBAC change in INT-3391.

Ava


From: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 2:41 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss) — decision

Captured. Action items I’m tracking:

- Update override policy doc + link decision section (PM-482)
- RBAC: create QUALITY_GATE_OVERRIDE_APPROVER role + limit membership (INT-3391)
- Console: add “Active overrides” view + expiry + ticket ids
- Observability: add metric + alert for active overrides > N hours remaining / unexpected spikes
- Incident comms: confirm #incidents posting format

I’ll send a short status update EOD with owners/dates.

Talia

—
Talia Benson
Program Manager
Redwood Inference

- RBAC needs to be role-based, not “admins.” Let’s implement a dedicated role:
  QUALITY_GATE_OVERRIDE_APPROVER
  and keep membership narrow.

- Override object needs to include: scope (account/route/model), diff hash, expiry, reason code, ticket ref, and approver set.

- Please avoid “VP in the loop for every override” unless we can make it asynchronous.
  Suggestion: two-person rule (PM + SRE/Sec) for <=48h, VP escalation only for >48h.

Priya

—
Priya Natarajan
VP Engineering, Platform
Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 1:37 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss) — decision

Decision (effective immediately for Hosted API routes; extend to Dedicated as we roll out):

1) Two-person approval REQUIRED for any override that permits applying/promoting a config change when the quality gate is failing.
   - Approver A: Optimize PM oncall OR Optimize product lead (Aditya as backup)
   - Approver B: SRE oncall (primary) OR Security (Marcus delegate) if reason code is security/compliance-critical

2) Time bounds:
   - Default expiration: 24 hours
   - Maximum: 48 hours
   - >48 hours requires VP escalation (Priya or me) AND a written plan to resolve (e.g., fix flaky suite, adjust prompt coverage) + follow-up owner.

3) Required fields/evidence (no exceptions):
   - Ticket id (INC/SUP/INT). No ticket, no override.
   - Gate run id(s) + artifact links
   - Config diff hash + scope (account_id / route_id / model)
   - Reason code (choose from a fixed list)
   - Free-text justification (1–3 paragraphs) including risk assessment

4) Audit & notification:
   - Emit audit log events for request/approve/deny/expire/revoke.
   - Events must include: account_id, route_id, model_id, config_diff_hash, gate_run_id(s), reason code, both approvers, expiry timestamp.
   - Overrides granted for production must post to #incidents and be visible in Console audit view.
   - Kevin: ensure these events are included in audit export (enterprise ask).

5) Enforcement must be defense-in-depth:
   - Optimize apply boundary AND rollout controller promotion checks must enforce gate==passed OR override==active.

Talia: please capture this as an update in PM-482 / the Confluence policy doc and track the RBAC change in INT-3391.

Ava


From: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 2:41 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss) — decision

Captured. Action items I’m tracking:

- Update override policy doc + link decision section (PM-482)
- RBAC: create QUALITY_GATE_OVERRIDE_APPROVER role + limit membership (INT-3391)
- Console: add “Active overrides” view + expiry + ticket ids
- Observability: add metric + alert for active overrides > N hours remaining / unexpected spikes
- Incident comms: confirm #incidents posting format

I’ll send a short status update EOD with owners/dates.

title: Approval: perf-canary paging policy (Phase 1) — owner + on-call load
preview:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 8:14 AM
Subject: Approval: perf-canary paging policy (Phase 1) — owner + on-call load

Hi Neha,

Requesting leadership/SRE sign-off to enable paging for perf-canary Phase 1 (us-east only) starting this week.

We’re ready on the implementation side; the remaining decision is the paging policy (what pages, who gets paged, expected page load).

Proposed policy (summary):
- Warning alerts (non-paging): route to #eng-runtime only. Trigger on p95 latency regression >= 10% OR tokens/sec drop >= 8% vs baseline, sustained 15m, min sample size met.
- Paging alerts (Sev2): only for “Top models” list (initially 3 models), us-east, sustained 30m, min samples + anti-flap.
- Paging alerts (Sev1): only if >= 2 top models regressing simultaneously OR regression >= 25% sustained 15m.

Ownership / routing:
- Primary responder: Serving Runtime on-call (the same rotation that owns runtime releases/rollbacks)
- Secondary: SRE on-call (only when attribution points to fleet/infra or if runtime on-call needs help isolating region/SKU impact)

On-call load expectation (based on prior 2 weeks of warning-only + threshold tuning):
- Warnings: ~2–6/day during tuning (non-paging)
- Pages: target <= 2/week after tuning; hard cap goal <= 1/day (otherwise we revisit thresholds)

Docs / references:
- Policy draft: Confluence “Per-model latency SLOs and alerting policy”
- Runbook: Confluence “Runbook: perf regression canary alert”
- Tracking: ENG-4878 + PM-642

Two specific approvals needed:
1) Are you comfortable with the page/no-page criteria for Phase 1?
2) Confirm ownership expectations: runtime on-call is primary, SRE secondary.

If OK, I’ll ask Release Eng to schedule “enable paging” in the Phase 1 checklist and we’ll post to #eng-releases.

Thanks,
Marissa
Product — Console & Rollouts
Redwood Inference


---

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 9:02 AM
Subject: Re: Approval: perf-canary paging policy (Phase 1) — owner + on-call load

Marissa — thanks.

Before I sign off, I want to make sure we’re crisp on two things:

1) “Primary responder” — are we paging the runtime on-call directly, or does this go to a broader #eng-oncall channel with runtime expected to pick it up? We’ve had confusion in the past.

2) Load expectations — I’m comfortable with <=2/week target, but I want a firm rollback/disable condition if we breach that (and who can make that call without waiting for a meeting).

Also, please confirm we’re not collecting/storing any customer prompts or IDs with these alerts (synthetic-only).

Mateo — can you sanity check the thresholds against expected runtime variance in us-east and confirm this won’t create a false-positive storm?

Neha


---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 9:47 AM
Subject: Re: Approval: perf-canary paging policy (Phase 1) — owner + on-call load

Neha,

Sanity check from runtime side:

- Thresholds: For us-east, the 10% p95 / 8% tok/s warning thresholds were noisy early, but with the current min-sample + sustain windows they’re stable. For paging, restricting to “top models only” + 30m sustained is conservative.

- Variance: We still see occasional short spikes correlated with telemetry ingestion delay and fleet rebalances. The proposed anti-flap + sustained 30m should filter those.

- False positive storm: Low risk given:
  1) page only on top models,
  2) sustained condition,
  3) min sample size,
  4) single region during Phase 1.

- Disable/rollback: We should explicitly empower runtime on-call to flip the canary paging to “warnings only” for the region if page volume exceeds the cap or if we suspect baseline drift (and then follow up in business hours).

Re: ownership, I strongly prefer this pages the runtime on-call directly. If it goes to a generic channel, it will bounce.

Finally, data: synthetic-only prompts; no customer prompts/IDs. The alert payload should only include model/version/region/SKU + observed vs baseline stats + dashboard links.

Mateo
Exec Sponsor — Serving Runtime


---

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 10:23 AM
Subject: Re: Approval: perf-canary paging policy (Phase 1) — owner + on-call load

Neha — answers inline.

> 1) “Primary responder” — are we paging the runtime on-call directly, or does this go to a broader #eng-oncall channel with runtime expected to pick it up?

Paging goes to the Serving Runtime on-call rotation directly (PagerDuty service: “Runtime - Serving”). We’ll also post a thread in #incidents for visibility + the non-paging signal will continue to go to #eng-runtime.

SRE is secondary/consult via escalation (documented in runbook). We are not creating a new “everyone” page path.

> 2) Load expectations — I’m comfortable with <=2/week target, but I want a firm rollback/disable condition if we breach that (and who can make that call without waiting for a meeting).

Proposed guardrails (will add to policy + runbook):
- If paging volume exceeds 3 pages in 24h OR >5 pages in 7 days in Phase 1, runtime on-call is authorized to:
  a) disable paging (downgrade to warnings) for the region, AND
  b) open an ENG ticket tagged “paging hygiene” for follow-up
- Decision maker: runtime on-call (no meeting required). Notify #eng-runtime + #eng-releases.

> Also, please confirm we’re not collecting/storing any customer prompts or IDs with these alerts (synthetic-only).

Confirmed: synthetic-only. Canary prompts are generated from our internal shape distribution; no request bodies are logged. Alert payloads include only aggregated metrics (observed vs baseline), model/version, region, hardware SKU, runtime build tag, kernel tag, and links.

Action items I’ll take if you approve:
- Update Confluence policy + runbook with the explicit disable criteria
- Ask Release Eng to include “paging enable” + “revert to warnings-only” steps in the Phase 1 checklist

Thanks,
Marissa


---

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 12:41 PM
Subject: Re: Approval: perf-canary paging policy (Phase 1) — owner + on-call load

Approved for Phase 1 with the guardrails captured as written.

Conditions for approval:
- Paging must target the Serving Runtime on-call rotation directly.
- Add the explicit “disable paging / downgrade to warnings” criteria to the runbook and the policy doc.
- Keep it scoped to us-east + top-model list for Phase 1; any expansion of paging scope needs a quick re-review after we have 1–2 weeks of page volume data.
- Synthetic-only, no customer data in payloads/logs.

Mateo — thank you for the variance sanity check. Please ensure runtime on-call is aligned that they can (and should) downgrade to warnings if page volume is breaching the cap.

Marissa — once the docs are updated, please link them in the release checklist thread so we have an audit trail.

Neha

title: Weekly exec update: Perf Canary rollout status (Week of Jan 26)
preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Jan 29, 2026 at 8:12 AM
Subject: Weekly exec update: Perf Canary rollout status (Week of Jan 26)

Neha,

Weekly exec update on the prod perf canary rollout (status as of Thu AM).

1) Rollout status
- Region: us-east (phase 1)
- Models covered (current):
  - llama-3.1-70b-instruct (hosted)
  - qwen2.5-72b-instruct (hosted)
  - mistral-small-24b-instruct (dedicated shared pool)
- Run mode: synthetic prompts only (no customer prompts); service account scope locked down.
- Canary load/overhead: steady-state ~0.2–0.3% GPU capacity for the covered pools in us-east; QPS caps working as expected.

2) Signals + regressions caught
- Real regression caught pre-broad rollout (good outcome):
  - Symptom: p95 end-to-end latency +11–14% on llama-3.1-70b-instruct over ~35 min window; tokens/sec down ~6–8%.
  - Attribution signals: runtime-only latency moved with it; queueing time flat; batch size distribution shifted smaller.
  - Likely cause: scheduler heuristic change in the runtime build (batched decode policy) interacting with one of the prefill length buckets.
  - Action taken: held the rollout, reverted the scheduling flag in us-east, and p95 returned within baseline band within ~20 mins.
  - Customer impact: none observed (contained to canary + small % internal traffic during controlled rollout).

3) False positives / noise
- We saw 2 warning-level flaps early in the week on qwen2.5-72b that we now believe were false positives.
  - Root issue: baseline drift combined with metric label churn (model-version tag formatting on one canary instance) created an apparent “new series” with low sample counts.
  - Fixes shipped/queued:
    - tightened minimum sample size gating + added a “series-age” requirement before alerts fire
    - normalized model+version labels (canonical registry ID) and blocked non-canonical values at emit time
    - incident-bot dedupe key now includes {model, region, runtime_build} to prevent thread spam

4) Dashboards + alerting posture
- Dashboards are live and usable for triage:
  - Overview: https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/perf-canary/perf-canary-overview?region=us-east
  - Per-model: https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/perf-canary/per-model?region=us-east
- Current alert posture:
  - Warning alerts enabled to #eng-runtime only (no paging yet)
  - Paging remains disabled until we complete one more week of threshold tuning + baseline stability checks

5) Next-week milestones (Jan 30–Feb 6)
- Expand model coverage in us-east from 3 -> 6 (top models + 1 high-risk kernel candidate)
- Baseline strategy hardening:
  - lock baseline artifacts by {model, model_version, runtime_build, GPU SKU}
  - add guardrails so baselines don’t auto-update during known rollout windows
- Enable paging for top-model sustained regressions in us-east only (target: mid/late week), contingent on:
  - <1 false page/week in “dry run”
  - stable telemetry pipeline latency (<2 min p95 ingestion)
- Rollout gates alignment (with Marissa): define “auto-pause” criteria for runtime releases (warning vs page behavior)

Requests / decisions needed
- No decisions required this week. If you want, we can do a 15-min readout next Tue with the latest false-positive rate and the revised paging thresholds.

Thanks,
Mateo

—
Mateo Alvarez
VP, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference


On Thu, Jan 29, 2026 at 9:21 AM Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Thanks Mateo — this is helpful.
>
> Two questions:
> 1) For the real regression you caught: are we confident it was scheduler-only (vs kernel selection)?
> 2) What’s your current best estimate of paging volume once enabled for top models in us-east?
>
> -Neha


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Jan 29, 2026 at 10:03 AM
Subject: Re: Weekly exec update: Perf Canary rollout status (Week of Jan 26)

1) Scheduler vs kernel: we’re not 100% certain yet, but evidence points scheduler.
- Kernel tag did not change across the window (same kernel_selection_id, same runtime build, no kernel rollout event).
- Queueing stayed flat, but runtime execution time per token increased slightly and batch sizes got smaller, which aligns with the decode batching heuristic change.
- We’re doing one controlled replay in staging today with the flag toggled to confirm causality.

2) Paging volume estimate: conservatively ~0–1 pages/week in us-east once tuned.
- We’ll start with paging only for sustained p95 regressions on the “top model” set with: (a) min sample gating, (b) 30–45 min sustained condition, and (c) anti-flap cooldown.
- If we see >1 false page in the first week, we’ll revert to warn-only and adjust thresholds.

If useful, I’ll include the staging replay result in next week’s update.

Mateo

—
Mateo Alvarez
VP, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

title: Exec update: Serving runtime tail latency investment (KV cache compaction) — timeline + risk management
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Ivanova <sofia.ivanova@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Jan 22, 2026 at 9:08 AM PST
Subject: Exec update: Serving runtime tail latency investment (KV cache compaction) — timeline + risk management

Team — sharing a quick exec update on the serving-runtime tail latency workstream (KV cache compaction + tier-aware eviction) since it’s now gating both SLO confidence and a couple of late-stage conversations.

Why this matters (business + technical):
- SLO / reliability: We’re seeing burst-driven p95/p99 spikes in sustained decode workloads with mixed sequence lengths, especially when request churn leaves the KV arena fragmented. In the worst case it creates opportunistic evictions at bad times and can tip us into OOM / request shedding.
- Cost: Today we’re compensating by leaving more headroom than we want (lower effective utilization). If we can keep KV memory contiguous/predictable under load, we should need less “safety padding” in dedicated pools and can be more aggressive on hosted concurrency without tail regressions.
- Customer pressure: This pattern shows up repeatedly in support and in enterprise eval questions (see SUP-28411, SUP-28433). PM has asked for a credible plan and crisp risk story.

What we’re doing:
- Allocator + fragmentation measurement: improve page/block layout + free-list management; add a fragmentation ratio metric that correlates with p99 spikes.
- Budgeted compaction: incremental compaction with an explicit time/bytes budget tied to scheduler boundaries (prefill/decode transitions) to avoid decode stalls.
- Tier-aware eviction: last-resort pressure relief that is safe + observable; interacts cleanly with pinned sessions.
- Guardrails: feature flag, kill-switch, backpressure integration, and rollout policy that auto-rolls back on tail regression.

Timeline (targeting runtime-1.22 train):
- Jan 20–31: discovery/measurement + final design/ADRs (in review now)
- Feb 3–21: implementation (allocator changes + scheduler hook + metrics + initial eviction policy)
- Feb 24–Mar 7: validation (benchmark lab + perf canary + stress/conformance)
- Mar 10+: staged rollout (internal canary → hosted subset → dedicated pools)

Risk management (what I want exec eyes on):
- Primary risk is introducing copy overhead / stalls during compaction. Mitigation is strict budgeting and scheduler-phase enforcement.
- Secondary risk is correctness edge cases (cancelled requests mid-relocation, pinned sessions). Mitigation is stress tests + conservative stopping conditions + kill-switch.
- We will hold rollout if: p99 up > agreed threshold, eviction rate spikes, or compaction time/bytes moved exceeds budget in steady state.

Asks:
1) Mateo/Sean: please confirm you’re aligned on “stop the line” thresholds + who has authority to flip the kill-switch during a canary.
2) Connor/Talia: I’d like this treated as a first-class item on the runtime-1.22 release readiness checklist.
3) Starting next week: weekly status note to this thread (wins, risks, current metrics, next gates). Keep it short.

Attachments:
- KV_Compaction_Review_Slides_v3.pdf
- Runtime_1.22_Rollout_Guardrails_Summary.docx

Ava Chen
VP, Product & Platform
Redwood Inference


---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Ivanova <sofia.ivanova@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Jan 22, 2026 at 10:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: Exec update: Serving runtime tail latency investment (KV cache compaction) — timeline + risk management

Aligned.

A couple notes to help frame risk:
- We are not doing “stop-the-world” defragmentation. Compaction is incremental with a hard budget (bytes moved + wall time) and can no-op if we’re in a latency-sensitive phase.
- Eviction is a pressure valve, not the primary mechanism. We expect it to be near-zero in steady state; if eviction is non-trivial, that’s a signal we should treat as capacity/traffic policy rather than “normal.”
- Omar + Sofia are owning correctness + scheduler integration; Noah is doing architecture/correctness review on the invariants.

Re: timeline, I think Feb implementation window is realistic provided we keep scope to allocator + minimal copy/pack kernels.

Mateo Alvarez
VP Runtime
Redwood Inference


---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Ivanova <sofia.ivanova@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Jan 22, 2026 at 11:11 AM PST
Subject: Re: Exec update: Serving runtime tail latency investment (KV cache compaction) — timeline + risk management

+1.

On the guardrails / authority question:
- During canary, oncall SRE has authority to flip the kill-switch if we hit rollback criteria (p99 sustained breach + compaction/eviction correlated) without waiting for runtime to be awake. Runtime gets paged/escalated in parallel.
- We’ll codify in the rollout policy: rollback on (a) p99 regression over baseline for N minutes, OR (b) compaction time budget violations + elevated tail, OR (c) eviction rate > threshold for N minutes.

Allison + Rafael are helping make sure the dashboards/alerts are in place before we expand beyond internal canary.

Sean Gallagher
Site Reliability Engineering
Redwood Inference


---

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>

Date: Thu, Jan 22, 2026 at 12:41 PM PST

- Current state: frag ratio trend + p95/p99 deltas (if available)
- Last week shipped: PRs landed / metrics added
- This week: next gate + owners
- Risks / asks (1–2 bullets)

Mateo, please have Omar post it (you can forward if needed). Sean, if anything changes on rollback criteria/alert thresholds during canary, call it out in the same update.

Ava

title: Perf canary rollout gates — align on signals/thresholds + escalation
preview:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: 
Date: Sat, Jan 10, 2026 at 9:18 AM PST
Subject: Perf canary rollout gates — align on signals/thresholds + escalation

Mateo / Neha — I’d like to lock down what *exactly* the perf canary will gate for runtime rollouts (and what’s just advisory), so release eng + SRE can operationalize without ambiguity.

Context: Product expectation is we can safely expand runtime changes (kernels/scheduler/model config) with automated “pause/rollback” criteria when canary indicates a real regression on top models.

Proposal v1 (please react / edit):

1) Signals to gate on (per model, per region, per tier if applicable)
- p95 end-to-end latency (primary)
- tokens/sec (secondary; throughput)
- queueing time p95 (attribution only; not gating initially unless you strongly want it)

2) Gate conditions (for runtime releases to Hosted + Dedicated)
- “Gate / auto-pause” if:
  A) p95 e2e latency regression >= 15% vs baseline AND sustained for 20 min AND min_samples >= 250, OR
  B) tokens/sec drop >= 12% vs baseline AND sustained for 20 min AND min_samples >= 250

- “Warn only / no gate” if:
  - latency regression 8–15% sustained for 30 min (min_samples >= 250)
  - tokens/sec drop 6–12% sustained for 30 min (min_samples >= 250)

3) Scope of models for gating (Phase 1)
- Gate only on “Top 3” models in us-east (Hosted) for first 2 weeks.
- After tuning, expand to Top 10 + any “high-risk” models that changed kernel/scheduler behavior in the release.

4) Escalation/ownership expectations
- Gate trigger should:
  - auto-create a release note in the rollout thread
  - alert #eng-runtime (warn) and oncall (page only if sustained top-model regression)
  - require a human “continue rollout” ack from runtime TL or designated release captain

Questions I need resolved to write this into the rollout workflow:
- Do we want *paging* tied to the same gate conditions, or more conservative (e.g., 30+ min sustained)?
- Is 15% too aggressive for p95? I’m trying to keep false positives down but still catch real regressions early.
- Any hard “never gate on tokens/sec” viewpoint? (I’ve heard mixed opinions.)

If you’re aligned, I’ll update PM-642 with the exact criteria and coordinate with releases on what the UI/automation does when a gate trips.

Thanks,
Marissa
Product — Console & Rollouts
Redwood Inference


---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 10, 2026 at 11:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: Perf canary rollout gates — align on signals/thresholds + escalation

Marissa — directionally yes. A couple tweaks to reduce “halt everything” on noise:

- Prefer gating on *runtime-only p95* (scheduler+kernel time) if we have it reliably, with e2e p95 as a corroborating signal. We’ve seen cases where e2e moves because of upstream network/ingest.
- I’d avoid gating on tokens/sec alone in phase 1. It’s a good signal but more sensitive to batch mix changes. I’m OK with tokens/sec contributing to a “confidence score” or requiring both signals.

My suggested gate rule for phase 1:
- Gate if p95 runtime-only latency regression >= 15% sustained 20 min (min_samples 250)
  AND e2e p95 also >= 10% (same window)

Warn rule:
- Warn if runtime-only p95 8–15% sustained 30 min

For escalation:
- Gate should *pause expansion* (stop progressing to next cell/region), not auto-rollback by default. Rollback is a human call unless we’re in a “known safe rollback” scenario.

If we can include build_id + kernel_selection_id in the alert payload, triage time drops a lot.

Mateo
VP Engineering, Serving Runtime


---

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: 
Date: Sat, Jan 10, 2026 at 12:37 PM PST
Subject: Re: Perf canary rollout gates — align on signals/thresholds + escalation

Adding SRE perspective:

1) Paging
- Please keep paging tighter than “gate.” Gates can trigger during business hours with the right people online; pages should be reserved for sustained customer-impacting regressions.
- For phase 1, I recommend:
  - Warn to #eng-runtime for 8–15% runtime p95 regression sustained 30 min.
  - Page on-call only if >= 20% runtime p95 regression sustained 30 min on a top model OR >= 15% sustained 60 min.

2) Anti-flap
- Whatever thresholds we pick, we need a minimum re-notify interval and dedupe keyed by (model, region, tier, runtime_build_id). Otherwise we will page every time the baseline window shifts.

3) Rollback behavior
- Agree with Mateo: auto-pause expansion is fine; auto-rollback should be opt-in and only once we’re confident baseline + sampling are stable.

4) Runtime-only vs e2e
- Strong +1 to runtime-only for gating. E2e is too noisy (networking, queueing outside runtime, etc.). But we should keep e2e on the dashboard/alert payload so on-call can quickly sanity check.

If helpful, I can draft the “page vs warn” wording we’ll publish in the on-call policy (and make sure it matches the current rotations).

Neha Kapoor
Head of SRE
Redwood Inference


---

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: 
Date: Sun, Jan 11, 2026 at 9:06 AM PST
Subject: Re: Perf canary rollout gates — align on signals/thresholds + escalation

This is helpful.

Updated v2 proposal (trying to incorporate both of you):

A) Rollout gate (auto-pause expansion, no auto-rollback)
- Gate if runtime-only p95 latency regression >= 15% vs baseline AND sustained 20 min AND min_samples >= 250
- AND (e2e p95 regression >= 10% OR queueing p95 regression >= 25%)
  (the second clause is just to avoid pausing due to a runtime-metric oddity; open to better formulation)

B) Warning
- Warn to #eng-runtime if runtime-only p95 regression 8–15% sustained 30 min (min_samples >= 250)

C) Paging (phase 1)
- Page if runtime-only p95 regression >= 20% sustained 30 min on a top model
- OR runtime-only p95 regression >= 15% sustained 60 min on a top model

D) tokens/sec
- Not a gate in phase 1.
- Still include in alert payload and dashboard; warn-only if tokens/sec drop >= 12% sustained 30 min (no page).

E) Required context in every gate/warn/page payload
- model_id + model_version
- runtime_build_id
- kernel_selection_id (if present)
- hardware_sku
- region
- links: per-model dashboard panel + runbook

If you’re OK with this, I’ll align release eng and update PM-642 with exact criteria and the product behavior (“auto-pause expansion” + required ack to continue).

Two things I’m unsure about:
1) Do we need the “AND (e2e or queueing)” clause at all, or should we gate on runtime-only alone?
2) If we don’t gate on tokens/sec, are we worried we’ll miss a batch/scheduler regression that preserves p95 but kills throughput? (I’m fine leaving it warn-only for now.)

Marissa


---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Jan 12, 2026 at 2:21 PM PST
Subject: Re: Perf canary rollout gates — align on signals/thresholds + escalation

I’d simplify further:

- Gate on runtime-only p95 alone, *but* require min_samples and a sustained window (which you already have).
- Drop the “AND (e2e or queueing)” clause. It’ll create edge cases where runtime regresses but queueing/e2e don’t move enough to trip, and we’ll be arguing about why the gate didn’t fire.

Instead, use e2e + queueing as triage breadcrumbs in the payload.

On tokens/sec: I agree it’s possible to miss a throughput-only regression. We can address that by adding a separate throughput canary later, but for now: warn-only.

One more ask: make “Top models for gating” an explicit allowlist in control plane config with an audit log. I don’t want accidental gating on a long tail model due to a config mistake.

Mateo


---

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: 
Date: Mon, Jan 12, 2026 at 4:07 PM PST
Subject: Re: Perf canary rollout gates — align on signals/thresholds + escalation

+1 to Mateo’s simplification.

Decision (from my side):
- Gate = runtime-only p95 regression >= 15% sustained 20 min + min_samples >= 250, top-model allowlist only.
- Warn = 8–15% sustained 30 min.
- Page (phase 1) = >= 20% sustained 30 min OR >= 15% sustained 60 min (top-model allowlist only).
- No auto-rollback; auto-pause expansion + human ack to continue.

Please ensure:
- Dedupe: (model_id, model_version, region, hardware_sku, runtime_build_id)
- Re-notify: >= 2 hours unless severity changes
- “Disable switch” documented (in case the canary itself is causing overhead or is obviously misbehaving)

Marissa — once you update PM-642, send me the final text. I’ll mirror it into the on-call policy doc so we don’t have drift between product/release behavior and paging expectations.

Neha
text:

2) Gate conditions (for runtime releases to Hosted + Dedicated)
- “Gate / auto-pause” if:
  A) p95 e2e latency regression >= 15% vs baseline AND sustained for 20 min AND min_samples >= 250, OR
  B) tokens/sec drop >= 12% vs baseline AND sustained for 20 min AND min_samples >= 250

- “Warn only / no gate” if:
  - latency regression 8–15% sustained for 30 min (min_samples >= 250)
  - tokens/sec drop 6–12% sustained for 30 min (min_samples >= 250)

3) Scope of models for gating (Phase 1)
- Gate only on “Top 3” models in us-east (Hosted) for first 2 weeks.
- After tuning, expand to Top 10 + any “high-risk” models that changed kernel/scheduler behavior in the release.

4) Escalation/ownership expectations
- Gate trigger should:
  - auto-create a release note in the rollout thread
  - alert #eng-runtime (warn) and oncall (page only if sustained top-model regression)
  - require a human “continue rollout” ack from runtime TL or designated release captain

Questions I need resolved to write this into the rollout workflow:
- Do we want *paging* tied to the same gate conditions, or more conservative (e.g., 30+ min sustained)?
- Is 15% too aggressive for p95? I’m trying to keep false positives down but still catch real regressions early.
- Any hard “never gate on tokens/sec” viewpoint? (I’ve heard mixed opinions.)

If you’re aligned, I’ll update PM-642 with the exact criteria and coordinate with releases on what the UI/automation does when a gate trips.

Thanks,
Marissa
Product — Console & Rollouts
Redwood Inference


---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 10, 2026 at 11:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: Perf canary rollout gates — align on signals/thresholds + escalation

Marissa — directionally yes. A couple tweaks to reduce “halt everything” on noise:

- Prefer gating on *runtime-only p95* (scheduler+kernel time) if we have it reliably, with e2e p95 as a corroborating signal. We’ve seen cases where e2e moves because of upstream network/ingest.
- I’d avoid gating on tokens/sec alone in phase 1. It’s a good signal but more sensitive to batch mix changes. I’m OK with tokens/sec contributing to a “confidence score” or requiring both signals.

My suggested gate rule for phase 1:
- Gate if p95 runtime-only latency regression >= 15% sustained 20 min (min_samples 250)
  AND e2e p95 also >= 10% (same window)

Warn rule:
- Warn if runtime-only p95 8–15% sustained 30 min

For escalation:
- Gate should *pause expansion* (stop progressing to next cell/region), not auto-rollback by default. Rollback is a human call unless we’re in a “known safe rollback” scenario.

If we can include build_id + kernel_selection_id in the alert payload, triage time drops a lot.

Mateo
VP Engineering, Serving Runtime


---

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: 
Date: Sat, Jan 10, 2026 at 12:37 PM PST
Subject: Re: Perf canary rollout gates — align on signals/thresholds + escalation

Adding SRE perspective:

1) Paging
- Please keep paging tighter than “gate.” Gates can trigger during business hours with the right people online; pages should be reserved for sustained customer-impacting regressions.
- For phase 1, I recommend:
  - Warn to #eng-runtime for 8–15% runtime p95 regression sustained 30 min.
  - Page on-call only if >= 20% runtime p95 regression sustained 30 min on a top model OR >= 15% sustained 60 min.

2) Anti-flap
- Whatever thresholds we pick, we need a minimum re-notify interval and dedupe keyed by (model, region, tier, runtime_build_id). Otherwise we will page every time the baseline window shifts.

3) Rollback behavior
- Agree with Mateo: auto-pause expansion is fine; auto-rollback should be opt-in and only once we’re confident baseline + sampling are stable.

4) Runtime-only vs e2e
- Strong +1 to runtime-only for gating. E2e is too noisy (networking, queueing outside runtime, etc.). But we should keep e2e on the dashboard/alert payload so on-call can quickly sanity check.

If helpful, I can draft the “page vs warn” wording we’ll publish in the on-call policy (and make sure it matches the current rotations).

Neha Kapoor
Head of SRE
Redwood Inference


---

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: 
Date: Sun, Jan 11, 2026 at 9:06 AM PST
Subject: Re: Perf canary rollout gates — align on signals/thresholds + escalation

This is helpful.

Updated v2 proposal (trying to incorporate both of you):

A) Rollout gate (auto-pause expansion, no auto-rollback)
- Gate if runtime-only p95 latency regression >= 15% vs baseline AND sustained 20 min AND min_samples >= 250
- AND (e2e p95 regression >= 10% OR queueing p95 regression >= 25%)
  (the second clause is just to avoid pausing due to a runtime-metric oddity; open to better formulation)

B) Warning
- Warn to #eng-runtime if runtime-only p95 regression 8–15% sustained 30 min (min_samples >= 250)

C) Paging (phase 1)
- Page if runtime-only p95 regression >= 20% sustained 30 min on a top model
- OR runtime-only p95 regression >= 15% sustained 60 min on a top model

D) tokens/sec
- Not a gate in phase 1.
- Still include in alert payload and dashboard; warn-only if tokens/sec drop >= 12% sustained 30 min (no page).

E) Required context in every gate/warn/page payload
- model_id + model_version
- runtime_build_id
- kernel_selection_id (if present)
- hardware_sku
- region
- links: per-model dashboard panel + runbook

If you’re OK with this, I’ll align release eng and update PM-642 with exact criteria and the product behavior (“auto-pause expansion” + required ack to continue).

Two things I’m unsure about:
1) Do we need the “AND (e2e or queueing)” clause at all, or should we gate on runtime-only alone?
2) If we don’t gate on tokens/sec, are we worried we’ll miss a batch/scheduler regression that preserves p95 but kills throughput? (I’m fine leaving it warn-only for now.)

Marissa


---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Jan 12, 2026 at 2:21 PM PST
Subject: Re: Perf canary rollout gates — align on signals/thresholds + escalation

I’d simplify further:

- Gate on runtime-only p95 alone, *but* require min_samples and a sustained window (which you already have).
- Drop the “AND (e2e or queueing)” clause. It’ll create edge cases where runtime regresses but queueing/e2e don’t move enough to trip, and we’ll be arguing about why the gate didn’t fire.

Instead, use e2e + queueing as triage breadcrumbs in the payload.

On tokens/sec: I agree it’s possible to miss a throughput-only regression. We can address that by adding a separate throughput canary later, but for now: warn-only.

One more ask: make “Top models for gating” an explicit allowlist in control plane config with an audit log. I don’t want accidental gating on a long tail model due to a config mistake.

Mateo


---

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: 
Date: Mon, Jan 12, 2026 at 4:07 PM PST
Subject: Re: Perf canary rollout gates — align on signals/thresholds + escalation

+1 to Mateo’s simplification.

Decision (from my side):
- Gate = runtime-only p95 regression >= 15% sustained 20 min + min_samples >= 250, top-model allowlist only.
- Warn = 8–15% sustained 30 min.
- Page (phase 1) = >= 20% sustained 30 min OR >= 15% sustained 60 min (top-model allowlist only).
- No auto-rollback; auto-pause expansion + human ack to continue.

Please ensure:
- Dedupe: (model_id, model_version, region, hardware_sku, runtime_build_id)
- Re-notify: >= 2 hours unless severity changes
- “Disable switch” documented (in case the canary itself is causing overhead or is obviously misbehaving)

Marissa — once you update PM-642, send me the final text. I’ll mirror it into the on-call policy doc so we don’t have drift between product/release behavior and paging expectations.

Neha
