title: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)
preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Sean — requesting SRE + exec approval to enable the mixed-workload scheduler prototype behind flag in a *limited* production canary (Hosted API, us-east-1).

Why now
- We have repeat internal + customer escalations where mixed short/long prompts inflate p95/TTFT (HOL blocking during long prefill).
- The runtime team has a prototype (two-queue short/long + aging + token budget guardrails) that shows consistent p95 improvement on mixed distributions without material throughput loss in our benchmark suite.

What we’re proposing (scope + blast radius)
- Region/pool: Hosted API us-east-1, pool: hosted-canary (not general).
- Traffic: 1% -> 5% (same pool) over ~3 hours if metrics stay within guardrails.
- Duration: 24 hours at 5% (or revert sooner if drift).
- Feature gating:
  - Flag: scheduler.mixed_workload.enabled=true (pool-scoped)
  - Policy knobs pinned (no auto-tuning) for the canary window.

Success criteria (must hit)
- Mixed workload p95 latency (end-to-end) improves >= 15% vs baseline (comparable traffic band), measured on canary + perf-canary.
- No p95 regression > 5% on steady-state short-only workload.
- No error-rate increase beyond normal variance; no sustained queue starvation counters.

Guardrails / rollback triggers (aligned to SRE doc)
- Rollback immediately if any of:
  - p95 (Hosted API chat) +10% for > 10 minutes vs same-hour baseline
  - p99 +15% for > 10 minutes
  - starvation_long_counter increments continuously for > 2 minutes (indicates long queue not making progress)
  - elevated OOM/compute errors attributable to batch construction changes
- Rollback options:
  1) Flip flag off (fast, no deploy) — expected to drain within minutes.
  2) Revert knobs to baseline (if only parameter-induced).
  3) Runtime rollback to 1.17.3 in hosted-canary (Connor has the release playbook ready).

Known risks (and mitigations)
- Risk: long-queue starvation under sustained short traffic
  - Mitigation: aging + min dispatch share + regression test merged (PR #24905).
- Risk: kernel selection instability when alternating short/long sequences
  - Mitigation: prefill/decode separation hooks + capped token budget per iteration; monitored via kernel_thrash metric + Nsight spot checks in staging.
- Risk: throughput dip due to policy overhead
  - Mitigation: overhead measured <1% CPU in staging; monitor tokens/sec and batch utilization.

Links / artifacts
- Results summary (attached): mixed-workload-results-summary-runtime-1.18.pdf
- Canary plan draft (attached): canary-plan-mixed-scheduler-us-east-v3.docx
- Confluence design + ADR:
  - https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/SERVE/Mixed-workload+scheduler+prototype+-+Design
  - https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ARCH/ADR-Short-Long-Prompt-Scheduling-Policy
- Perf-canary dashboard:
  - https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/perf-mixed/mixed-workload-perf-canary?var-runtime=1.18.0-rc3

Request
- Sean: please reply with SRE sign-off (or required edits) so Connor can schedule the window today.
- If approved, we’ll do the ramp during US business hours with Rafael as ops point and Aisha watching SLO + rollback triggers.

Thanks,
Mateo
Executive Sponsor, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

Attachment: mixed-workload-results-summary-runtime-1.18.pdf (application/pdf)
Attachment: canary-plan-mixed-scheduler-us-east-v3.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document)

---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 10:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

A couple asks before I sign off:

1) Can you confirm the change is *pool-scoped only* (no spillover to other hosted pools via shared config)?
2) What’s our exact dashboard + alert set for queue starvation + per-class waiting time? I don’t want this to be “we’ll eyeball it.”
3) In the results summary: what’s the worst-case scenario you saw (any regressions on long-only or long-spike mixes)?

If we can nail those three, I’m comfortable with a 1% -> 5% ramp with a tight rollback.

-Sean

---

From: Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 10:36 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Responding inline:

> 1) confirm pool-scoped only
Yes. The flag + knobs are resolved at pool config level (runtime config service), and we’re only enabling for pool=hosted-canary in us-east-1. No global default change. (Config diff is attached in the canary plan doc; Rafael can also paste the exact key path if helpful.)

> 2) dashboard + alert set
Allison has the dashboard spec + alerts wired. We’ll be monitoring:
- scheduler_queue_depth{class=short|long}
- scheduler_time_in_queue_ms_p95{class=short|long}
- scheduler_starvation_events_total{class=long}

- plus end-to-end p95/p99 and TTFT.

> 3) worst-case scenario seen
In “long-spike” synthetic (10 minutes of 70% long-context), we saw:
- Mixed p95 improved still (less HOL for short),

We did see one starvation corner case early (continuous short flood); that’s what PR #24905 addresses, and it’s in the 1.18.0-rc3 build proposed.

text:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Sean — requesting SRE + exec approval to enable the mixed-workload scheduler prototype behind flag in a *limited* production canary (Hosted API, us-east-1).

Why now
- We have repeat internal + customer escalations where mixed short/long prompts inflate p95/TTFT (HOL blocking during long prefill).
- The runtime team has a prototype (two-queue short/long + aging + token budget guardrails) that shows consistent p95 improvement on mixed distributions without material throughput loss in our benchmark suite.

What we’re proposing (scope + blast radius)
- Region/pool: Hosted API us-east-1, pool: hosted-canary (not general).
- Traffic: 1% -> 5% (same pool) over ~3 hours if metrics stay within guardrails.
- Duration: 24 hours at 5% (or revert sooner if drift).
- Feature gating:
  - Flag: scheduler.mixed_workload.enabled=true (pool-scoped)
  - Policy knobs pinned (no auto-tuning) for the canary window.

Success criteria (must hit)
- Mixed workload p95 latency (end-to-end) improves >= 15% vs baseline (comparable traffic band), measured on canary + perf-canary.
- No p95 regression > 5% on steady-state short-only workload.
- No error-rate increase beyond normal variance; no sustained queue starvation counters.

Guardrails / rollback triggers (aligned to SRE doc)
- Rollback immediately if any of:
  - p95 (Hosted API chat) +10% for > 10 minutes vs same-hour baseline
  - p99 +15% for > 10 minutes
  - starvation_long_counter increments continuously for > 2 minutes (indicates long queue not making progress)
  - elevated OOM/compute errors attributable to batch construction changes
- Rollback options:
  1) Flip flag off (fast, no deploy) — expected to drain within minutes.
  2) Revert knobs to baseline (if only parameter-induced).
  3) Runtime rollback to 1.17.3 in hosted-canary (Connor has the release playbook ready).

Known risks (and mitigations)
- Risk: long-queue starvation under sustained short traffic
  - Mitigation: aging + min dispatch share + regression test merged (PR #24905).
- Risk: kernel selection instability when alternating short/long sequences
  - Mitigation: prefill/decode separation hooks + capped token budget per iteration; monitored via kernel_thrash metric + Nsight spot checks in staging.
- Risk: throughput dip due to policy overhead
  - Mitigation: overhead measured <1% CPU in staging; monitor tokens/sec and batch utilization.

Links / artifacts
- Results summary (attached): mixed-workload-results-summary-runtime-1.18.pdf
- Canary plan draft (attached): canary-plan-mixed-scheduler-us-east-v3.docx
- Confluence design + ADR:
  - https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/SERVE/Mixed-workload+scheduler+prototype+-+Design
  - https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ARCH/ADR-Short-Long-Prompt-Scheduling-Policy
- Perf-canary dashboard:
  - https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/perf-mixed/mixed-workload-perf-canary?var-runtime=1.18.0-rc3

Request
- Sean: please reply with SRE sign-off (or required edits) so Connor can schedule the window today.
- If approved, we’ll do the ramp during US business hours with Rafael as ops point and Aisha watching SLO + rollback triggers.

Thanks,
Mateo
Executive Sponsor, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

Attachment: mixed-workload-results-summary-runtime-1.18.pdf (application/pdf)
Attachment: canary-plan-mixed-scheduler-us-east-v3.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document)

---

3) In the results summary: what’s the worst-case scenario you saw (any regressions on long-only or long-spike mixes)?

If we can nail those three, I’m comfortable with a 1% -> 5% ramp with a tight rollback.

-Sean

title: MAP passoff playbook: acceptance tests & failure-mode plan
preview:
From: Sarah Goldstein <sarah.goldstein@healthlytics.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 25 Nov 2025 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Evaluating Redwood — MAP & acceptance criteria

Hi Mateo,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the team was impressed with the latency profiles and the model catalog. We want to map out a short evaluation so the product and security teams can validate fit before procurement. High-level ask: a 4–6 week evaluation with clear exit gates and acceptance tests that our engineering and security teams can run.

A few constraints from our side:
- We need evidence of p95 inference latency under 500ms for our 512-token workflow.
- A sample of 5k production-like prompts for concordance testing (we can share).
- Audit logs and retention details for SOC/compliance review.

Could you send a MAP draft with milestones, owner responsibilities, and explicit pass/fail criteria? Also include failure-mode definitions (what constitutes a stop or rollback). Procurement will need a preliminary quote to scope the pilot capacity.

Thanks,
Sarah
Sarah Goldstein
Head of AI Programs, HealthLytics

---
Attachment: none


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sarah Goldstein <sarah.goldstein@healthlytics.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 25 Nov 2025 11:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Evaluating Redwood — MAP & acceptance criteria

Hi Sarah — appreciated the time. Attaching a MAP draft that breaks the evaluation into four discrete gates and lists explicit acceptance tests and failure modes. High-level summary below; full details in the attached spreadsheet (HealthLytics_MAP_draft.xlsx).

Proposed MAP highlights:
- Week 0 (Kickoff): access, test data ingest (owner: HealthLytics eng), baseline latency smoke test (owner: Redwood SE).
- Gate 1 (Smoke, Day 3–5): correctness sanity, end-to-end integration, and basic latency smoke (p95 < 700ms). Pass -> proceed.
- Gate 2 (Performance & Stability, Week 2–3): run 10k inference calls with production-like distribution; p95 < 500ms, <2% error rate. Pass -> proceed.
- Gate 3 (Quality & Concordance, Week 3–4): run concordance suite (5k prompts). Acceptance: top-line concordance >= 92% vs baseline model or agreed oracle; critical failure modes <1%.
- Gate 4 (Security & Ops signoff, Week 4–6): SOC evidence, audit log sample, KMS integration checks.

Exit / rollback triggers (examples):
- If Gate 2 p95 > 650ms for >24h despite tuning -> automatic rollback to previous model variant and joint post-mortem.
- If concordance drops below 88% on focused category tests -> stop and troubleshoot; require remediation and re-run.

Deliverables we will provide:
- A test harness and runner for your 5k concordance set.
- Weekly readout templates + per-route latency/cost dashboards.

Next steps: please review the attached MAP and confirm owners for the ingest, security checks, and who will sign the Gate 4 Ops acceptance. We can schedule kickoff the week of Dec 1 if that works.

Thanks,
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Attachments: HealthLytics_MAP_draft.xlsx


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sarah Goldstein <sarah.goldstein@healthlytics.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 26 Nov 2025 08:40:00 -0800
Subject: Re: MAP passoff playbook: technical acceptance tests (details)

Hi all — adding the technical acceptance checklist and a few test artifacts so your eng team knows exactly what we will run. I'm attaching a compact markdown with scripted tests (acceptance_tests.md). Quick notes:

Technical acceptance checklist (examples):
- Model variant: v2.1-quant (primary), fallback v2.1-fp16. Test both for functional parity.
- Sequence lengths: run tests at 64, 256, and 512 token distributions.
- Batching profile: simulate p50 and p95 concurrency; we will run with batch sizes up to 8 to reflect production traffic.
- KV cache behavior: long-session tests with repeated conversations; ensure cache hit rate >85% after warmup.
- Adversarial prompts: 200 prompt set to verify safety/suppression rules.

Artifacts we will deliver: test harness (dockerized), CSV results, and a short runbook for reproducing any failing cases. If you want, we can push the harness to a temporary S3 bucket and your engineers can kick it off.

On the rollback criterion: we'll document exact thresholds in the Gate 2 sheet (e.g., sustained p95 > 650ms for 3 consecutive runs triggers stop).

-Karthik
Principal SE, Redwood Inference
Attachments: acceptance_tests.md

We will grant access to a read-only audit-log view scoped to the evaluation timeframe (for your security team). Please confirm who from HealthLytics will validate Gate 3 (quality) and Gate 4 (security). Also linking a preliminary quote so procurement has context (redwood_quote_v1.pdf).

If the team accepts this MAP, I recommend a short SOW addendum that lists the specific acceptance artifacts and sign-off roles — that speeds final procurement. Happy to draft if you want.

Cheers,
Monica Patel
Senior Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference

- Turnaround: we’ll aim to triage any failing test within 24 business hours.

Acceptance examples (for your checklist):
- Concordance: >= 92% on the shared 5k sample (primary metric).
- Latency: p95 <= 500ms for the 512-token profile during a continuous 4-hour window.
- Error budget: <2% server-side errors across the run.
- Safety: no more than 0.1% safety overrides on the adversarial set.

We will grant access to a read-only audit-log view scoped to the evaluation timeframe (for your security team). Please confirm who from HealthLytics will validate Gate 3 (quality) and Gate 4 (security). Also linking a preliminary quote so procurement has context (redwood_quote_v1.pdf).

If the team accepts this MAP, I recommend a short SOW addendum that lists the specific acceptance artifacts and sign-off roles — that speeds final procurement. Happy to draft if you want.

Cheers,
Monica Patel
Senior Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference

Team — after reviewing the RFP and CompVendor workbook, here are mech proposals we can take into Monday's call. Please keep this thread internal.

TL;DR: We can get within ~8-10% of CompVendor's landed economics with a blended package and focused migration support. Proposed optional constructs:

Option A — Consumption-heavy (short-term win)\- 12-month committed consumption with a 15% unit discount and 2-month consumption credit applied in first 90 days.\- Migration stipend: $60k fixed credit to cover integration effort; apply as one-time invoice credit.\- Operational support: 2-week dedicated migration window with Connor as lead (reduced go-live risk).

Option B — Seat + Consumption (better retention economics)\- 12-month term with a 4-seat minimum for dedicated runtime + consumption at 18% discounted rate.\- Onboarding credit: $80k covering professional services (handshake + runbook).

Commercial guardrails: do not drop below 18% effective unit discount on any BAU consumption unless we secure a higher committed volume or multi-year deal. For procurement asks for parity pricing we can offer a one-time migration credit in lieu of permanent list price movement.

Lauren — can you quantify the revenue impact of each option (ARR slope and net new dollars) and set recommended approval thresholds? Kimberly — please check legal on whether a one-time stipend should be a credit against invoices or a separate service SOW. Connor — prepare a short migration timeline (days of effort + key checkpoints).

Mateo

--
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Solutions, Redwood Inference

- Term: 12 months committed (starts on go-live).
- Pricing: Consumption billing with a blended 18% discount off list for all token generation/embedding usage.
- Onboarding credit: $75,000 one-time invoice credit applied within 30 days of go-live.

- Optional seat bundle: If you opt for dedicated seat pool (min 4 seats), an additional 3% discount on consumption applies (bringing total to ~21% off list).

Estimated landed economics vs CompVendor: assuming your stated volume (~9M tokens/month) the blended Option above lands within ~7% of CompVendor's quoted landed cost when migration credits are amortized over 12 months.

1) If accepted in principle, we will produce a formal term sheet + commercial appendix for procurement.
2) Schedule kickoff: propose Wed 11/19 10:00 PT for legal + technical scoping.

title: Packaging realignment & feature-pool mapping — HealthBridge upgrade/downgrade options
preview:
From: Ellen Davis <ellen@healthbridge.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement <procure@healthbridge.ai>
Date: 2025-09-16T09:14:00-07:00
Subject: Packaging review: options for our changing usage pattern

Hi Mateo,

Thanks again for the demo last week. As HealthBridge ramps up ingestion for a new doc pipeline, our usage profile has shifted — fewer long-running chats, a lot more short burst embeddings and reranking. We need help understanding whether we should move plans (upgrade to a throughput-focused tier), rebundle features, or keep our current plan and apply credits.

Can you send a short outline of:
- Recommended plan (what changes if we move to a throughput-first plan)
- Downgrade path if we later scale back (how credits/proration would work)
- Which features are pooled vs seat-based under each option

Attaching our current usage snapshot.

Attachment: HealthBridge_Current_Usage.xlsx (xlsx)

Would like to align before our renewal next month.

Best,
Ellen
Head of Product, HealthBridge
ellen@healthbridge.ai


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Ellen Davis <ellen@healthbridge.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-09-16T13:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Packaging review: options for our changing usage pattern

Hi Ellen — thanks for the clear note and the usage file. Looping in Ben (pricing) and Kimberly (product) so we can give an answer you can share with procurement.

High-level thoughts (quick, so we can book time to walk through numbers):
1) Throughput-first plan (what you'd gain)
   - Predictable token throughput for high-concurrency embedding/rerank workloads
   - Lower per-token price when steady throughput is reserved; includes higher burst buffer for short spikes
   - Feature mapping: embeddings + reranking calls billed from the throughput pool; standard chat/LLM calls remain per-token unless explicitly pooled

2) Downgrade path (safety net)
   - 30-day notice to reduce reserved throughput with pro-rated credits for the remainder of the month
   - If you move down during term, we convert unused reserved tokens into a one-time credit at a blended rate (not full list), plus a 90-day grace for rollback to previous tier

3) Bundling guidance
   - Recommend moving embeddings/rerank into a pooled throughput bucket and keeping advanced moderation and trace-level observability as add-ons.
   - Option: keep a small seat allocation for conversational power users to preserve low latency for interactive flows.

Next steps I suggest: 1) Ben prepares two pricing scenarios (conservative and aggressive throughput reserve), 2) Kimberly sketches feature-bucket mapping and any limitations, 3) 30-minute call later this week to review.

Attached a short summary of the proposal and a first-pass feature pool mapping.

Attachment: redwood_proposal_summary_20250916.pdf (pdf)

When's good for you on Thursday/Friday?

Cheers,
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0126


From: Ellen Davis <ellen@healthbridge.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement <procure@healthbridge.ai>
Date: 2025-09-17T08:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Packaging review: options for our changing usage pattern

Thanks Mateo — that helps. Procurement is copied; two quick clarifications before the call: 

- If we over-reserve and want to step down mid-term, how fast are credits applied and how do they show on invoices? (Procurement wants the accounting implication.)
- On feature pooling: are there any hard limits where embeddings in a pooled bucket can interfere with chat latency SLAs?

We're available Friday 10:00–11:00 PT.

—Ellen

On Tue, Sep 16, 2025 at 1:02 PM Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Ellen — thanks for the clear note and the usage file. Looping in Ben (pricing) and Kimberly (product) so we can give an answer you can share with procurement.
> 
> High-level thoughts (quick, so we can book time to walk through numbers):
> 1) Throughput-first plan (what you'd gain)
>    - Predictable token throughput for high-concurrency embedding/rerank workloads
>    - Lower per-token price when steady throughput is reserved; includes higher burst buffer for short spikes
>    - Feature mapping: embeddings + reranking calls billed from the throughput pool; standard chat/LLM calls remain per-token unless explicitly pooled
> 
> 2) Downgrade path (safety net)
>    - 30-day notice to reduce reserved throughput with pro-rated credits for the remainder of the month
>    - If you move down during term, we convert unused reserved tokens into a one-time credit at a blended rate (not full list), plus a 90-day grace for rollback to previous tier
> 
> 3) Bundling guidance
>    - Recommend moving embeddings/rerank into a pooled throughput bucket and keeping advanced moderation and trace-level observability as add-ons.
>    - Option: keep a small seat allocation for conversational power users to preserve low latency for interactive flows.
> 
> Next steps I suggest: 1) Ben prepares two pricing scenarios (conservative and aggressive throughput reserve), 2) Kimberly sketches feature-bucket mapping and any limitations, 3) 30-minute call later this week to review.



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ellen Davis <ellen@healthbridge.ai>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-09-18T16:24:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Packaging review: options for our changing usage pattern

Ellen — adding the numbers you asked for so procurement has something to review before Friday. Short answers up front: credits apply the billing cycle after the downgrade is processed; they appear as a line-item credit and reduce the following invoice. There is no off-book manual crediting unless negotiated in the contract.

Key details (summary):
- Invoice treatment: pro-rated credit posted in the next billing cycle; example included in the CSV.
- Speed: typical processing time is 5–7 business days after request, faster if we have written approval from a PO/authorized signer.
- Latency interaction: embedding workloads in the pooled throughput bucket are scheduled with priority shaping — they do not preempt reserved low-latency conversational nodes. In practice that means you keep sub-200ms p95 for chat, with embeddings run on separate instance pools. Edge cases: sustained >3x burst for 24+ hours will trigger autoscaling recommendations and may require temporary burst credits.

Attached: pricing-scenarios.csv — includes two reserve levels and the blended credit math for downgrade. Also added the HubSpot deal link and a DocuSign placeholder for an optional amendment.

Attachment: pricing-scenarios.csv (csv)

HubSpot: https://hubspot.example/deal/HB-4821
DocuSign: https://docu.sign/example-envelope/abcd1234

If that looks good we'll finalize and I'll circulate a short amendment (1 page) that covers the downgrade mechanics and the feature-pool mapping.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0199

title: Billing consolidation & VAT handling for Beryl Systems acquisition
preview:
From: Sophie Hart <sophie.hart@berylsystems.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-09-03T13:12:00Z
Subject: New billing remit & VAT for Beryl Systems UK

Hi Mateo / Marissa,

As a quick follow-up from our accuracy call this morning: the acquisition of AmberTech closed last week and as part of the integration we're centralizing Redwood invoices under the new legal entity, Beryl Systems UK Ltd. Please update your billing records to reflect the new remit address and VAT treatment.

Key items: 
- New legal name: Beryl Systems UK Ltd
- Billing address: 8 Kingsford Street, London, EC1V 7QT, United Kingdom
- VAT registration number: GB987654321
- Effective date for invoicing cutover: 2025-10-01 (we want invoices issued to the new entity from that date onward)

I've attached the company's bank remittance details to help with any wire/ACH references (Beryl_ACH_Wire_Instructions.pdf). We may need a formal billing entity change form from Redwood to satisfy our AP team — happy to sign anything you require.

Two questions for Redwood: 
1) Will you need the Certificate of Incorporation or purchase agreement to make the name change?
2) How will VAT be handled on invoices issued from 10/01 onward — will you include VAT on Redwood invoices to a UK-registered buyer or mark them as zero-rated for B2B?

Thanks — Sophie

Sophie Hart
Head of Finance, Beryl Systems
Sophie.Hart@berylsystems.com | +44 20 7123 4567

Attachment: Beryl_ACH_Wire_Instructions.pdf (stub)


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Sophie Hart <sophie.hart@berylsystems.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-09-03T16:05:00Z
Subject: Re: New billing remit & VAT for Beryl Systems UK

Hi Sophie — thanks for the heads-up and congrats on the close.

We're happy to support the consolidation. To move this through our billing and legal systems I'll need the following from your side:
- A signed copy of the acquisition closing statement or Certificate of Incorporation for Beryl Systems UK Ltd (for our audit file).
- Confirmation whether the tax residency for Redwood invoices will be B2B (VAT reverse charge) or if you require Redwood to apply UK VAT on our service fees.
- Point of contact in AP for remittance reconciliation (name + payables@ email) if different from Liam.

I've attached our standard billing entity change form (billing_entity_change_form.pdf) — if you can sign and return that, Laura will schedule the change for the requested effective date. For transparency, here is our proposed workflow:
1) Receive signed change form + Certificate_of_Incorporation (or equivalent).
2) Finance team updates legal name, VAT fields, and remittance instructions in our ERP.
3) Pilot: generate a sample invoice for 2025-10-01 and share for AP signoff (no payment movement required).
4) Switch production invoices on 2025-10-01; older invoices remain tied to prior entity and must be paid per their original remittance instructions.

If you prefer, I can also open a HubSpot ticket and queue a DocuSign envelope for the form (faster for signatures).

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com | +1 415 555 0198

Attachment: billing_entity_change_form.pdf (please sign and return)

On Wed, Sep 3, 2025 at 13:12 Sophie Hart <sophie.hart@berylsystems.com> wrote:
> (original message)


From: Sophie Hart <sophie.hart@berylsystems.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@berylsystems.com>
Date: 2025-09-05T09:34:00Z
Subject: Re: New billing remit & VAT for Beryl Systems UK

Hi Mateo / team,

Thanks — attaching the Certificate_of_Incorporation_Beryl_UK.pdf and the signed change form (signed by our CFO). AP contact is Liam O'Neill (liam.oneill@berylsystems.com).

To clarify VAT treatment: Beryl Systems UK Ltd is a UK VAT registrant and we expect B2B reverse charge to apply for service fees. Our preference is that Redwood not charge UK VAT on invoices to Beryl Systems UK Ltd but include the VAT registration number and note "VAT reverse charge applies" on the invoice. If Redwood requires charging VAT at source we can discuss, but that would affect our AP flow.

One more ask: do you prorate or adjust invoices that span the cutover date? Our ACQ team expects the subscription usage invoice for September (period: 09/01–09/30) — will that be billed to the legacy entity or to the new UK entity? We can accept a pro forma sample for reconciliation.

Thanks — Sophie

Attachment: Certificate_of_Incorporation_Beryl_UK.pdf (stub)


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Sophie Hart <sophie.hart@berylsystems.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-09-09T16:45:00Z
Subject: Re: New billing remit & VAT for Beryl Systems UK — next steps

Hi Sophie / Mateo,

Appreciate the attachments — I've reviewed the certificate and the signed form. We'll proceed with the planned cutover on 2025-10-01. Summary of actions from Redwood Billing: 

- Effective invoicing entity: invoices dated on or after 2025-10-01 will be issued to "Beryl Systems UK Ltd" with the VAT number GB987654321 and the billing address provided.
- VAT handling: we'll mark invoices as B2B with a note "VAT reverse charge applies" and include your VAT number. Our system will not add UK VAT for B2B reverse-charge cases. If the nature of services triggers a different tax treatment we'll flag it, but for typical inference API consumption the reverse charge is appropriate.
- September invoice (period 09/01–09/30): this will be issued to the legacy legal entity (per our standard pre-cutover rule) and should be paid to the remittance instructions on that invoice. That keeps month-bound billing consistent. If you need us to re-bill September to the UK entity as a one-time exception, please confirm and we can evaluate accounting implications; this will require sign-off from our revenue recognition team.
- Wire/ACH references: we'll update our ERP with the remittance details you provided so applied payments to future invoices will reconcile to the new entity.

Next items for you: 
1) Please return the signed billing_entity_change_form.pdf (we have a signed copy attached but need the original signed scan on letterhead for our audit) — academic copies accepted via DocuSign are OK.
2) Confirm whether you want September rebilled to the new UK entity (yes/no).
3) Provide any AP contact change for remittance notification other than Liam.

I've attached a sample invoice PDF we will generate before the cutover (20251001-invoice-sample.pdf) — review and confirm there are no missing legal references. Once you confirm, we will queue the change and share the DocuSign envelope for official signature retention.

Best regards,
Laura Bennett
Manager, Billing Operations — Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.com | +1 628 555 0122

Attachment: 20251001-invoice-sample.pdf (sample for review)

title: FastPath MAP: accelerate reservation & GPU pool sizing checklist
preview:
From: Samantha Lee <samantha@healthgrid.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: HealthGrid Procurement <procurement@healthgrid.com>
Date: 2025-08-03T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: FastPath: need MAP & procurement gating for Q4 reserve

Hi Mateo / Ben —

Thanks for the quick intro last week. We have an exec ask to shorten the procurement and staging timeline for our Q4 launch. Procurement asked for:
- a MAP with milestone owners and gating criteria
- lead times for Dedicated vs Private deployment (VPC / on‑prem)
- recommended GPU pool sizing for our forecasted 6M token throughput

Can Redwood put together a FastPath MAP (two-week cadence) so procurement can present to finance on 8/12? I attached the initial RFP so you can see acceptance criteria.

Attachments: HealthGrid_RFP_Q3.pdf

Best,
Samantha
Head of Platform Integrations
HealthGrid


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Samantha Lee <samantha@healthgrid.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-08-03T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: FastPath: need MAP & procurement gating for Q4 reserve

Samantha —

Thanks, attaching a FastPath MAP skeleton (MAP_FastPath_v1.xlsx) with owners and a proposed gating checklist. High-level calls here:

- Lead times (typical): Dedicated (hosted/reserved GPU pool): 8–12 weeks to guaranteed capacity; Private VPC: 10–14 weeks; On‑prem: 12–16 weeks (procurement + rack + network + acceptance).
- Recommended starter GPU pool (production): 8x A100-80GB or 12x A30-40GB depending on model quantization. For heavy decoding we recommend more A100s; for throughput-focused embeddings/short prompts A30 tranches are cost-effective.
- Staged tranches: 25% baseline (staging), 50% ramp, 25% production buffer — acceptance gates tied to latency SLO and tail QPS.

Next steps I propose: quick call tomorrow to lock tranche dates, then we finalize the MAP and draft a short procurement checklist your team can hand to finance.

Link to MAP: https://drive.redwood.com/teams/sales/HealthGrid/MAP_FastPath_v1.xlsx
Attachments: MAP_FastPath_v1.xlsx

—Mateo
Sr. Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.ai>, Samantha Lee <samantha@healthgrid.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-08-04T08:27:00-07:00
Subject: Re: FastPath: need MAP & procurement gating for Q4 reserve

On Mon, Aug 3, 2025 at 11:03 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Samantha —
>
> Thanks, attaching a FastPath MAP skeleton...

Quick additions from capacity:
- If HealthGrid can commit to a 12‑month reservation we can prioritize your tranche for earlier delivery windows (can shorten lead time by ~2–3 weeks).
- Suggested initial commit for safe runway: equivalent of 10 TFLOPS sustained (est. 8x A100 or 16x A30). We'll size tranches based on projected peak QPS in 30‑/60‑/90‑day buckets (Ben will provide a lightweight sizing worksheet).
- Acceptance gating: SOC2 evidence and packet capture test for private deployments (Neha will own SOC2 artifact delivery).

I attached a rough procurement timeline that mirrors supply constraints we’re seeing.

Attachments: procurement_timeline_v2.xlsx

—Ben
Head of Customer Capacity, Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Samantha Lee <samantha@healthgrid.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-08-05T14:10:00-07:00
Subject: Re: FastPath: need MAP & procurement gating for Q4 reserve

Thanks team — pulling this into a two‑week sprint plan. Proposed milestones (high level):

Week 1 (8/4–8/8): final MAP, tranche dates, procurement checklist (owner: HealthGrid procurement + Ben)
Week 2 (8/11–8/15): finalize PO, SOC2 bundle, staging provisioning window
Gates for finance approval:
- Signed PO or 3‑month commitment letter (min)
- Finalized acceptance tests with SLOs (latency 95th < 250ms for LLM endpoint)
- Clarified data residency and KMS requirements

If HealthGrid wants an earlier tranche, we can propose a priority addendum in the contract to shorten procurement queues. Call scheduled: 8/6 10:00am PT to walk tech and procurement gating.

—Marissa
Director, Onboarding & Customer Success


From: Samantha Lee <samantha@healthgrid.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: HealthGrid Procurement <procurement@healthgrid.com>
Date: 2025-08-06T15:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: FastPath: need MAP & procurement gating for Q4 reserve

Thanks — this looks good. Confirming:
- We’ll aim to sign a 12‑month reservation if the priority addendum is included.
- Procurement will prepare the PO template and the SOC2 checklist by 8/11.
- We’ll run the tech readout 8/6 10:00am PT (Samantha, Platform + procurement team).

One quick ask for the call: can someone prepare a short slide (2–3) that summarizes lead times and the cost delta between 8x A100 baseline vs 16x A30 baseline? That will help finance during approval.

Thanks,
Samantha
Head of Platform Integrations, HealthGrid

title: MSA & Order Form redline + Ariba onboarding details
preview:
From: Sofia Bryant <sofia.bryant@healthex.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: David Lee <david.lee@healthex.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Jul 2025 16:04:00 -0700
Subject: Vendor setup + MSA redline questions for Healthex onboarding

Hi Marissa,

Thanks again for the walkthrough last week. Procurement is ready to start the portal enrollment but we have a couple of dependencies before we can complete Ariba vendor registration:

- We received your MSA but our legal team redlined Section 6 (Data Residency) and Section 9 (Indemnity). Can you confirm whether Redwood is willing to accept the proposed indemnity cap change?
- Ariba requires a PO acknowledgement flow. Do you accept an electronic PO acknowledgement via Ariba (997/855) or do you require paper-based PO acceptance?
- Portal fields: we need a canonical mapping from your Order Form to Ariba fields (Tax ID, remit address, PO acceptance SLA, EDI partner ID). Can you provide a crosswalk?

I can loop in our Ariba admin if it helps. We aim to have vendor ID and first test POs live by next week.

Regards,
Sofia Bryant
Sr. Procurement Manager
Healthex Solutions
[Attachment: Healthex_onboarding_questions.pdf]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Sofia Bryant <sofia.bryant@healthex.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Jul 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor setup + MSA redline questions for Healthex onboarding

Hi Sofia — thanks for the clear list. We’re aligned on the timeline and I’ve attached the items below to make the handoff smoother:

- redlined_MSA_2025-07-16.pdf (our legal team’s response; redlines are tracked)
- orderform_field_crosswalk_Healthex.xlsx (mapping of Redwood Order Form fields to Ariba/Po schema)
- portal_onboarding_notes.txt (practical notes for Ariba registration and test PO sequence)

Quick answers up front:
- Indemnity cap: legal has proposed a middle ground (see p.7 of the redline). We’re willing to accept the reduced cap if Healthex agrees to the standard limitation on consequential damages and to preserve Redwood’s data processing terms.
- PO acknowledgement: Redwood accepts Ariba electronic acknowledgements (997/855). Our ops team prefers the 855 acceptance flow for automated routing. If Healthex requires a manual acknowledgement, we can support that as an exception but it will add a step to PO processing.
- Field crosswalk: the attached spreadsheet maps the Order Form line items and required metadata to Ariba fields; the key asks from your side are Tax ID (EIN) and PO acceptance SLA (we default to 3 business days).

title: Enabling streaming + tooling: schema governance & phased activation
preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Sana Khan <sana.k@orbitalhealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 12 Jul 2025 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Enabling streaming + tooling: schema governance & phased activation

Hi Sana —

Thanks again for the productive call this week. Per our kickoff, I put together a compact activation plan so we can enable streaming + tool-calls with structured JSON outputs in two short sprints. High-level goals:

- Validate the JSON schema for clinical summaries (payload size and required fields)
- Enable function/tool calling for medication lookups and appointment scheduling
- Turn on streaming (server-sent tokens) for the patient summary flow with KV-prefix caching

Immediate asks for Orbital Health:
1) Share a sample of the real output you expect (3-5 examples) — we included an example file attached.
2) Confirm which fields must be immutable in the schema (e.g., patient_id, encounter_id) vs. optional metadata.
3) Procurement: please let us know if DocuSign + PO cadence is needed to start Dedicated capacity (Ben cc'd).

Attached: redwood-schema-gov-checklist.pdf, orbital-mvp-payload-examples.json, activation-cadence.xlsx

Proposed 2-week activation cadence:
- Week 0 (this week): Schema sign-off, tooling spec (function signatures)
- Week 1: Dev integration + internal E2E tests (feature flag off)
- Week 2: Customer staging run, then production toggle with canary rollout (10% -> 100%)

If this aligns, Ben and I will schedule a 30-minute walkthrough Monday to go through the schema and the function call surface.

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
Customer Success Manager | Redwood Inference
mateo@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0122

---
Attachments: redwood-schema-gov-checklist.pdf (PDF, 72KB); orbital-mvp-payload-examples.json (JSON, 14KB); activation-cadence.xlsx (XLSX, 18KB)

From: Sana Khan <sana.k@orbitalhealth.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: James Li <james.li@orbitalhealth.com>
Date: Sat, 12 Jul 2025 12:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Enabling streaming + tooling: schema governance & phased activation

Thanks Mateo — this is helpful. Quick clarifications: 

- Payloads: I attached two internal examples (redacted) to our internal ticket; I can also push sanitized versions to your Drive link if that helps.
- Immutable fields: patient_id and encounter_id are immutable. We also need a stable \"source_system\" field. Timestamps can be generated by your runtime.
- Function/tooling: medication_lookup should return code + name + formulary_status; appointment_create needs an external request-id echo back for idempotency.

Question: streaming with KV-prefix cache — can we control cache TTL per route? For some flows we need a very small TTL (5s) to avoid stale vitals.

Procurement: procurement@orbitalhealth loops me in for PO and SOW. James will own timelines from our side.

Can you confirm the canary rollback plan if the structured output fails schema validation in prod?

Regards,
Sana Khan
Senior Product Manager, Orbital Health
sana.k@orbitalhealth.com

Date: Sun, 13 Jul 2025 08:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Enabling streaming + tooling: schema governance & phased activation

Hi Sana / James —

Quick answers and next steps inline: 

- Cache TTL: yes — TTL is configurable per route in Redwood. For vitals we recommend 3–10s; we can make that per-environment so staging uses a shorter TTL.
- Canary rollback: our rollout tooling supports automatic validation gates. If structured-output schema validation fails on a canary slice, the deployment auto-reverts the route to the previous model variant and flags the route in the Console with a regression ticket. We will also emit a webhook to your onboarding webhook (if you enable it) with the failure trace.

Action items I will take this week:
- Monica: lead a 30-minute schema walkthrough with your dev(Ops) team on Monday 10:00AM PT — can you confirm attendee list?
- Ben: coordinate procurement ask and confirm whether orbital needs a PO before we spin up Dedicated reserved capacity.
- Mateo: push sanitized payload examples to the Drive link and mark fields that are required vs. optional in the attached checklist.

Sana: if that timing works, I'll send a calendar invite for Monday 10:00AM PT.

Best,
Mateo

title: MSA / Order Form — third-party IP claim routing & indemnity compromise (BrightForge)
preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Jun 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: BrightForge MSA/order form — third-party indemnity routing (initial ask)

Team —

BrightForge sent back an MSA + order form with aggressive indemnity language. They want Redwood to (i) defend and indemnify for any third-party IP claim arising from the customer’s use of the service, and (ii) include subcontractors/OSS dependencies without carveouts. They attached the redline (BrightForge_MSA_redline_v3.docx) and an evidentiary list they expect us to populate.

Key asks from me:
- Quick legal read on acceptable carveouts (OSS runtime, downstream open-source modules, third-party infra).
- Commercial: what’s the minimum concession we can offer to get this signed this week (Ben) — are escrow/insurance offsets workable?
- Contracts ops: confirm how Order Form v2 maps to our standard SLA/limitation templates (Laura).

I suggest the following starting posture: Redwood broadly offers defense for software it authors; carve out third-party OSS and customer-added content, and add a narrow customer notice + right to assume defense clause. If legal can draft a one-paragraph compromise we can present to BrightForge.

Attachments: BrightForge_MSA_redline_v3.docx (docx), 3P_dependency_evidence_list.xlsx (xlsx)

Thanks,
Mateo
Revenue — Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Jun 2025 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: BrightForge MSA/order form — third-party indemnity routing (legal read)

On Wed, Jun 25, 2025 at 9:12 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team —
>
> BrightForge sent back an MSA + order form with aggressive indemnity language...

I read their redline. High level: they have expanded \"Customer Indemnity\" to capture 3P claims that appear caused by the customer's configuration or their use case, and removed common OSS carveouts.

Redlines I propose (summary):
- Preserve our standard \"Third-Party IP\" carveout for widely-used open-source runtimes and libraries (list to be supplied). We can't accept open-ended OSS defense obligations — too much exposure.
- Add a narrow \"Assume and Control\" mechanism: if customer gives notice and reasonably cooperates, we can assume defense for claims that are demonstrably caused by Redwood’s deliverables (not customer modifications or their data).
- Limit remedies to defense and settlement up to a capped indemnity amount tied to the order form value (suggestion: 12 months' fees as default). For willful infringement, allow higher make-whole remedies but subject to negotiation.
- Require that customer provide the evidence list and use our approved counsel if we assume defense (we can designate outside counsel).

I drafted a one-paragraph compromise for the MSA (I can convert to track changes in the attached doc). Important: we should insist on preserving our limitation of liability and exclude punitive damages.

If Ben wants to trade commercial concessions, my preference is to take a modest revenue credit or abbreviated service credit rather than broadening indemnity. Escrow only helps in theory; insurance offsets require additional procurement queries.

I will upload a tracked-version of the MSA soon.

Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel, Contracts
Redwood Inference


From: Sarah Ortiz <sarah.ortiz@brightforge.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@brightforge.com
Date: Thu, 26 Jun 2025 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MSA / OF redline — BrightForge position

Hi Mateo and Ben —

Thanks for the quick turnaround. A few clarifications from our side so you understand the ask: BrightForge uses Redwood in regulated production workflows where customers have downstream obligations. We must be able to point to vendor indemnities for third-party infringement claims that arise while our customers consume the product. Our legal team therefore needs defense & indemnity for third-party IP claims that arise from the integrated solution.

We are open to a reasonable cap tied to the annual order value and to a process where we provide a prioritized evidence packet. However, we cannot accept a general OSS carveout that would let vendors disclaim responsibility for dependencies they push into production.

Team —

I converted Kimberly's summary into tracked language and inserted the compromise into Section 7 (see attached tracked doc in our internal doc store). Summary of the compromise we will present to BrightForge:

1) Scope: Redwood will defend and indemnify for third-party IP claims only to the extent the claim is directly caused by Redwood-authored components as delivered in the Agreement; claims resulting from Customer modifications, Customer-provided data, or third-party OSS consumed/modified by Customer are excluded.

2) OSS carveout & exception list: Broad OSS-Runtime carveout, with an explicit exception list limited to core runtime libraries we ship (we will produce the list within 24 hours).

Subject: BrightForge MSA/order form — third-party indemnity routing (initial ask)

Team —

BrightForge sent back an MSA + order form with aggressive indemnity language. They want Redwood to (i) defend and indemnify for any third-party IP claim arising from the customer’s use of the service, and (ii) include subcontractors/OSS dependencies without carveouts. They attached the redline (BrightForge_MSA_redline_v3.docx) and an evidentiary list they expect us to populate.

Key asks from me:
- Quick legal read on acceptable carveouts (OSS runtime, downstream open-source modules, third-party infra).
- Commercial: what’s the minimum concession we can offer to get this signed this week (Ben) — are escrow/insurance offsets workable?
- Contracts ops: confirm how Order Form v2 maps to our standard SLA/limitation templates (Laura).

I suggest the following starting posture: Redwood broadly offers defense for software it authors; carve out third-party OSS and customer-added content, and add a narrow customer notice + right to assume defense clause. If legal can draft a one-paragraph compromise we can present to BrightForge.

Attachments: BrightForge_MSA_redline_v3.docx (docx), 3P_dependency_evidence_list.xlsx (xlsx)

Thanks,
Mateo
Revenue — Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>

Date: Wed, 25 Jun 2025 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: BrightForge MSA/order form — third-party indemnity routing (legal read)

On Wed, Jun 25, 2025 at 9:12 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team —
>
> BrightForge sent back an MSA + order form with aggressive indemnity language...

I read their redline. High level: they have expanded \"Customer Indemnity\" to capture 3P claims that appear caused by the customer's configuration or their use case, and removed common OSS carveouts.

Redlines I propose (summary):
- Preserve our standard \"Third-Party IP\" carveout for widely-used open-source runtimes and libraries (list to be supplied). We can't accept open-ended OSS defense obligations — too much exposure.
- Add a narrow \"Assume and Control\" mechanism: if customer gives notice and reasonably cooperates, we can assume defense for claims that are demonstrably caused by Redwood’s deliverables (not customer modifications or their data).
- Limit remedies to defense and settlement up to a capped indemnity amount tied to the order form value (suggestion: 12 months' fees as default). For willful infringement, allow higher make-whole remedies but subject to negotiation.
- Require that customer provide the evidence list and use our approved counsel if we assume defense (we can designate outside counsel).

I drafted a one-paragraph compromise for the MSA (I can convert to track changes in the attached doc). Important: we should insist on preserving our limitation of liability and exclude punitive damages.

If Ben wants to trade commercial concessions, my preference is to take a modest revenue credit or abbreviated service credit rather than broadening indemnity. Escrow only helps in theory; insurance offsets require additional procurement queries.

I will upload a tracked-version of the MSA soon.

Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel, Contracts
Redwood Inference

title: Proposed MAP, success definitions & timeline to commit
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Perez <sara.perez@clearpathhealth.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Jun 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Proposed MAP, initial success criteria & acceptance tests

Hi Sara —

Great to meet last week. Per our conversation I attached a first-pass mutual action plan (MAP) and an \"acceptance tests\" draft that maps the success criteria to concrete checks we can run during the 4-week evaluation. Key points up front:

- Goal: Validate integration with existing ClearPath EHR pipeline + achieve >92% classification concordance on our canonical prompt set, and maintain P95 latency < 320ms for 512-token responses.
- Exit gate (go): evidence of functional integration, two weeks of steady concordance >= 92% on the evaluation set, and no SEV-level incidents during the trial window.
- Exit gate (no-go): concordance < 88% for the majority of the rollout tests OR average token cost > projected threshold after optimization (we'll show cost projections).

Attachments: ClearPath_MAP_v1.xlsx, acceptance_tests_v1.md (both in this thread).

Can you review the attached MAP and acceptance tests and let us know where procurement and security want evidence (SOC2/pen test artifacts)? If you're OK with the gates above we can lock the timeline and get Legal/Procurement involved for a PO/SOW.

Proposed next steps:
1) You review and flag any missing acceptance checks (by Tue 6/17).
2) We deliver a lightweight smoke-test pack (we'll attach) and a runbook for test execution.
3) Kickoff pilot week of 6/23, 4-week evaluation window, decision call w/ execs in week 4.

Thanks — happy to jump on a 30-minute call if that helps.

— Ben
Account Exec, Redwood Inference



From: Sara Perez <sara.perez@clearpathhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Daniel Lowe <daniel.lowe@clearpathhealth.com>, ClearPath Health Procurement <procurement@clearpathhealth.com>
Date: Fri, 13 Jun 2025 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposed MAP, initial success criteria & acceptance tests

Ben, thanks — I skimmed both attachments and will circulate to Daniel (clinical lead) and procurement. A couple quick asks:

- Can you add an explicit data isolation check (we need to confirm data never persists outside our VPC during the pilot)?
- We need SOC 2 Type II evidence and recent pen test summary to share with security — can you point me to those artifacts?
- Procurement requests an SOW/template pricing that aligns to the MAP milestones so we can start internal approvals.

We like the proposed timeline but need to align procurement calendar: our purchasing blackout is 6/25–6/29 (month-end approvals), so if the pilot kickoff is 6/23 we may not have a PO until early July. Can the pilot proceed on an MSA + temporary credit while procurement finalizes the PO?

I'll review acceptance test specifics with Daniel and come back with comments by Monday.

— Sara
Director, Clinical Innovation
ClearPath Health



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Perez <sara.perez@clearpathhealth.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, 14 Jun 2025 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposed MAP, initial success criteria & acceptance tests

Sara — appreciate the quick review and the procurement heads-up. A few clarifications inline and below.

On data isolation: Redwood Private can be configured for VPC-only egress for the pilot. I'll attach a short evidence pack and a link to our SOC2 summary (see drive link in related links) — we can provide an MSA + refundable credits for the pilot while PO is in flight; legal has approved this pattern 90% of the time.

Quick, concrete acceptance checklist (short version):
- Integration: API end-to-end ingest -> model response -> destination mapped; three sample patient journeys verified. (Owner: ClearPath + Redwood infra)
- Quality: Concordance > 92% on pre-shared canonical prompt set (run nightly across pilot window).
- Reliability: P95 inference latency <= 320ms for 512-token baseline under pilot load profile (we'll provide the load harness).
- Safety/Failover: fallthrough behavior verified when model unavailable (auto-fallback to baseline model, no adverse clinical outputs).
- Observability: token-level cost visibility and per-route latency traces enabled at test start.

If you and Daniel are comfortable with those as the primary gates we will:
1) Finalize MAP spreadsheet and acceptance_tests_v1.md (attached).
2) Deliver smoke-tests.yaml and latency-signals.csv to run the initial tech validation (attachments incoming from Marissa).
3) Offer the MSA + pilot-credit option to cover the short procurement gap.

Proposed decision cadence to commit:
- Week 0 (kickoff): baseline integration + test harness run.
- Weeks 1–3: metric collection and triage.
- Week 4: decision call (Go/No-go). If Go, begin SOW/PO handoff and production onboarding cadence.

I'll also add a short rollback clause we recommend (if concordance drops to <88% after any major config change, we'll pause the rollout and revert to previous model).

On SOC2/pen test evidence: see the shared link and we'll ship a redacted pen-test summary and our SOC2 attestation this afternoon.

— Ben

On Fri, Jun 13, 2025 at 11:05 AM Sara Perez <sara.perez@clearpathhealth.com> wrote:
> Ben, thanks — I skimmed both attachments and will circulate to Daniel (clinical lead) and procurement.
> ...



From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Sara Perez <sara.perez@clearpathhealth.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Jun 2025 10:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposed MAP, initial success criteria & acceptance tests — smoke test details

Hi all — piggybacking on Ben's checklist with the engineering/QA acceptance tests we'll run during the pilot. Attaching smoke-tests.yaml and latency-signals.csv. Highlights and execution notes:

- Smoke test suite (execution window: kickoff day):
  * Auth/ingest: 10 synthetic payloads through the real integration pipeline; confirm no PII persists in logs.
  * End-to-end functional: 5 real-case samples (redacted) with expected structured output schema.
  * Failover: simulate model node loss and confirm fallback path within 2s and no malformed clinical output.

- Metric thresholds to mark test pass/fail during pilot:
  * Concordance: pass >= 92%, caution 88–92%, fail < 88%.
  * Latency: pass P95 <= 320ms, caution 320–380ms, fail > 380ms.
  * Cost per 1k tokens: we will calculate against your expected query volume; we'll flag if >10% above projection.

- Rollback conditions: any single-day concordance drop below 85% triggers automatic pause and rollback; repeated SEV incidents (>=2 within 7 days) triggers pilot suspension.

Operational notes: we'll need a ClearPath point-of-contact to run the smoke-suite and provide logs for the first two days (we recommend Daniel or a SW engineer). Also please confirm which environment (staging vs pre-prod) you'll prefer for the pilot.

Attachments: smoke-tests.yaml (runbook), latency-signals.csv (load profile).

— Marissa Cole
Solutions Engineering, Redwood Inference



From: ClearPath Health Procurement <procurement@clearpathhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Sara Perez <sara.perez@clearpathhealth.com>
Cc: Daniel Lowe <daniel.lowe@clearpathhealth.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Jun 2025 09:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Proposed MAP, initial success criteria & acceptance tests — procurement info required

Hi Ben/Sara — procurement here. To move forward we need:
1) A statement of work (SOW) or template SOW that matches the MAP milestones and includes payment milestones tied to the acceptance gates.
2) A redacted SOC2 Type II report or letter of attestation + proof of current cyber insurance.
3) Pricing spreadsheet (preferably milestone-based) and your preferred PO terms (Net30/Net45).

If you can provide drafts of the SOW and pricing by 6/20 we can route for an expedited review. Otherwise the earliest we can approve a PO is 7/2 due to our internal blackout week. For the pilot, is Redwood able to proceed on a temporary credit arrangement (as Ben suggested) pending PO?

Thanks — procurement team
ClearPath Health

To: ClearPath Health Procurement <procurement@clearpathhealth.com>, Sara Perez <sara.perez@clearpathhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Jun 2025 15:35:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Proposed MAP, initial success criteria & acceptance tests — procurement info required

Redwood is fine running the pilot under an MSA + refundable credit note for the short procurement gap; we'll include the credit terms in the SOW draft.

I'll schedule a 30-minute alignment call with procurement + Sara + Daniel for Thurs 6/19 10:00am PT to walk the MAP gates and acceptance checks — invite sent. If that works we'll:

- Finalize MAP spreadsheet and acceptance_tests_v1.md based on Daniel's feedback (target: 6/20).
- Deliver smoke-tests artifacts and schedule the first run (tentative: 6/23 kickoff).

Hi all — piggybacking on Ben's checklist with the engineering/QA acceptance tests we'll run during the pilot. Attaching smoke-tests.yaml and latency-signals.csv. Highlights and execution notes:

- Smoke test suite (execution window: kickoff day):
  * Auth/ingest: 10 synthetic payloads through the real integration pipeline; confirm no PII persists in logs.
  * End-to-end functional: 5 real-case samples (redacted) with expected structured output schema.
  * Failover: simulate model node loss and confirm fallback path within 2s and no malformed clinical output.

- Metric thresholds to mark test pass/fail during pilot:
  * Concordance: pass >= 92%, caution 88–92%, fail < 88%.
  * Latency: pass P95 <= 320ms, caution 320–380ms, fail > 380ms.
  * Cost per 1k tokens: we will calculate against your expected query volume; we'll flag if >10% above projection.

- Rollback conditions: any single-day concordance drop below 85% triggers automatic pause and rollback; repeated SEV incidents (>=2 within 7 days) triggers pilot suspension.

Operational notes: we'll need a ClearPath point-of-contact to run the smoke-suite and provide logs for the first two days (we recommend Daniel or a SW engineer). Also please confirm which environment (staging vs pre-prod) you'll prefer for the pilot.

Attachments: smoke-tests.yaml (runbook), latency-signals.csv (load profile).

title: Proof of deletion and model-training exclusion — request
preview:
From: Ethan Ross <security@finnovate.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 11 Jun 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Data deletion + training assurance — quick Qs

Hi Mateo,

Thanks for taking this. We're doing a compliance review for Finnovate's PII flows ahead of a scheduled audit. A couple of specific asks from our security team:

1) Can you confirm in plain language whether Redwood uses customer prompt/response payloads to further train or fine-tune models used in production?
2) If we ask for an explicit "erase" of a tenant's data, what exact artifacts are removed, and what persists (e.g., KV cache, debug snapshots, daily backups, analytics telemetry)?
3) We need a way to validate deletion later (some sort of signed certificate or log extract). Can Redwood provide a machine-readable proof of deletion or an attest document signed by product/security?

Appreciate concise answers and any artifacts you can share — timeline for our audit is EOD next week.

Thanks,
Ethan Ross
Security Engineer | Finnovate
security@finnovate.com

--
Attachment: example_scc_requirements.pdf (pdf, 42KB)


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Ross <security@finnovate.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Jun 2025 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Data deletion + training assurance — quick Qs

Ethan — thanks for the clear list. TL;DR upfront: Redwood does NOT use customer prompt/response payloads from the Hosted or Dedicated services to train or fine-tune our general-purpose models unless a customer explicitly opts into a "research" program; we can provide a signed Deletion Certificate and an erase-log summary for your tenant. Details below.

1) Training usage:
- Hosted & Dedicated: Customer API requests, completions, embeddings are excluded from our model training pipelines by default. We separate telemetry used for service reliability from any training datasets.
- Private/VPC: Data never leaves the customer-controlled environment unless explicitly routed.
- Opt-in research programs are documented and require explicit contract language; you are not enrolled.

2) Deletion semantics (what we remove vs retain):
- Deleted/erased user content: removed from primary stores and from short-lived caches (KV caches, request caches) within the erase job window (typically < 24 hours for active cache eviction).
- Logging & traces: application traces and request logs are retained for up to 30 days in standard plans for debugging; we can reduce or disable this retention via contract or on an account-by-account basis.
- Snapshots & backups: nightly snapshots are retained for 90 days by default; erase requests will mark tenant data as deleted and we run a tombstone lifecycle that removes data from subsequent backups but historical immutable snapshots may require an extended purge window (we can shorten this to 30 days under enterprise commitments).
- Telemetry/metrics (aggregated): aggregated usage metrics are anonymized and retained for product analytics; they do not contain raw prompt/response text.

3) Proof of deletion / certificate:
- We can generate a signed Deletion Certificate that summarizes the erase job run (timestamp, affected data partitions, KV eviction result, backup purge marker). The certificate references a tamper-evident log entry id and an attached erase_log_summary that lists truncated log lines that verify deletion steps (no raw customer text beyond hashes).

Next steps I recommend:
- Confirm target tenant (org id + region) for the certificate.
- If you need accelerated purge of historical snapshots, indicate desired maximum retention.

I'll loop in Priyom and Mina to help produce the certificate + legal wording. If that's acceptable, I'll produce a draft by EOD tomorrow.

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
mateo_alvarez@redwood.ai

--
Attachment: redwood_data_destruction_overview.pdf (pdf, 18KB)


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.ai>

Date: Wed, 11 Jun 2025 10:15:00 -07:00

Can Product/Sec/Legal sign off on the wording and generate the certificate? Target tenant: finnovate-org-472 (US region). They mentioned an audit timeline EOD next week.

From the mail:

> 2) If we ask for an explicit "erase" of a tenant's data, what exact artifacts are removed, and what persists (e.g., KV cache, debug snapshots, daily backups, analytics telemetry)?
> 3) We need a way to validate deletion later (some sort of signed certificate or log extract).

Suggested minimal cert fields: tenant id, erase timestamp, list of subsystems touched (primary store, KV cache, backups), tombstone id, operator id. Priyom — can you produce? Mina — advise on backup purge windows. Lauren — any legal phrasing re: "we do not train on customer data" you prefer?

Thanks — Mateo

title: MSA/Order Form redlines — split jurisdiction fallback for hybrid deployment
preview:
From: Jodie Park <jodie.park@tidewave.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Amelia Ross <amelia.ross@tidewave.com>
Date: 2025-06-08T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Redlines: MSA + Order Form — venue and emergency relief

Hi Mateo,

Attached are Tidewave's redlines to the MSA and Order Form focused on governing law, venue and injunctive relief. Key points they changed:
- They want UK law for the MSA and court jurisdiction in London.
- Broad injunctive-relief carve that precludes arbitration for equitable relief.
- A clause requiring immediate notice and preservation of logs for 30 days.

Questions:

2) Any privacy/regulatory constraints given Tidewave has EU ops? If yes, does that push us toward allowing EU seat for certain claims?

Thanks — Mateo

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2025-06-08T14:30:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Fwd: Redlines: MSA + Order Form — venue and emergency relief

Mateo — quick read and suggested approach below. TL;DR: we can accommodate a two-track clause that preserves arbitration for monetary claims but permits court access for provisional/injunctive relief and limited discovery preservation. Also provide a limited governing-law compromise.

Proposed mechanics (summary):
- Governing law: Delaware law (for consistency) but add express recognition that UK courts may have emergency jurisdiction for immediate injunctive relief in respect of actions taken within the UK. Language: "This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflicts of law principles; provided, however, that either party may seek provisional or injunctive relief in any competent court in the venue where relief is sought in order to protect its intellectual property or to preserve evidence."
- Dispute resolution: Mandatory arbitration (AAA/ICDR) for all disputes seeking monetary relief or declaratory relief. Carveout: either party may seek interim equitable relief in court (narrowly tailored) — this preserves our arbitration posture while allowing Tidewave the immediate relief they want.
- Electronic evidence preservation: Require preservation for 60 days on notice (we can push from their 30 to 60; it's reasonable operationally). Include a narrowly tailored notice and scope to avoid broad fishing expeditions.
- EU/Data: If material personal data moves to EU customer-controlled infra, include a regional recognition clause: for EU-only claims arising from GDPR, parties may agree to local regulatory cooperation rather than venue change. (Flag: ops to confirm where logs are stored.)

I've dropped a short draft with redlineable clauses in the attached file. If sales wants a milder commercial concession, we can offer a mutual waiver of class claims and a commitment to meet fast escalation SLAs.

Attachment: proposed_split_jurisdiction_clauses.txt (txt)

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer | Sr. Counsel, Redwood Inference

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2025-06-08T15:02:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Fwd: Redlines: MSA + Order Form — venue and emergency relief

Thanks team. Two quick commercial notes for legal framing:
- Tidewave is asking to sign this week to hit their internal quarter. We can give a one-time concession if language is tight and limited.
- Deal impact: if we over-index to UK law/courts it will add product and SRE time for local compliance requests. Prefer the Delaware + carveout approach so engineering can keep standard preservation workflows.

Can you send me the exact clause you plan to send to them? I want to include it in my reply to Amelia/Jodie so they can see the compromise.

—Laura
Laura Bennett | Sales Director, Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jodie Park <jodie.park@tidewave.com>, Amelia Ross <amelia.ross@tidewave.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2025-06-09T08:45:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Redlines: MSA + Order Form — proposed compromise

Jodie / Amelia — thanks for the redlines. We discussed internally and propose the following limited compromise to keep your operational needs but preserve arbitration for monetary disputes:

1) Governing law: "This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflict of law principles. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may seek provisional or injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction where relief is sought to preserve evidence or protect intellectual property."

2) Dispute resolution: "Except for provisional or injunctive relief as set forth above, all disputes arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be finally resolved by binding arbitration administered by the ICDR under its rules, by a single arbitrator; judgment on the award may be entered in any court of competent jurisdiction."

3) Preservation: On receipt of written notice that a party intends to seek injunctive relief, the receiving party will preserve specified logs and metadata for 60 days, with a narrowly described scope (service request IDs, timestamps, API logs for the relevant tenant).

We believe this preserves your ability to get urgent relief locally while keeping the main dispute track in arbitration. Attached is a clean response and the redlined clauses for ease of review. If this is acceptable, we can update the MSA and send for signature. If Tidewave insists on UK substantive governing law, we'll need to escalate for executive approval and consider a limited concession (e.g., UK law for Order Form only).

Attachments: Redlines_response_Tidewave_MSA_20250609.docx (docx)

Next steps: please confirm whether Tidewave can accept the above (we can fast-track if you confirm today). If not, flag items that require exec review and we will loop in Laura's VP.

Best,
Mateo Alvarez | Account Lead, Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com
