title: Post-demo: Concurrency & streaming SLOs — next steps
preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@astrapay.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Apr 2025 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo: Concurrency & streaming SLOs — next steps

Hi Elena / AstraPay team,

Thanks again for the demo earlier — great discussion. This thread is a short post-call recap and list of items we flagged that will drive the SLO/quotas conversation. Please correct me if I missed anything.

Summary (what we heard)
- Primary workload: live customer chat + realtime invoice extraction for high-value merchants.
- Target p95 latency requirement: 250ms for streaming responses (chat typing experience).
- Expected steady-state request rate: 120 rps for the pilot (US region), with 350 rps burst windows during peak 10-minute periods.
- Concurrency: service needs to support ~280 concurrent active request streams; individual user sessions may open multiple parallel requests (some clients multiplex 2–3 per session).
- Connection pooling: they prefer persistent HTTP/2 connections and aggressive connection reuse — multiplexing on a few long-lived sockets.
- KV/cache warmups: they will warm up prompts for top 200 merchants; cold-starts expected but should be limited.

Open asks for AstraPay (please confirm):
1) Clarify ratio of streaming vs non-streaming calls (you mentioned ~80/20 streaming in the demo?).
2) Confirm burst cadence (how often do you expect the 350 rps bursts per day/week?).
3) Are there single-tenant data residency constraints for any merchant subset?

What Redwood needs to propose
- We will map those numbers into a recommended rate-limit/quota profile and a proposed Dedicated capacity plan (or hosted tier with guaranteed concurrency).
- We will include recommended autoscale/burst settings and a proposed connection-pooling guidance doc.

Attachments: I attached a quick SLO worksheet (slo_requirements.xlsx) and our current ratecard for parallelism scenarios (redwood-ratecard-v2.pdf).

Next steps
- Elena: confirm the three clarifying points above by EOD Thursday so we can get a tailored quota proposal ready for Monday.
- Marissa / Ben: I’ll coordinate an internal ops sizing and aim to have a draft quota matrix by Monday.

Thanks,
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
mateo@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0172



From: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@astrapay.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, AstraPay Procurement <procure@astrapay.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Apr 2025 15:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Concurrency & streaming SLOs — next steps

Thanks Mateo — quick answers below.

1) Streaming vs non-streaming: roughly 75% streaming (typing/partial tokens) and 25% non-streaming (batch invoice extraction).
2) Burst cadence: peaks are during merchant settlement windows — typically 3 x/day: 8:00–8:15, 12:00–12:10, 20:30–20:45 PST. Expect those 10–15 minute windows to hit ~300–350 rps. Outside those windows, load is stable around 100–130 rps.
3) Residency: for now all merchants are US-based; no single-tenant air-gapped needs for pilot.

A couple of clarifying constraints:
- We MUST keep token-level latency p95 under 300ms for a good UX; 250ms is preferable but 300ms is our guarantee line.
- Connection strategy: our client SDK will rely on HTTP/2 multiplexing and expects to reuse ~12 long-lived connections per region per service instance. We want Redwood’s guidance on socket counts / connection pooling best practices.

We reviewed the attachments at a glance — the ratecard looks sane. Curious if the Dedicated proposal will include a guaranteed concurrency pool (we're sensitive to throttling during bursts).

Elena
Elena Ruiz
Head of Product, Integrations | AstraPay
elena.ruiz@astrapay.com



From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@astrapay.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Apr 2025 09:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Concurrency & streaming SLOs — next steps

Thanks Elena — this is helpful. Based on your numbers we ran a quick ops sizing and here are the proposal highlights we’d like you to review: 

Proposed quota / SLO profile (pilot)
- Steady throughput: 130 rps (hosted) with soft-cap at 180 rps.
- Burst capacity: we recommend a Dedicated burst pool that can accommodate up to 380 rps for 15-minute windows; auto-fallback to hosted compatible model variants when Dedicated pool depleted.
- Concurrency guarantees: Reserved concurrency pool of 300 active request slots (streams). This maps to the 280 concurrent streams you requested and leaves headroom for control plane jitter.
- Streaming latency target: Redwood will commit to p95 <= 275ms for streaming across the reserved pool; we will instrument token-level tracing during pilot to validate.

Connection pooling guidance (high level)
- Recommend reusing 8–12 HTTP/2 connections per region per service instance. Each connection can carry many multiplexed streams; we’ll provide recommended max concurrent streams and a socket-backoff strategy in the full doc.

Rate-limit / throttle behavior
- Per-client key rate limits: baseline 180 rps with burst tokens that draw from the Dedicated pool. If pool is exhausted, Redwood will apply soft throttles (queueing + backoff headers) before returning 429s — we’ll tune the queuing window to avoid user-visible degradation.

Attachments: draft quota matrix attached (I exported as slo_requirements.xlsx with the populated fields) and an annotated ratecard (redwood-ratecard-v2.pdf).

Next steps
- Elena: please confirm the burst windows and whether you want the Dedicated burst pool to be active-contingent (only billed during bursts) or reserved-monthly. Procurement has been cc’d for that specific question.
- Ben: can you prepare a simple canary plan for the pilot (traffic ramp + rollback triggers) so procurement sees operational controls?

Thanks — will follow up with a short one-pager on connection pool parameters by Friday.

Marissa Cole
Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com



From: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@astrapay.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, AstraPay Procurement <procure@astrapay.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Apr 2025 11:32:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Concurrency & streaming SLOs — next steps

Marissa — thanks, this looks good and addresses the majority of our concerns. A few quick confirmations: 

- We confirm the three daily burst windows listed earlier.
- For billing: AstraPay prefers the Dedicated burst pool to be \"active-contingent\" (only billed for consumed burst capacity during the pilot). If performance warrants, we can move to a reserved monthly contract for production.
- Please send the canary plan and the connection-pool one-pager; procurement wants the one-pager included in the commercial packet.

Forwarding Mateo’s original SLO worksheet (attached).

Elena

- Expected steady-state request rate: 120 rps for the pilot (US region), with 350 rps burst windows during peak 10-minute periods.
- Concurrency: service needs to support ~280 concurrent active request streams; individual user sessions may open multiple parallel requests (some clients multiplex 2–3 per session).
- Connection pooling: they prefer persistent HTTP/2 connections and aggressive connection reuse — multiplexing on a few long-lived sockets.
- KV/cache warmups: they will warm up prompts for top 200 merchants; cold-starts expected but should be limited.

Open asks for AstraPay (please confirm):
1) Clarify ratio of streaming vs non-streaming calls (you mentioned ~80/20 streaming in the demo?).
2) Confirm burst cadence (how often do you expect the 350 rps bursts per day/week?).
3) Are there single-tenant data residency constraints for any merchant subset?

What Redwood needs to propose
- We will map those numbers into a recommended rate-limit/quota profile and a proposed Dedicated capacity plan (or hosted tier with guaranteed concurrency).
- We will include recommended autoscale/burst settings and a proposed connection-pooling guidance doc.

Attachments: I attached a quick SLO worksheet (slo_requirements.xlsx) and our current ratecard for parallelism scenarios (redwood-ratecard-v2.pdf).

Next steps
- Elena: confirm the three clarifying points above by EOD Thursday so we can get a tailored quota proposal ready for Monday.
- Marissa / Ben: I’ll coordinate an internal ops sizing and aim to have a draft quota matrix by Monday.

Thanks,
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
mateo@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0172



From: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@astrapay.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, AstraPay Procurement <procure@astrapay.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Apr 2025 15:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Concurrency & streaming SLOs — next steps

Thanks Mateo — quick answers below.

1) Streaming vs non-streaming: roughly 75% streaming (typing/partial tokens) and 25% non-streaming (batch invoice extraction).
2) Burst cadence: peaks are during merchant settlement windows — typically 3 x/day: 8:00–8:15, 12:00–12:10, 20:30–20:45 PST. Expect those 10–15 minute windows to hit ~300–350 rps. Outside those windows, load is stable around 100–130 rps.
3) Residency: for now all merchants are US-based; no single-tenant air-gapped needs for pilot.

A couple of clarifying constraints:
- We MUST keep token-level latency p95 under 300ms for a good UX; 250ms is preferable but 300ms is our guarantee line.
- Connection strategy: our client SDK will rely on HTTP/2 multiplexing and expects to reuse ~12 long-lived connections per region per service instance. We want Redwood’s guidance on socket counts / connection pooling best practices.

We reviewed the attachments at a glance — the ratecard looks sane. Curious if the Dedicated proposal will include a guaranteed concurrency pool (we're sensitive to throttling during bursts).

Elena
Elena Ruiz
Head of Product, Integrations | AstraPay
elena.ruiz@astrapay.com

Date: Wed, 30 Apr 2025 09:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Concurrency & streaming SLOs — next steps

Thanks Elena — this is helpful. Based on your numbers we ran a quick ops sizing and here are the proposal highlights we’d like you to review: 

Proposed quota / SLO profile (pilot)
- Steady throughput: 130 rps (hosted) with soft-cap at 180 rps.
- Burst capacity: we recommend a Dedicated burst pool that can accommodate up to 380 rps for 15-minute windows; auto-fallback to hosted compatible model variants when Dedicated pool depleted.
- Concurrency guarantees: Reserved concurrency pool of 300 active request slots (streams). This maps to the 280 concurrent streams you requested and leaves headroom for control plane jitter.
- Streaming latency target: Redwood will commit to p95 <= 275ms for streaming across the reserved pool; we will instrument token-level tracing during pilot to validate.

Connection pooling guidance (high level)
- Recommend reusing 8–12 HTTP/2 connections per region per service instance. Each connection can carry many multiplexed streams; we’ll provide recommended max concurrent streams and a socket-backoff strategy in the full doc.

Rate-limit / throttle behavior
- Per-client key rate limits: baseline 180 rps with burst tokens that draw from the Dedicated pool. If pool is exhausted, Redwood will apply soft throttles (queueing + backoff headers) before returning 429s — we’ll tune the queuing window to avoid user-visible degradation.

Attachments: draft quota matrix attached (I exported as slo_requirements.xlsx with the populated fields) and an annotated ratecard (redwood-ratecard-v2.pdf).

Next steps
- Elena: please confirm the burst windows and whether you want the Dedicated burst pool to be active-contingent (only billed during bursts) or reserved-monthly. Procurement has been cc’d for that specific question.
- Ben: can you prepare a simple canary plan for the pilot (traffic ramp + rollback triggers) so procurement sees operational controls?

Date: Mon, 10 Mar 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Action: finalizing DPA annex — exporter role, export controls, and signature flow

Hi Susan —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Attached is the annotated DPA (Acura_DPA_annotated_v3.pdf) with Redwood's proposed edits. Two quick asks so we can close the redline before legal handoff:

1) Exporter / controller role: your legal team proposed that Acura is the exporter for customer uploads from EU regions. We need written confirmation whether Acura will accept exporter responsibilities (Article 26-style delegation) or whether you expect Redwood to remain the primary exporter for any cross-border routing. The distinction affects the SCCs exhibit and the signature path.

2) Procurement path & signature authority: who at Acura will sign the DPA annex + Order Form? If it's a separate signatory from the MSA signer, we should route the annex to them in parallel via DocuSign to avoid a second round of countersigns.

We propose the following next steps:
- Acura confirms exporter role by Thursday EOD (13 Mar).
- Redwood returns a clean PDF for signature and sends to procurement (DocuSign link included once confirmed).
- If Acura needs a transfer-impact addendum, flag it now — we can scope a short addendum rather than re-negotiating the whole DPA.

I've cc'd Laura and Ben for operational and legal coverage; they can jump in on any technical constraints or evidence requests.

Best,

Mateo Alvarez

I will ask Gina (legal) to confirm formal acceptance in writing; expect that by Wednesday (12 Mar) morning.

2) Signatory: Procurement will sign the Order Form, but our Legal (Gina) needs to countersign the DPA annex. Sounds like parallel routing is fine — procurement will start signature on the Order Form once we get the final PDF; Gina will handle the annex.

Heads-up: procurement flagged an internal SLA exception request for data export logs — they want 12 months of audit logs retained. Can Redwood confirm retention policy and whether that requires order-form-level commitment?

—Susan
Susan Patel
Head of Partnerships, AcuraHealth
susan@acurahealth.com


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Susan Patel <susan@acurahealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 12 Mar 2025 08:17:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Action: finalizing DPA annex — exporter role, export controls, and signature flow

Susan — thank you, that helps. Gina's written confirmation on the exporter acceptance will be sufficient for legal to proceed. A few clarifications from our side:

- SCCs: Redwood will supply the 2021 SCCs exhibit and update the Annex II references. We will include the standard processor-to-processor carveout language only if Acura explicitly designates Redwood as a subprocessor for specific services — otherwise we'll keep processor responsibilities as drafted.

- Audit log retention: short answer is yes, Redwood can provide 12 months of tokenized audit logs for this integration. Two operational notes:
  * We log token-level access and export events; the retention is configurable but 12 months is supported under Dedicated and Private modes. For Hosted API customers we can commit to 12 months of aggregated audit logs via an Order Form addendum (we'll mark it as a billing add-on if it increases storage/egress).
  * If Acura requires raw event exports on a schedule (SFTP), there may be an additional setup fee.

Next steps proposed by Redwood:
- Irené (cc'd) will prepare a clean DPA annex with: 2021 SCCs exhibit, one-paragraph exporter designation block that references Acura's written confirmation, and a 12-month audit log clause.
- We'll push the clean PDF by Fri 14 Mar and include the DocuSign routing: Procurement -> Acura procurement signer; DPA annex -> Gina Ortiz (legal).

Can you confirm procurement's target signature date? We'll prioritize the PDF accordingly.

Thanks — Mateo

Confirming Acura accepts the exporter designation for uploads originating from EU clinics for the specific pilot accounts listed in Appendix A. We will sign the DPA annex once Redwood incorporates the 2021 SCCs and includes the 12-month audit log retention clause.

Procurement aims to complete signatures by 21 Mar. Please route DocuSign to me for the annex and to our procurement signer for the Order Form in parallel. If you can get the finalized PDF to us today, procurement will queue it for next-week signature.

One small request from legal: can Redwood include a sentence clarifying that any transfer-impact addendum will be scoped only to the pilot accounts (Appendix A) and will not alter the global MSA baseline unless both parties agree in writing? That will keep the pilot carveout contained.

title: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)
Attachments: CacheTelemetrySprint_OnePager_2025-03-03.pdf; CacheMetricsCardinalityBudget_v6.xlsx

Team — requesting go/no-go today so we can start implementation tomorrow.

Proposed sprint: Prefix + KV cache telemetry + initial “increase hit-rate” recommendations MVP (internal report / minimal Console stub).

Scope (2–3 weeks):
1) Runtime instrumentation (prefix cache + KV cache):
   - hit/miss counters + partial-hit semantics (per ADR)
   - miss reason taxonomy (stable set)
   - eviction + memory pressure signals
   - wasted prefill metric (privacy-safe, bucketed)
   - sampling + local rate limiting
2) Joinability/tags from gateway/control plane:
   - model_id + model_version_id, route_id, deployment_mode, region
   - strict allowlists; no per-tenant high-cardinality labels in raw metrics
3) Ingestion + cardinality controls:
   - enforce label policy + drop/aggregate forbidden label combos
   - sampling strategy to keep cost stable
4) Dashboards/alerts + runbook:
   - SRE dashboards for triage and regression detection
   - guardrail SLO proposal (internal; no customer SLA)
5) Recommendations MVP:
   - daily batch job that produces per-route/actionable levers (cache bytes/TTL/policy/routing locality/prompt canonicalization)

Key risks (need explicit sign-off):
- Metric cardinality/cost blowup if labels drift (route/model/version alone can be high)
- Runtime overhead: must be near-zero on p95/p99
- Privacy: no raw prompts; only bucketed lengths + hashed/canonicalized features
- Cross-system join correctness (model version IDs, route IDs)

Acceptance gates (proposal):
- Perf canary: p95 latency regression <0.5% at default sampling; no measurable throughput drop
- Cardinality: within budget in attached sheet; ingestion drops/guards tested in staging
- Data QA: dashboards show sane counts across Hosted + Dedicated canary; missing-tag rate <1% on canary

Owners (draft):
- Runtime instrumentation: Omar (lead), Samir (impl), Noah (review), Sofia (approval)
- Platform tags/join keys: Ethan + Amara
- Ingestion/cardinality: Logan (lead) + Nadia + Caleb
- Dashboards/alerts/runbook: Allison (lead) + Sergio + Aisha; approval: Sean
- Perf/overhead: Marcus (owner)
- Rollout/canary: Connor
- Program mgmt: Talia

If approved, I’d like a single-thread “go” from each of you (Runtime, Platform/Telemetry, Reliability/SRE) + any hard constraints we need to bake into the plan.

Thanks,
Ava
CTO, Redwood Inference


---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:34 AM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

Go from Runtime.

Hard constraints from our side:
- Instrumentation MUST be behind config flags, default on only where we’ve validated overhead (staging/canary first).
- Streaming retries + continuous batching semantics have to be correct (no double counting). We’ve been burned here.
- Miss reason taxonomy needs to be stable; don’t iterate labels every other day or SRE dashboards will churn.

Omar is set as lead; Noah will review the final metric definitions vs the ADR before we flip anything beyond canary.

Mateo
VP Engineering, Runtime


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 10:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

Go from Platform/Telemetry with two conditions:

1) Cardinality policy is non-negotiable:
   - allowlist labels only; no “free-form route name”, no tenant_id, no request_id
   - route_id/model_version_id must be normalized (single source of truth from control plane)
   - prompt-length/token-count must be bucketed (per standards)

2) Ingestion needs an explicit “drop detection” signal.
   We need to know when guards are dropping series so dashboards don’t show false “0 hits”. Please make that part of ENG-4823/4825 acceptance.

Logan/Nadia will own schema enforcement; Caleb will implement the guardrails.

Priya
VP Engineering, Platform


---

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 10:29 AM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

Go from Reliability.

A few guardrails:
- Please treat this as an operational change. Rollout needs canary gates + rollback plan (Connor + Sean to sign off before prod enablement).
- I want “telemetry pipeline health” on the same dashboard page as cache hit-rate. When data is missing, we need a clear indicator.
- Any p99 regression >1% at canary should block rollout unless explicitly approved by Runtime + SRE.

If we hit these gates, I’m comfortable proceeding.

Neha Kapoor
VP Infrastructure & Reliability


---

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)
Attachments: CacheTelemetrySprint_OnePager_2025-03-03.pdf; CacheMetricsCardinalityBudget_v6.xlsx

Team — requesting go/no-go today so we can start implementation tomorrow.

Proposed sprint: Prefix + KV cache telemetry + initial “increase hit-rate” recommendations MVP (internal report / minimal Console stub).

Scope (2–3 weeks):
1) Runtime instrumentation (prefix cache + KV cache):
   - hit/miss counters + partial-hit semantics (per ADR)
   - miss reason taxonomy (stable set)
   - eviction + memory pressure signals
   - wasted prefill metric (privacy-safe, bucketed)
   - sampling + local rate limiting
2) Joinability/tags from gateway/control plane:
   - model_id + model_version_id, route_id, deployment_mode, region
   - strict allowlists; no per-tenant high-cardinality labels in raw metrics
3) Ingestion + cardinality controls:
   - enforce label policy + drop/aggregate forbidden label combos
   - sampling strategy to keep cost stable
4) Dashboards/alerts + runbook:
   - SRE dashboards for triage and regression detection
   - guardrail SLO proposal (internal; no customer SLA)
5) Recommendations MVP:
   - daily batch job that produces per-route/actionable levers (cache bytes/TTL/policy/routing locality/prompt canonicalization)

Key risks (need explicit sign-off):
- Metric cardinality/cost blowup if labels drift (route/model/version alone can be high)
- Runtime overhead: must be near-zero on p95/p99
- Privacy: no raw prompts; only bucketed lengths + hashed/canonicalized features
- Cross-system join correctness (model version IDs, route IDs)

Acceptance gates (proposal):
- Perf canary: p95 latency regression <0.5% at default sampling; no measurable throughput drop
- Cardinality: within budget in attached sheet; ingestion drops/guards tested in staging
- Data QA: dashboards show sane counts across Hosted + Dedicated canary; missing-tag rate <1% on canary

Owners (draft):
- Runtime instrumentation: Omar (lead), Samir (impl), Noah (review), Sofia (approval)
- Platform tags/join keys: Ethan + Amara
- Ingestion/cardinality: Logan (lead) + Nadia + Caleb
- Dashboards/alerts/runbook: Allison (lead) + Sergio + Aisha; approval: Sean
- Perf/overhead: Marcus (owner)
- Rollout/canary: Connor
- Program mgmt: Talia

If approved, I’d like a single-thread “go” from each of you (Runtime, Platform/Telemetry, Reliability/SRE) + any hard constraints we need to bake into the plan.

Thanks,
Ava
CTO, Redwood Inference

Hard constraints from our side:

- Streaming retries + continuous batching semantics have to be correct (no double counting). We’ve been burned here.
- Miss reason taxonomy needs to be stable; don’t iterate labels every other day or SRE dashboards will churn.

Omar is set as lead; Noah will review the final metric definitions vs the ADR before we flip anything beyond canary.

Go from Platform/Telemetry with two conditions:

title: Runtime 1.19 rollout approval — TP stability (Dedicated-first) — go/no-go
preview:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Noah Patel <noah.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen.phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Runtime 1.19 rollout approval — TP stability (Dedicated-first) — go/no-go
Attachments: runtime-1.19-tp-stability-canary-readout-2025-03-03.pdf; tp-fix-rollout-checklist-v3.docx

Requesting go/no-go today for Runtime 1.19 rollout focused on TP stability fixes.

Proposed sequencing (Dedicated-first):
1) Dedicated canary: 2 clusters (A100 80GB + H100), 10% traffic per cluster, 24 hours.
2) Dedicated ramp: 25% -> 50% -> 100% over 3–4 days if green.
3) Hosted: start 5% after Dedicated hits 50% and we’ve had 48 hours without new hang signatures.

Ask:
- SRE: risk acceptance + paging/rollback criteria.
- Product: Dedicated-first sequencing + comms readiness.

Key gating checks are in the attached checklist; canary readout attached. Primary owners: Noah (tech), Connor (rollout), Rafael (SRE on-call readiness), Lauren/Owen (customer/support readiness).

Links for quick reference:
- Linear ENG-4829 (release plan)
- Linear ENG-4821 (initiative)
- Dashboard panels/alerts (observability-pack/pr-412)

If we’re aligned, Connor will schedule the Dedicated canary window for tomorrow morning PT.

Marissa Cole
Rollouts / Canary Product Owner
Redwood Inference

---

From: Noah Patel <noah.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen.phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:31 AM PST
Subject: Re: Runtime 1.19 rollout approval — TP stability (Dedicated-first) — go/no-go

Adding a short canary summary here (details in the PDF):

What’s in 1.19 (TP stability slice):
- Scheduler guardrail to prevent collective starvation (PR-21841)
- NCCL stream/event sync hardening (PR-21853)
- TP watchdog + safe abort/drain (PR-21860) behind flag TP_WATCHDOG=on
- KV compaction safety in TP (PR-21866)
- Fix deadlock on cancelled streaming requests (PR-21890)
- Better NCCL error surfacing (PR-21912)
- Safer defaults/guardrails for TP concurrency caps (PR-21897) behind flag TP_SAFE_DEFAULTS=on

Canary (perf-canary + benchmark-lab) results:
- Repro harness (mixed prefill/decode) that previously wedged within 20–40 mins: now no hangs over 6 hours x 3 runs on A100-80GB (TP=4) and 4 hours x 2 runs on H100 (TP=4).
- Watchdog behaved as designed under fault injection: abort -> drain -> new replica takes traffic (no global GPU wedging).
- p50/p95 latency: no meaningful change; p99 improved on the specific mixed workload due to fewer stalls/timeouts. We do see a ~0.8–1.6% throughput hit in a microbench when NCCL sync is forced; we’ve scoped sync to only the collective boundaries and left fast path unchanged.

Known risks / what we didn’t solve:
- If NCCL itself hard-hangs at the driver level, watchdog will drain but we may still need node recycle (runbook covers).
- False positive risk on watchdog for long-context bursts; we set defaults conservatively and will enable by tier.

My recommendation: proceed with Dedicated-first as Marissa outlined. Keep TP_WATCHDOG off for first 10% canary, then enable after 2 hours if error budget looks clean.

Noah

---

From: Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Noah Patel <noah.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen.phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:54 AM PST
Subject: Re: Runtime 1.19 rollout approval — TP stability (Dedicated-first) — go/no-go

SRE/Obs notes:
- The new heartbeat gap alert is configured to page only when (a) heartbeat gap > 90s AND (b) request timeout rate > threshold in same cluster. This avoids paging on transient backpressure.
- Dedicated canary dashboards are ready; alert routes to eng-sre + eng-runtime-oncall.
- Please keep additional NCCL debug logging OFF by default (INT-2219), and only flip for a 30–60 min window if we need deeper traces.

No objections from me to canarying tomorrow, assuming we keep rollback criteria strict.

Aisha

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Noah Patel <noah.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen.phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 10:13 AM PST
Subject: Re: Runtime 1.19 rollout approval — TP stability (Dedicated-first) — go/no-go

SRE approval contingent on these explicit rollback triggers during canary (Dedicated):

Rollback immediately if any of the following occur in canary clusters:
1) >2 replica drains/hour due to TP stall signals (watchdog or heartbeat gap) for 2 consecutive hours.
2) p99 latency +25% vs baseline for 30 minutes (cluster-level) with correlated GPU util < 40% (stall signature).
3) Any recurrence of the "decode iteration stuck" signature (same as Feb 17 Sev1) lasting >5 minutes.

Operational readiness:
- On-call runbook is updated with the drain/replace and concurrency cap steps.
- We will have an SRE (me or Tomáš) in the canary channel for the first 4 hours.

I support Dedicated-first. Hosted should wait until Dedicated 50% is green for 48 hours (as Marissa said).

Rafael

Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen.phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>

Requesting go/no-go today for Runtime 1.19 rollout focused on TP stability fixes.

Proposed sequencing (Dedicated-first):
1) Dedicated canary: 2 clusters (A100 80GB + H100), 10% traffic per cluster, 24 hours.
2) Dedicated ramp: 25% -> 50% -> 100% over 3–4 days if green.
3) Hosted: start 5% after Dedicated hits 50% and we’ve had 48 hours without new hang signatures.

Ask:
- SRE: risk acceptance + paging/rollback criteria.
- Product: Dedicated-first sequencing + comms readiness.

Key gating checks are in the attached checklist; canary readout attached. Primary owners: Noah (tech), Connor (rollout), Rafael (SRE on-call readiness), Lauren/Owen (customer/support readiness).

Links for quick reference:
- Linear ENG-4829 (release plan)
- Linear ENG-4821 (initiative)

If we’re aligned, Connor will schedule the Dedicated canary window for tomorrow morning PT.

Marissa Cole
Rollouts / Canary Product Owner
Redwood Inference

From: Noah Patel <noah.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Owen Phillips <owen.phillips@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:31 AM PST
Subject: Re: Runtime 1.19 rollout approval — TP stability (Dedicated-first) — go/no-go

Adding a short canary summary here (details in the PDF):

What’s in 1.19 (TP stability slice):
- Scheduler guardrail to prevent collective starvation (PR-21841)
- NCCL stream/event sync hardening (PR-21853)
- TP watchdog + safe abort/drain (PR-21860) behind flag TP_WATCHDOG=on
- KV compaction safety in TP (PR-21866)
- Fix deadlock on cancelled streaming requests (PR-21890)
- Better NCCL error surfacing (PR-21912)
- Safer defaults/guardrails for TP concurrency caps (PR-21897) behind flag TP_SAFE_DEFAULTS=on

Canary (perf-canary + benchmark-lab) results:
- Repro harness (mixed prefill/decode) that previously wedged within 20–40 mins: now no hangs over 6 hours x 3 runs on A100-80GB (TP=4) and 4 hours x 2 runs on H100 (TP=4).
- Watchdog behaved as designed under fault injection: abort -> drain -> new replica takes traffic (no global GPU wedging).
- p50/p95 latency: no meaningful change; p99 improved on the specific mixed workload due to fewer stalls/timeouts. We do see a ~0.8–1.6% throughput hit in a microbench when NCCL sync is forced; we’ve scoped sync to only the collective boundaries and left fast path unchanged.

Known risks / what we didn’t solve:
- If NCCL itself hard-hangs at the driver level, watchdog will drain but we may still need node recycle (runbook covers).
- False positive risk on watchdog for long-context bursts; we set defaults conservatively and will enable by tier.

My recommendation: proceed with Dedicated-first as Marissa outlined. Keep TP_WATCHDOG off for first 10% canary, then enable after 2 hours if error budget looks clean.

Noah

Aisha

Rollback immediately if any of the following occur in canary clusters:

- On-call runbook is updated with the drain/replace and concurrency cap steps.

title: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)
preview:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Team — requesting leadership approval today so we can lock Beta scope and start partner onboarding this week.

TL;DR proposal (Beta):
- Opt-in Quantization Profile Library with route-level enablement (Hosted + Dedicated). No default-on.
- 2–3 design partners: Acme AI + Northstar Search (confirmed interest), 1 enterprise candidate pending security review (FalconBank).
- Clear launch gates + rollback criteria; publish docs that emphasize “opt-in + customer-run evals.”

Key call-outs / decisions needed:
1) Scope: route-level toggle only (no tenant-wide defaults in Beta). Console + Config API.
2) Profile selection semantics: require explicit versioned profile ref (no “latest” alias) to avoid silent changes.
3) Launch gates: schema/validator, compatibility guardrails, determinism, eval thresholds, dashboards/alerts, and kill-switch.

Attachments:
- Quantization Profile Library — Beta Plan v0.9 (link).pdf
- Quant Profile Beta — Launch Gates Checklist (v1).xlsx

If we can get a “ship Beta under allowlist + gates below” approval by EOD, I’ll update the plan and move us into execution.

Marissa
Product Lead, Optimize


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 10:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

+1 on route-level only for Beta.

Edits I need reflected before approval:
- RBAC: Only route admins can change quantization settings. Console must show a confirmation step + “what will change” diff.
- Audit: Every enable/disable + profile ref update generates an audit event with actor, route, old/new ref, and resolved outcome.
- Compatibility guardrails: if incompatible, policy engine must deterministically fall back to baseline (and we must emit a “fallback” metric so we can alert).

On semantics: I strongly agree with no “latest”/auto-updating alias for Beta. If we want a “recommended” alias later, we’ll need explicit customer action + visible changelog.

Priya
Platform Sponsor

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 10:41 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Adding runtime gates I consider non-negotiable for Beta:

1) Determinism:
- For a given (model_id, model_variant, quant_profile_id) we must apply the same kernels/precision path across hosts, or fail closed to baseline.

2) Safety switches:
- Global kill-switch that forces baseline regardless of route config.
- Fast propagation. Target: <5 min worst-case across Hosted.

3) Observability:
- Per-request log annotation: quant_profile_id and resolution outcome (applied/fallback/disabled).
- Metrics: enabled_rate, fallback_rate, and p95 decode latency deltas.

If these are in the gates checklist (and tracked in the release checklist), I’m comfortable.

Mateo
Serving Runtime Sponsor

Aligned with the approach.

Applied ML edits for the plan:
- We will only publish profiles that pass the quant regression thresholds on Redwood prompt sets (task-family gates + statistical confidence). Any borderline deltas are “block publication” until recalibrated.
- Beta customers must be told (in docs + Console copy): “Enabling a profile can change outputs; run your own evals. Redwood provides guardrails, not guarantees.”
- Require an explicit rollback plan per partner (who flips the switch, what metrics, what timeframe). We can provide a playbook.

Arjun will own the partner-facing “what we need from you” checklist (prompt set, acceptance criteria, who to page).

Maya

Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 1:08 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

I reviewed the gates checklist attachment. Suggested tightening (so we don’t debate on the day-of):

Launch gates (must-pass):
- Config migration complete in staging + canary, rollback tested.
- Kill-switch tested end-to-end (flip -> policy/routing -> runtime baseline) + runbook updated.
- Dashboards live: enabled routes, fallback_rate, 5xx, p95 latency (prefill + decode if available), cost/token delta.
- Alerts:
  - fallback_rate > 1% sustained (15m) => page + auto-freeze rollout
  - p95 latency regression > 10% on enabled routes (30m) => page
  - quality guardrail signal (eval harness) => block further profile publication

Rollout proposal:
- Week 1: internal perf-canary + 0.1% on one design partner route
- Week 2: 1% -> 10% with explicit signoff

If we can align on these gates today, release engineering is good.

Connor
Release / Rollout Lead

1) Scope locked to route-level enablement only (no tenant defaults), Hosted + Dedicated.
2) Profile refs must be explicit and versioned (no “latest” alias in Beta).
3) Platform requirements:
   - RBAC limited to route admins; confirmation modal + visible diff
   - Audit events for all changes incl. actor + old/new + outcome
   - Compatibility checks + deterministic fallback to baseline
4) Runtime requirements:
   - Deterministic selection per (model, variant, profile)
   - Global kill-switch with <5 min propagation target
   - Request logs/metrics tag quant_profile_id + applied/fallback/disabled
5) Applied ML requirements:
   - Only publish profiles that pass regression thresholds
   - Explicit customer messaging: outputs can change; run your evals
   - Partner-by-partner rollback owner + criteria
6) Release gates / rollout:
   - Must-pass gates + dashboards/alerts + runbook (per Connor’s list)
   - 0.1% -> 1% -> 10% staged ramp with explicit signoff
7) Design partners:
   - Acme AI + Northstar Search as initial two; FalconBank optional if security review completes without expanding scope.

If you approve this direction, Marissa will issue Beta Plan v1.0 today and we’ll proceed with partner onboarding + execution.

Aditya

text:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Team — requesting leadership approval today so we can lock Beta scope and start partner onboarding this week.

TL;DR proposal (Beta):
- Opt-in Quantization Profile Library with route-level enablement (Hosted + Dedicated). No default-on.
- 2–3 design partners: Acme AI + Northstar Search (confirmed interest), 1 enterprise candidate pending security review (FalconBank).
- Clear launch gates + rollback criteria; publish docs that emphasize “opt-in + customer-run evals.”

Key call-outs / decisions needed:
1) Scope: route-level toggle only (no tenant-wide defaults in Beta). Console + Config API.
2) Profile selection semantics: require explicit versioned profile ref (no “latest” alias) to avoid silent changes.
3) Launch gates: schema/validator, compatibility guardrails, determinism, eval thresholds, dashboards/alerts, and kill-switch.

Attachments:
- Quantization Profile Library — Beta Plan v0.9 (link).pdf
- Quant Profile Beta — Launch Gates Checklist (v1).xlsx

If we can get a “ship Beta under allowlist + gates below” approval by EOD, I’ll update the plan and move us into execution.

Marissa
Product Lead, Optimize


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 10:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

+1 on route-level only for Beta.

Edits I need reflected before approval:
- RBAC: Only route admins can change quantization settings. Console must show a confirmation step + “what will change” diff.
- Audit: Every enable/disable + profile ref update generates an audit event with actor, route, old/new ref, and resolved outcome.
- Compatibility guardrails: if incompatible, policy engine must deterministically fall back to baseline (and we must emit a “fallback” metric so we can alert).

On semantics: I strongly agree with no “latest”/auto-updating alias for Beta. If we want a “recommended” alias later, we’ll need explicit customer action + visible changelog.

Priya
Platform Sponsor


---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 10:41 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Adding runtime gates I consider non-negotiable for Beta:

1) Determinism:
- For a given (model_id, model_variant, quant_profile_id) we must apply the same kernels/precision path across hosts, or fail closed to baseline.

2) Safety switches:
- Global kill-switch that forces baseline regardless of route config.
- Fast propagation. Target: <5 min worst-case across Hosted.

3) Observability:
- Per-request log annotation: quant_profile_id and resolution outcome (applied/fallback/disabled).
- Metrics: enabled_rate, fallback_rate, and p95 decode latency deltas.

If these are in the gates checklist (and tracked in the release checklist), I’m comfortable.

Mateo
Serving Runtime Sponsor


---

From: Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 11:26 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Aligned with the approach.

Applied ML edits for the plan:
- We will only publish profiles that pass the quant regression thresholds on Redwood prompt sets (task-family gates + statistical confidence). Any borderline deltas are “block publication” until recalibrated.
- Beta customers must be told (in docs + Console copy): “Enabling a profile can change outputs; run your own evals. Redwood provides guardrails, not guarantees.”
- Require an explicit rollback plan per partner (who flips the switch, what metrics, what timeframe). We can provide a playbook.

Arjun will own the partner-facing “what we need from you” checklist (prompt set, acceptance criteria, who to page).

Maya
Applied ML Sponsor


---

From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 1:08 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

I reviewed the gates checklist attachment. Suggested tightening (so we don’t debate on the day-of):

Launch gates (must-pass):
- Config migration complete in staging + canary, rollback tested.
- Kill-switch tested end-to-end (flip -> policy/routing -> runtime baseline) + runbook updated.
- Dashboards live: enabled routes, fallback_rate, 5xx, p95 latency (prefill + decode if available), cost/token delta.
- Alerts:
  - fallback_rate > 1% sustained (15m) => page + auto-freeze rollout
  - p95 latency regression > 10% on enabled routes (30m) => page
  - quality guardrail signal (eval harness) => block further profile publication

Rollout proposal:
- Week 1: internal perf-canary + 0.1% on one design partner route
- Week 2: 1% -> 10% with explicit signoff

If we can align on these gates today, release engineering is good.

Connor
Release / Rollout Lead
